subject: Biology
question_text: Who was the first person to conduct a systematic study of chromosomes during division and called this process mitosis? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Walther Flemming
Fredrich Miescher: Discovered molecule of DNA (1869) and called it 'Nuclein'. Thomas Hunt Morgan: Won nobel prize in Physiology in 1933. Explained the role of chromosomes in heredity in 1910. Antonie van Leeuwenhoek: Father of Microbiology. Discovered free living cells in pond water (1674). Also discovered sperm cells and RBCs.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct regarding the prokaryotic cell? [SSC CHSL 14/03/2023]

options.a: Their size is generally in the range of 1-10 μm.
options.b: Their chromosomes are composed of only nucleic acid.
options.d: They have only very small ribosomes as organelles.

solution:
(C) Membrane-bound cell organelles are present
Prokaryotic cell: They lack true nucleus and other organelles. They contain a nucleus like undefined structure called nucleoid. E.g. bacteria, cyanobacteria, mycoplasma (smallest cell). Bacteria contains flagella for mobility. It consists of 3 parts: Basal body, hook and filament.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following are present only in plant cells? [SSC GD 12/01/2023]
solution:
(D) Plastids
Plastids: They are double membrane organelles which contain their own DNA and ribosomes like mitochondria. Types: 1. Chromoplasts -synthesize and store pigments other than chlorophyll. 2. Chloroplast- stores chlorophyll pigment. 3. Leucoplast- It is colourless and stores food. *Mg metal is present in chlorophyll.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which organelle is responsible for producing ATP, the cell's energy currency? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Mitochondria
Mitochondria: Powerhouse of cell. Sausage shaped (0.5-1μm in diameter and 4-10 μm in length) and double membraned, contain its own DNA and ribosomes. Outer membrane is porous and inner membrane is folded inwards to form layers called cristae. ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) synthesis takes place in inner membrane.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is the fundamental structural unit of living organisms? [SSC CGL 15/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Cell
Cell: Also known as building blocks of life. Study of cell: Cytology. Discovered by Robert Hooke (Father of cytology) in 1665 in cork (dead cell). Living cell discovered by Antonie van Leeuwenhoek in 1674. *Largest cell: Ostrich egg *Smallest cell - mycoplasma. In male: Largest - Neuron Smallest - sperm In female: Largest - ovum Smallest - RBC.

subject: Biology
question_text: ________ are a kind of waste disposal system of the cell. [SSC GD 27/01/2023]
solution:
(C) Lysosome
Lysosome: spherical sac like structure containing hydrolytic enzymes (formed in rough endoplasmic reticulum). They digest unwanted materials by releasing the enzyme. Also known as "stomach of the cell" and "suicidal bags of cell". Ribosomes: a non-membranous organelle that performs protein synthesis and present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. Group of ribosomes attached to mRNA are called polysomes.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the fourth step in mitosis where non-kinetochore spindle fibers lengthen and elongate the cell? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Anaphase
There are 4 steps of mitosis: 1. Prophase: Chromosomes become compact and nuclear envelope breaks down to release chromosomes. 2. Metaphase: chromosomes starts aligning in the centre of cell or metaphase plate(imaginary line). 3. Anaphase: Duplicated chromosomes (sister chromatids) separate and move to opposite ends of cell. 4. Telophase: cell is nearly done dividing and cytokinesis takes place. Cytokinesis: physical process of division of one cell into two cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is an integral membrane protein that forms the tetramer and facilitates the diffusion of water and some small, unmodified solutes across cellular membranes? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Aquaporins
Aquaporins: found in plants, animals and all microorganisms. Highly selective for water transport. In animals, they help concentrate and dilute urine while in plants they help to take water from soil and transport it to cells. Mucins: a protein that forms mucus for protection and lubrication in body. Present in respiratory tract, digestive and reproductive tracts. Immunoglobulin: Also known as antibodies. Helps body fight infection.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a function of the cytoskeleton? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Cell support and shape
Cytoskeleton: a network of protein fibres that gives cell shape and mechanical support. Present in all cells plants, animals and bacteria. Consists of three main types of fibres: microtubules, intermediate filaments and microfilaments. Protein synthesis occurs in ribosomes present in cytoplasm or attached to rough endoplasmic reticulum (rough due to ribosomes).

subject: Biology
question_text: The basophilic nucleoprotein granules that are scattered within the cytoplasm of a nerve cell and help in protein synthesis are known as: [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Nissl Bodies
Nissl Bodies: made up of rough endoplasmic reticulum and free ribosomes in cell body of neuron. Discovered by Franz Nissl in 1884, which is structural and functional unit of nervous system. Made up of three different parts: dendrites (branch like), cell body (contains nucleus) and axon (tail like structure). *Synapse: gap between two neurons.

subject: Biology
question_text: ________ is the fluid content inside the plasma membrane. [SSC GD 06/02/2023]
solution:
(A) Cytoplasm
Cytoplasm: A transparent fluid present between plasma membrane and nuclear envelope. Cytosol is the ground substance for cytoplasm. All the cell organelles are present in the cytoplasm. Protect organelle from damage. Nucleus: "Brain of cell". Discovered by Robert Brown in 1831. Consists of nuclear envelope and nucleoplasm, chromatin thread like structure of genetic material. Present in nucleus of cell. During division, chromatin gets organised into chromosomes.

subject: Biology
question_text: Who first observed ribosomes in the mid-1950s and described them as small particles in the cytoplasm that were preferentially attached to the endoplasmic reticulum membrane? [SSC CPO 04/10/2024]
solution:
(D) George E. Palade
George E. Palade: He also discovered cytoplasm in 1835 with Albert Claude and Christian de Duve. Albert Kolliker: Discovered mitochondria in 1857. He identified mitochondria as group of granules in membrane.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is not present in a nucleus? [SSC GD 13/01/2023]
solution:
(D) Vacuole
DNA: Deoxyribonucleic Acid. It exists as a double helical structure which is discovered by Watson and Crick in 1953. There are base pairs held by weak hydrogen bonds in these helix which are Adenine(A)-Thymine(T) and Guanine(G)-Cytosine(C). Gene is a functional segment of DNA. Nucleolus: Present in nucleus and is main site for ribosome production.

subject: Biology
question_text: In the process of cell division, the formation of phragmoplast between the two daughter nuclei occurs during: [SSC MTS 05/08/2022]
solution:
(A) The cytokinesis
Cytokinesis is division of cytoplasm and depends on karyokinesis while karyokinesis is division of nucleus. Phases of cell cycle: 1. G0 phase - resting or stable phase of cell means cell is not dividing, preparing for division. 2. G1 phase - Cell grows without replication of DNA. 3. S-phase - Synthesize DNA/replicate. 4. G2 phase - cell grows and prepare for division. 5. M phase - Cell Division starts.

subject: Biology
question_text: Breakdown of pyruvate into lactic acid and energy in the lack of oxygen takes place in which of the following? [SSC CHSL 13/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Human muscle cells
Glycolysis: Break down of glucose into pyruvate. This pyruvate further break down by 3 different pathways in different conditions. In presence of oxygen: Pyruvate -> CO2 + H2O + Energy (mitochondria). In absence of oxygen: Pyruvate -> Ethanol + CO2 + Energy (in yeast). In lack of oxygen: Pyruvate -> Lactic acid + Energy (human muscle cell).

subject: Biology
question_text: The nucleus is separated from ________ by a nuclear membrane. [SSC GD 23/01/2023]
solution:
(D) Cytoplasm
Nucleus consists of nuclear envelope and nucleoplasm. Nucleoplasm (nucleolus + chromatin material) is a liquid ground substance covered by nuclear envelope. This nuclear envelope permit transfer of material between nucleoplasm and cytoplasm.
Cytoplasm:- Refer to question 11.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the difference between a somatic cell and a reproductive cell? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]

options.a: Somatic cells undergo mitotic cell division while reproductive cells undergo meiotic cell division.
options.b: The somatic cells have mitochondria but the reproductive cells do not have any mitochondria.
options.c: Somatic cells do not contain any chromosomes while reproductive cells have their own chromosomes.
options.d: The somatic cell and reproductive cell are neither diploid nor haploid.

solution:
(A) Somatic cells undergo mitotic cell division while reproductive cells undergo meiotic cell division
Mitosis: In mitosis, nucleus divides one time and produce two diploid daughter cells. It helps in healing and growth and malfunction results in Cancer. Meiosis: In meiosis, nucleus divide two times and produce four haploid daughter cells. It causes genetic variation and malfunction results in disorders like Down syndrome and Turner syndrome.

subject: Biology
question_text: Eukaryotic organisms can have very complex functions to sustain themselves. At the cellular level, these involve several different types of chemical functions like energy production, metabolism, etc. What are the membrane-bound structures called, which are present within the cell to keep each of these functionalities separate? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Organelles
Cell organelles support complex activities of cell and are of different types. a) Vacuole - provide turgidity and rigidity to cell. b) Endoplasmic reticulum: perform manufacturing and transport of protein, fats and lipids. c) Golgi apparatus: "Post office of cell" as it performs packaging and dispatching. d) Lysosomes: "suicidal bag of cell" as it helps to clean cell by digesting foreign material. e) Mitochondria: "powerhouse of cell" f) Plastids: only in plants.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which organelle contains enzymes that help break down fatty acids and detoxify certain compounds in the cell? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Peroxisomes
Peroxisomes: They contain enzymes for oxidation reactions and form oxidative crystals. They also performs chemical detoxification. Centriole: Only found in animal cell and helps them divide and organise microtubules. 9+0 structure of triple microtubules. Vacuole: Stores cell sap and prevent shock and injury.

subject: Biology
question_text: ________ is a group of cells similar in structure and function. [SSC CGL 07/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Tissue
Study of Tissue: Histology. Tissues are divided into plant tissue and animal tissue. Plant tissue are made up of dead cells while animal tissue are made up of living cells. Group of tissues forms an organ which further form organ system.

subject: Biology
question_text: In plant cells, ________ are primarily organelles in which materials such as starch, oils, and protein granules are stored. [SSC GD 31/01/2023]
solution:
(D) Leucoplasts
Leucoplast- a type of plastid which is colourless and stores food. It is divided into three types a) Amyloplast-stores carbohydrates. b) Alluroplast-stores protein c) Elaioplasts- stores fat. Chromoplasts-contain pigments except chlorophyll.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following are situated in the nucleus of a cell? [SSC MTS 22/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Chromosome and genes
Chromosomes consist of DNA and proteins (histone). 23 pairs of chromosome (22+1). 22 are autosomal and 1 is sex chromosome means it determines sex of an individual. *Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease is caused by duplication of the peripheral Myelin protein 22 (PMP22) gene on chromosome 17.
Genes- functional segment of DNA.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the component that is unique only to prokaryotes. [SSC CHSL 31/05/2022]
solution:
(A) Mesosome
Mesosome: inward fold in the membrane of bacteria. Prokaryotic cell: cells in which true nucleus and membrane bound organelles are absent. They have nucleus like undefined structure called nucleoid.

subject: Biology
question_text: In animal cells, which membrane-bound cell organelles are generally smaller and help to separate waste products? [SSC CHSL 31/05/2022]
solution:
(D) Vacuole
Vacuole: In animals, vacuoles are small and temporary while in plants vacuoles are large and permanent. They occupy around 50-90% of plant cell space. Stores cell sap (amino acid + sugar + organic acid). Golgi apparatus: discovered by Camillo Golgi in 1898. It performs the function of packaging and dispatching. It has two faces a) cis face- toward nucleus and convex in shape. b) trans or maturing phase- toward plasma membrane and concave in shape. It is also involved in the formation of lysosomes.

subject: Biology
question_text: The cytoplasm has ________, such as ribosomes, mitochondria, and Golgi bodies. [SSC GD 02/02/2023]
solution:
(C) Organelles
For explanation, refer question 18.

subject: Biology
question_text: When a cell divides by meiosis, how many new cells are formed? [SSC MTS 13/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Four new cells
Meiosis-daughter cells are haploid. Meiosis helps in genetic variation and malfunction can cause disorders. a) Down syndrome: extra copy of chromosome 21. Symptoms: development delays, distinct facial features and increased risk for health problems. b) Turner Syndrome: missing X chromosome in female. Symptoms: wide chest, short and wide neck and infertility. c) Klinefelter syndrome: an extra X chromosome in male. Symptoms: breast growth, infertility.

subject: Biology
question_text: The outermost cover of plant cells is/are known as: [SSC CGL 26/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Cell Wall
Cell Wall: It is a non-living and freely permeable covering and it determines shape of cell. It also provides strength and turgidity to cell. Made up of cellulose and hemicellulose in plants. In bacteria: peptidoglycan or peptin. In fungus:- complex sugar called chitin. *Glycocalyx- outer covering of bacterial cell.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the statement that is NOT true about ATP. [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Secretion of enzymes
ATP- Adenosine Triphosphate. Energy currency of cell. Synthesized in Mitochondria. Provides energy for all biochemical processes. When Energy is released ATP converts into ADP (Adenosine diphosphate).

subject: Biology
question_text: In which year did E. Gorter and F. Grendel make a breakthrough by examining the surface area of lipids and concluded that the lipid surface surrounding cells must be of two layers? [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
solution:
(D) 1925
E. Gorter and F. Grendel extracted lipids from RBCs for this experiment. 1910: Thomas Hunt Morgan mapped a gene to a chromosome for 1st time. 1945: Alexander Fleming received Nobel Prize in physiology for discovery of penicillin.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements is correct about mitochondria? [SSC CHSL 30/05/2022]

options.a: Mitochondria is present only in bacteria.
options.b: Mitochondria without outer membrane is called mitoplast.
options.c: Mitochondria is not bound by any membrane.
options.d: Mitochondria is present in all living cells.

solution:
(B) Mitochondria without outer membrane is called mitoplast
For explanation, refer question 4.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following causes enlargement and extension growth of cells? [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Turgor pressure
Turgor means tension in the cells that pushes cell membrane against cell wall. Imbibition: When a seed absorbs water and become swelled. First step for seed germination. Osmotic pressure: depends on concentration of solute in solution. Pressure potential: pressure applied by water in plant cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which organelle is found both in eukaryotic and prokaryotic cells? [SSC MTS 18/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Ribosomes
Ribosomes are present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells but in prokaryotes these are smaller and simpler (70s) while in eukaryotes ribosomes are complex (80s).

subject: Biology
question_text: In Golgi apparatus, the maturing face is: [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Concave
Maturing phase: also known as trans phase. Towards cell/plasma membrane. Cis phase: towards nucleus and convex. In plant cells, Golgi apparatus present as dictyosomes(separate units).

subject: Biology
question_text: ________ is the chemical process in which different types of chemical reactions are involved in controlling the living state of the cells in an organism. [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Metabolism
Metabolism: Two major parts of metabolism are a) catabolism- break down molecules into simple units to form energy. E.g. glycolysis and digestion b) anabolism- formation of complex molecules from simpler and requires energy. E.g. photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
question_text: Amoeba acquires its food through the process of: [SSC MTS 19/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Endocytosis
Endocytosis: movement of particle into the cell from external environment. It is active transport from low concentration to high concentration. It is of two types a) phagocytosis: cell's eating process b) pinocytosis: drinking process. Extracellular fluid is taken into the cell. Endocytosis: movement of particles from cell to external environment (cell vomiting). Used for discarding undigested residue from endocytosis or enzymes and hormones.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following statements is correct regarding the cell membrane? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
I. The membrane is selectively permeable to some molecules present on either side of it.
II. Water may also move across this membrane from lower to higher concentration.
solution:
(B) Only I
Cell membrane/plasma membrane: Outermost, living, thin, flexible and selectively permeable. Composed of protein (60-80%) and lipids. Flexibility helps cell to engulf food and other materials. Bodyguard of cell and provides a definite shape.

subject: Biology
question_text: During which phase of the cell cycle do metabolic changes assemble the cytoplasmic material required for mitosis and cytokinesis? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(C) G2 phase
Phases of cell cycle: 1. G0 phase- resting or stable phase of cell means cell is not dividing or preparing for division. 2. G1 phase- Cell grows without replication of DNA. 3. S-phase- Synthesize DNA 4. G2 phase- cell grows and prepare for division. 5. M phase- Cell Division starts.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which scientist among the following coined the term 'protoplasm' for the fluid substance of the cell? [SSC GD 18/11/2021]
solution:
(D) Purkinje
Purkinje coined the term protoplasm in 1839. Protoplasm includes cytoplasm and nucleus of cell. Schwann: Gave cell theory along with Schleiden in 1838. "Cell is the basic unit of life". Virchow:- modified the cell theory in 1855. "Omnis cellula e cellula" new cells come from pre-existing cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which process is used by the unicellular freshwater organisms and most plant cells to take in water? [SSC GD 18/11/2021]
solution:
(C) Osmosis
Osmosis: Movement of water from high concentration to lower concentration through a semi permeable membrane. It doesn't require energy and lasts upto equilibrium. E.g. When RBC is put into a hypotonic solution (solution's concentration > cell's concentration) It swells while in hypertonic solution (solution < cell) it shrinks to reach equilibrium/ Isotonic (solution concentration = cell concentration).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following cell organelles is also known as the 'suicide bag' of a cell? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Lysosomes
For explanation, refer question 6.

subject: Biology
question_text: In 1879, who observed cell division in salamander embryos, where cells divide at fixed intervals? [SSC CHSL 10/06/2022]
solution:
(B) Walther Flemming
Albert Rudolf von Kolliker: Discovered mitochondria in 1857. Garth L. Nicolson: Gave Fluid Mosaic Model of plasma membrane with Seymour J. Singer in 1972. It describes the structural features of membrane. It consists of different macromolecules that make it like a mosaic.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following pair of cell-shape is correct? [SSC GD 11/07/2023]
I. Red blood cells - Spherical shape
II. Muscle cells - Spindle shape
solution:
(C) Only II
Red Blood Cells: Biconcave disc shaped. Other name: erythrocytes. Lifespan: 120 days. "Spleen" is known as graveyard of RBC. Normal Range: 4.5 to 6.5 Million cells per microlitre of blood. Muscle cells: Elongated or spindle shaped. These are of three types a) Skeletal: Unbranched, multinucleated and easily fatigued. b) Smooth/Visceral - Unbranched, uni-nucleated and do not fatigue easily. c) Cardiac -branched, uni-nucleated and striated.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which biological stain is used in histology and cytology to colour all cell nuclei red? [SSC CHSL 06/06/2022]
solution:
(A) Safranin
Phenolphthalein: It is a pH indicator. Become colourless in acid and pink in base. Methyl orange: Turns RED in acid and YELLOW in base.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following enzymes are essential for DNA replication in an animal cell? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]

options.a: Reverse transcriptase, DNA polymerase, DNA primase
options.b: Topoisomerase, DNA ligase, DNA polymerase, DNA primase

solution:
(B) Topoisomerase, DNA ligase, DNA polymerase, DNA primase
#Reverse Transcriptase is involved in reverse transcription (convert RNA into DNA) not DNA replication. So option A,C and D can be easily eliminated. DNA replication - process of forming DNA replicas from an original molecule of DNA. Topoisomerase - introduce cuts in DNA strands. DNA ligase - rejoin the DNA strands by making a phosphodiester bond. DNA polymerase -synthesize DNA during replication. DNA primase - synthesize primers (short RNA sequence) and initiate replication.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following pairs is INCORRECTLY matched? [SSC CGL 11/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Nucleus: lipid metabolism
Nucleus stores DNA the genetic material and controls the activity of other parts of cell. Called Brain of cell. Discovered by Robert Brown in 1831. It is the site where transcription occurs. Transcription: It is the process of formation of RNA from DNA.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following cell organelles functions both as a passage-way for intracellular transport and as a manufacturing surface? [SSC MTS 08/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Endoplasmic reticulum
Endoplasmic reticulum is a large network of tubes and sheets and occurs in 3 forms. a) Long tubules b) Vesicles (oblong bags) c) Cisternae (closed, fluid filled sacs). Attached to plasma membrane and outer nuclear membrane. There are 2 types of ER- Smooth (ribosomes absent) and Rough (ribosomes present). Protein synthesis occurs in Rough ER while fat and lipid synthesis occurs in Smooth ER. ER also detoxify poison and drugs entering into cell.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the component found only in a eukaryotic cell. [SSC CHSL 31/05/2022]
solution:
(B) Nuclear membrane
Nucleus is not present in prokaryotic cells while eukaryotic cells have a true nucleus hence nuclear membrane is only present in eukaryotic cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which cell found in the bone marrow acts as a repair system for the body? [SSC MTS 11/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Stem cells
Stem cells are found in almost all tissues. They can renew themselves to repair the damage or they can undergo differentiation to make new cells. Stem cells are generally collected from blood or bone marrow for donating.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which cell theory proved that the quasi-fluid nature of lipid enables lateral movement of proteins within the overall bilayer? [SSC CHSL 24/05/2022]
solution:
(B) Fluid Mosaic Model
Fluid Mosaic Model is given by Singer and Nicolson in 1972. According to the Model, cell membrane is a double layered structure in which lipids, protein and fat is present. It has two polar ends at extreme and non polar ends inside. It explains the flexibility of layer and accounts for selective permeability of layer.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which year did Rudolf Virchow published an essay titled 'Cellular Pathology' in which he famously declared that 'every cell arises from another cell'? [SSC CHSL 10/06/2022]
solution:
(A) 1855
In 1838, Schleiden and Schwann gave "cell theory" saying all living beings are composed of basic unit called cell. This was further modified by Virchow in 1855 called "omnis cellula e cellula" means new cells come from pre existing cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: What do you call the fluid matrix filling in the cell? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Cytoplasm
The term cytoplasm is first coined by Albert R. von Kolliker 1863. Protoplasm is a fluid in the cell in which organelles are suspended. It is a negatively charged fluid. Without cytoplasm the cell will dehydrate. Cytosol is the ground substance for cytoplasm. Cytoplasm also contains water and some enzymes.

subject: Biology
question_text: The main function of centriole is: [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]

options.a: To help cell division in animal cell.
options.b: To maintain rigidity in plant cell.
options.c: To maintain mineral composition in bacterial cell.
options.d: To help plant cell for multiplication.

solution:
(A) To help cell division in animal cell
Centriole - They exist as 9+0 structure of triple microtubules composed of a protein called 'tubulin'. Discovered by Boveri and Beneden in 1887. Present in animal cells and some lower plants such as chlamydomonas. Absent in higher plants.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the percentage of proteins in the cell membrane? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(A) 60-80%
Cell membrane is a living and elastic structure which protects the cell as it is outermost covering if cell wall is absent. It facilitate movement as it is selectively permeable. Flexibility helps the cell to engulf food and other materials. There's two types of movement occurs across cell membrane. a) Diffusion b) osmosis.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following illustrations shows the mitochondrial structural details? [SSC CGL 09/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Mitochondria is a sausage shaped structure with two membranes
Outer membrane of mitochondria is porous and ATP synthesis occurs in inner membrane. It is 0.5-1 μm in diameter and 4-10 μm in length.

subject: Biology
question_text: Under the electron microscope, which cell organelle is visible as either clusters or single, small dots that float freely in the cytoplasm? [SSC CHSL 25/05/2022]
solution:
(D) Ribosomes
Ribosomes are main site for protein synthesis. First discovered by George e-palade in 1955. Formation of ribosomes called ribogenesis occurs in nucleolus of the nucleus. In prokaryotic cell ribosomes are 70s while in eukaryotic cells ribosomes are 80s.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements about the cell cycle is FALSE? [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]

options.a: In G1 phase, the cell grows physically larger and copies organelles.
options.b: In S phase, replication of DNA takes place.
options.c: In M phase, the cell grows physically larger and copies organelles.
options.d: Preparation of mitosis takes place in G2 phase.

solution:
(C) In M phase, the cell grows physically larger and copies organelles
In M phase, the actual cell division takes place while in G1 phase the cell becomes physically larger and copies organelles except nucleus #hence the statement is correct.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is an example of prokaryotic cells? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Bacteria
Bacteria are single celled simple organisms. They are microscopic and lacks a true nucleus. They have flagella for movement which is divided into 3 parts:- basal body, hook and filament. Plasmodium and leishmania both are protozoa. Plasmodium cause malaria and leishmania cause Kala Azar (Black fever). Binary fission occurs in leishmania and multiple fission occurs in plasmodium.

subject: Biology
question_text: The primary function of which globular structure is to produce and assemble the ribosomes of the cell? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Nucleolus
Nucleolus is present in the nucleus of cell along with chromatin material. Nucleolus and chromatin material together form nucleoplasm which is a liquid ground substance covered by nuclear envelope. Peroxisomes: They contain enzymes and oxidative crystals that help breakdown fatty acids and detoxify cell.

subject: Biology
question_text: Besides the cell organelles, there are various types of non-living components within a cell, which are known as: [SSC CHSL 09/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Cytoplasmic Inclusion
They are non living components in which no metabolic activity takes place. Examples - glycogen granules, pigments, crystals and lipids. Glycogen granules are the most abundant cytoplasmic inclusion. Plasmodesmata: These are channels in cell membrane which allow one cell to interact with another cell and allow the movement of molecules like nutrients.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is the major microtubule-organizing centre (MTOC) of eukaryotic cells involved in various cellular processes, including sensory reception, locomotion, and embryogenesis? [SSC CHSL 30/05/2022]
solution:
(D) Centrosome
Centrosome is a structure which helps in the organisation of microtubules. It is also involved in cell division as it helps to maintain equal distance between chromosome. Locomotion: It is the movement and change in position of cell.

subject: Biology
question_text: During cell division, the formation of a new cell wall begins by the accumulation of: [SSC MTS 13/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Phragmoplast
Phragmoplast: It forms during late cytokinesis to form cell wall between daughter cells. Golgi body is involved in the formation of phragmoplast. Periplasm: it is the space between inner and outer membrane of a cell. Protoplast: a cell without cell wall.

subject: Biology
question_text: Who among the following scientists discovered free-living cells in pond water for the first time? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Antonie van Leeuwenhoek
He is known as the father of microbiology. Discovered sperm and RBCs. Robert Hooke: Discovered dead cell in 1665, Father of cytology. Robert Brown: Discovered nucleus in 1831. Franz Bauer: A botanist famous for his microscopic drawings of plant.

subject: Biology
question_text: When two nuclei fuse together forming one nucleus during cell fusion, it is known as: [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Synkaryon
Eukaryon: Eu is the word for True. Eukaryon means cells with true nucleus i.e. eukaryotic cells. Heterokaryon: a cell that has multiple genetically different nucleus. Syncytium: A cell formed by the fusion of multiple cells together.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which cell organelle performs the principal function of packaging materials? [SSC MTS 14/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Golgi apparatus
Discovered by Camillo Golgi in 1898. It is involved in the formation of lysosomes. Also perform packaging, dispatching and modifying proteins and lipids. 'Post office of the cell'.

subject: Biology
question_text: There are ________ main types of cell division. [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(D) 2
There are two types of cell division: mitosis and meiosis. For more, refer question 17.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the role of ribosomes of a polysome? [SSC CHSL 09/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Translate the mRNA into proteins.
Polysomes are the group of ribosomes attached to mRNA (messenger RNA). Before binding to mRNA ribosomes are present freely in the cytoplasm.

subject: Biology
question_text: Robert Hooke discovered the cell in 1665 by observing a ________ under a microscope. [SSC GD 22/11/2021]
solution:
(A) Piece of cork
For other discoveries related to cell refer question 41 and 62.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the major constituent of cellular energy transport systems? [SSC GD 02/02/2023]
solution:
(A) Phosphorus
Phosphorus is present in ATP as Triphosphate which when release energy converts into ADP by removing one phosphate group. When another phosphate group is removed from ADP it becomes AMP Adenine Monophosphate. ATP is also known as 'Energy currency of cell'.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following statements is correct about Endoplasmic Reticulum? [SSC MTS 19/07/2022]
I. It is a large network of membrane-bound tubules and sheets.
II. It is similar in structure to the plasma membrane.
solution:
(C) Both I and II
Endoplasmic reticulum is attached to plasma membrane and outer nuclear membrane and act as a channel for transport of material between cytoplasm and nucleus. It's main function is to manufacture and transport protein, fats and lipids. It also helps in membrane biogenesis.

subject: Biology
question_text: A group of ribosomes attached to mRNA is known as: [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Polysomes
Monomer and polymer: Monomers are small molecule e.g. amino acid, sugars. While polymers are repeating chains of monomer. E.g. protein (chain of amino acids), DNA, cellulose. Polypeptide: chain of amino acid linked by peptide bonds.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which type of cell lacks a membrane-bound nucleus? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Prokaryotic cells
Instead of membrane bound nucleus prokaryotic cells have nucleus like undefined structure called nucleoid.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the function of the Endoplasmic Reticulum (ER)? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Lipid synthesis and detoxification
Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum - Due to absence of ribosomes it synthesizes fats and lipids. Rough endoplasmic reticulum - It synthesizes protein as they have protein forming units called ribosomes. It is also involved in formation of hydrolytic enzymes for lysosomes.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which cell organelle is defined as the small round organelle that undergoes oxidation reactions to produce hydrogen peroxide? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Peroxisomes
Helps in detoxification of reactive oxygen molecules like hydrogen peroxide by breaking it down into water and oxygen through metabolic processes. Also carry out beta-oxidation of fatty acids.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following organelles is not found in animal cells? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(C) Cell wall
In plants and some organisms there's an extra protective covering called cell wall. It is non-living and freely permeable and provide strength and turgidity. In plants, it's made up of cellulose and hemicellulose. In bacteria: peptidoglycan. In fungus: chitin and In algae: pectin. *Glycocalyx - is the outermost layer of bacterial cell.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following best represents the cells of a meristem of plants? [CAPF 2022]

options.a: Dense cytoplasm, thin cell wall, large nuclei and absence of vacuoles
options.b: Dense cytoplasm, thin cell wall, large nuclei and large vacuoles.
options.c: Dense cytoplasm, thin cell wall, small nuclei and absence of vacuoles
options.d: Dense cytoplasm, thick cell wall, small nuclei and large vacuoles.

solution:
(A) Dense cytoplasm, thin cell wall, large nuclei and absence of vacuoles
Use elimination: Vacuoles are absent in meristematic plant tissue because they need to divide. The generally occur in growing regions of plant and are of three type a) apical-in the tips of root and shoots. Increase length of root and stem. b) intercalary-in the base of leaf and increase length of leaf and internodes. c) lateral/cambium-present beneath the bark and increase girth of stem.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following combinations correctly represents the genetic materials in prokaryotes and eukaryotes respectively? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(A) Nucleoid and chromatin
Prokaryotic cells lack true nucleus and only have a undefined structure called nucleoid hence nucleoid represent genetic material in prokaryotes. In eukaryotes, chromatin material is present inside nucleus as a thread like structure and represent genetic material. Chromatin material gets organised into chromosome during division.

subject: Biology
question_text: Lysosomes are sacs of the cell filled with digestive enzymes. These digestive enzymes are synthesised by [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(C) Rough endoplasmic reticulum
Lysosome acts as a waste disposal system to cell. They digest foreign material by releasing hydrolytic enzymes these hydrolytic enzymes are formed in rough endoplasmic reticulum while lysosome itself formulate in Golgi body. Rough endoplasmic reticulum contains ribosomes which synthesize proteins.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which organelle other than nucleus in eukaryotic cells has its own DNA, ribosomes and proteins? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(B) Mitochondria
Mitochondria and plastids are two cell organelles that contain their own DNA and ribosomes but plastids are present only in plant cells while mitochondria is present in all eukaryotes. Nucleosomes - basic structural unit of chromatin. It consist of segment of DNA wrapped around histone proteins.

subject: Biology
question_text: For protein synthesis, the amino acids are recognized and carried by: [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(D) tRNA
tRNA - Transfer RNA. It uses specific anticodon and acts as a bridge during translation. mRNA - Messenger RNA. It carries genetic information from DNA to ribosomes. snRNA - small nuclear RNA. It is involved in RNA processing. miRNA - micro RNA. It regulates gene expression after transcription.

subject: Biology
question_text: In vertebrates, smooth endoplasmic reticulum in cells of which organ plays a crucial role in detoxification of toxic compounds? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(D) Liver
Smooth endoplasmic reticulum - Ribosomes are absent so they synthesize fats and lipids and carry out detoxification. Spleen - Graveyard of RBCs. Liver have specialised cells for regeneration called kupffer cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following structure is NOT found in prokaryotes? [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(A) Nuclear envelope
Nuclear envelope is a membrane surrounding nucleus so it is not present in prokaryotes as they don't have a true nucleus. Nuclear envelope permit transfer of material between nucleoplasm and cytoplasm and separates them.

subject: Biology
question_text: Cell wall is not present in cells of [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(D) Humans
Cell wall is a dead and freely permeable layer which is only present in plant cells and some microorganism but completely absent in animal cells. In plants it is made up of cellulose and hemicellulose and provide strength and turgidity. In fungus it's made up of complex sugar called chitin. In bacteria it is made up of peptidoglycan or peptin.

subject: Biology
question_text: Osmosis is the process of movement of water molecules from its [CDS I 2021]

options.a: higher concentration to its lower concentration through a cell wall.
options.b: lower concentration to its higher concentration through a fully permeable membrane.
options.c: higher concentration to its lower concentration through a fully permeable membrane.
options.d: higher concentration to its lower concentration through a semi-permeable membrane.

solution:
(D) Higher concentration to its lower concentration through a semipermeable membrane
Osmosis occurs through a semipermeable membrane so we can use elimination. It is a passive transport (no energy required). This process last up to equilibrium.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which cell organelles have their own DNA and Ribosomes? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(B) Mitochondria and Plastids
Mitochondria is the main site for production of ATP which occurs in its inner membrane. Plastids are only present in plant cells and are of three types a) chromoplasts-contain pigments b) chloroplast - contains chlorophyll and carry out photosynthesis c) leucoplast - they works as a storage unit.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements is correct about the cell membrane? [CDS I 2022]

options.a: It is made up of cellulose.
options.b: It is a permeable structure for all substances.
options.c: It is flexible and enables the cell to engulf many materials.
options.d: It does not allow diffusion of gases like oxygen and carbon dioxide.

solution:
(C) It is flexible and enables the cell to engulf many materials
Cell membrane or plasma membrane is a thin, elastic and living layer which provides a definite shape to the cell. It is made up of proteins, lipids. It is selectively permeable in nature and facilitate movement of certain molecules.

subject: Biology
question_text: In plant cells [CDS II 2022]

options.a: Nucleus does not need a membrane but chloroplasts are surrounded by their own membrane.
options.b: Nucleus and chloroplasts are surrounded separately by their own membrane.
options.c: Nucleus is surrounded by membrane but chloroplasts do not need membrane.
options.d: Both nucleus and chloroplasts are not surrounded by any membrane.

solution:
(B) Nucleus and chloroplast are surrounded separately by their own membrane
Nucleus is present in all eukaryotic cells and is a membrane bound organelle. It contains of nucleolus and nuclear envelope (membrane). Nucleus is first discovered by Robert Brown in 1831. Chloroplast is a type of plastid which contain chlorophyll pigment and carry out photosynthesis. Magnesium metal is present in chlorophyll.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is a feature of Membrane? [CDS II 2022]

options.a: Bilayer of phospholipid molecule in which proteins and cholesterol are embedded.
options.b: Bilayer of proteins molecule in which lipid and cholesterol are embedded.
options.c: Bilayer of neutral lipid in which proteins and cholesterol are embedded.
options.d: Bilayer of neutral lipid lacking cholesterol and proteins.

solution:
(A) Bilayers of phospholipid molecule in which proteins and cholesterol are embedded.
It is explained under fluid mosaic model of plasma membrane given by Singer and Nicolson in 1972. It describes the structural features of membrane. Cell membrane is a double layered structure in which lipids protein and fat is present. It has two polar ends at extreme and nonpolar ends inside.

subject: Biology
question_text: If the red blood cells (RBCs) of human blood are isolated and are diluted in normal saline (an isotonic solution to blood), what will happen to the RBCs? [CDS I 2023]

options.b: The RBCs will swell and burst
options.d: No change in the diameters of the RBCs

solution:
(D) No change in the diameters of RBCs
There is no change in the diameter because RBC are put in an isotonic solution. It means the solution and cell both are in equilibrium with each other and osmosis last up to equilibrium so there will be no change.

subject: Biology
question_text: In plant cells, RNA is present in [CDS I 2023]

options.c: nuclei, cytoplasm, mitochondria, chloroplast reticulum and endoplasmic reticulum
options.d: nuclei, cytoplasm, mitochondria, chloroplast and ribosomes

solution:
(D) Nuclei, cytoplasm, mitochondria, chloroplast and ribosomes
RNA- Ribonucleic acid. It is a single stranded structure that contains ribose sugar. In RNA Thymine is replaced by uracil. RNA also act as genetic material in viruses.

subject: Biology
question_text: In the following simplified diagram of a plant cell, five parts/organelles are marked from 1 to 5. Which one of the following correctly represents the above parts/organelles? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(C) 1-cell wall, 2-cell membrane, 3-endoplasmic reticulum, 4-nucleus, 5-vacuole
As mentioned in question that it is a plant cell so the outermost layer will be plant cell. Cell membrane is present in all cells so 2nd must be cell membrane. 3rd is connected to nuclear membrane from one side and cell membrane from another so it is a characteristic of Endoplasmic reticulum. 4th is nucleus that contains genetic material. 5th is covering most of the space in the cell so identifying as vacuole.

subject: Biology
question_text: Chromosomes are composed of DNA and: [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(C) Proteins
Chromosome: There are 23 pairs of chromosome out of which 22 pairs are autosomal and one is sex Chromosomes. Chromosomes are comprised of DNA and proteins. These proteins are histone. DNA's double helical structure is discovered by Watson and Crick in 1953. And molecule of DNA is discovered by Friedrich Miescher in 1869.

subject: Biology
question_text: Bacteria have an undefined nuclear region which lacks a nuclear membrane and contains only DNA without proteins. Such a region is called: [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(D) Nucleoid
Nucleoid is a nucleus like undefined structure present in prokaryotic cell like bacteria. Prokaryotic cells lack membrane bound organelles and have only one chromosome. They have 70s ribosome.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is the correct combination of organelles and their functions? [CDS II 2023]

options.a: Mitochondria - Respiration; Chloroplast - Photosynthesis; Ribosome - Protein Synthesis; Rough endoplasmic reticulum - Transport of proteins
options.b: Mitochondria - Respiration; Chloroplast - Photosynthesis; Ribosome - Transport of proteins; Rough endoplasmic reticulum - Protein synthesis
options.c: Mitochondria - Respiration; Chloroplast - Protein synthesis; Ribosome - Photosynthesis; Rough endoplasmic reticulum - Transport of proteins
options.d: Mitochondria - Photosynthesis; Chloroplast - Respiration; Ribosome - Protein synthesis; Rough endoplasmic reticulum - Transport of proteins

solution:
(A) Mitochondria - respiration, Chloroplast - photosynthesis, Ribosome - protein synthesis, Rough endoplasmic reticulum - transport of proteins
Cellular respiration is a process by which cells use glucose to produce ATP and it occurs in mitochondria. Chloroplast contain chlorophyll pigment and carry out photosynthesis. Ribosome is a site for protein synthesis. Rough endoplasmic reticulum has ribosomes and it carry out the transfer of proteins.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which organelle in animal cells can digest foreign materials? [CDS II 2024]
solution:
(B) Lysosomes
Refer question 4.

subject: Biology
question_text: In a mammalian cell, the protein is synthesized by ________. [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(A) Ribosome
Ribosomes are made up of protein and ribosomal RNA. They are not membrane bound. They are the main site for protein synthesis and present in Rough endoplasmic reticulum and freely in cytoplasm. They can read messenger RNA (mRNA) sequence, they translate and carry the amino acids for protein synthesis. Ribosomes are formed in nucleolus.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following cell organelles is not lined by membrane? [CISF AC 2022]
solution:
(B) Ribosome
Detailed explanation in question 22.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
DNA Barcoding can be a tool to:
1. assess the age of a plant or animal.
2. distinguish among species that look alike.
3. identify undesirable animal or plant materials in processed foods.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(D) 2 and 3
DNA barcoding is a method to identify species by using a short segment of their DNA. This process is first carried out by Paul Hebert in 2003. It can distinguish between species and discover new species. It is also used in taxonomy research, in the conservation of species at risk and identify contaminants in food. But it cannot assess the age of a plant or animal.

subject: Biology
question_text: The movement of a substance against a diffusion gradient with the use of energy is known as [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]
solution:
(A) Active transport
Active transport does require energy but passive transport doesn't. Diffusion in itself is a passive transport as the movement is from high concentration to low concentration but when the movement is against the diffusion gradient means lower to higher concentration, the process will require energy hence called active transport. Osmosis is also a passive transport.

subject: Biology
question_text: Movement of materials to different parts of cytoplasm from nucleus is generally carried out by [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]
solution:
(D) Endoplasmic reticulum
Endoplasmic reticulum is a large network of tubes and sheets. It is attached to plasma membrane from one side and outer nuclear membrane from another. It serves as a channel of transport between cytoplasm and nucleus. It also detoxify poison and drugs.

subject: Biology
question_text: In mitochondria, ATP synthesizing chemical reactions occur in the [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(B) Inner membrane (written as (C) Inner membrane in solutions)
ATP formation in mitochondria take place in inner membrane through the process of oxidative phosphorylation where 36 ATP formed. enzyme named ATP synthase also helps in the synthesis of ATP.

subject: Biology
question_text: If human blood is placed in a 2% detergent solution, what will happen to the RBC? [NDA I 2021]

options.b: The RBC will swell and become turgid.
options.c: The RBC will swell and burst.

solution:
(D) The RBC will lyse
2% detergent solution will lead to the disintegration of plasma membrane. Salt of detergent will disturb the lipid bilayer of membrane and cause the breakdown. Hemolysis: Process of breakdown of RBC. Concentration of salt in RBC is 0.9%.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following plant plastids stores starch, oil and protein granules? [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(B) Leucoplast
In leucoplast, starch is stored by amyloplast, oil by elaioplast and proteins by alluroplast.

subject: Biology
question_text: A cell is unable to synthesize lipids. Which of its cell organelles might be defective? [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(A) Smooth endoplasmic reticulum
Smooth endoplasmic reticulum lack ribosomes. They synthesize fats and lipids. Also carry out detoxification. So, when smooth endoplasmic reticulum get defected process of lipid synthesis stops.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements about vacuoles is NOT correct? [NDA I 2022]

options.a: In plants, there is a large central vacuole that may occupy 90% of total cell volume.
options.b: In plant cells, vacuoles provide turgidity and rigidity.
options.c: In Amoeba, vacuoles have role in nutrition.
options.d: Vacuoles are absent in animal cells.

solution:
(D) Vacuoles are absent in animal cells
In plants, Vacuole is a larger structure as it covers more than 90% space. It also provides turgidity and rigidity. In amoeba, vacuole is involved in nutrition as food vacuole digest the food taken by endocytosis with the help of pseudopodia. It also expel the waste. So statements A, B and C is correct. Vacuoles are not absent in animal cells. They are smaller and temporary.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements about animal cells and plant cells is correct? [NDA I 2022]

options.a: Animal cells have only cell membrane not cell wall, whereas plant cells have only cell wall not cell membrane.
options.b: Animal cells have only cell membrane not cell wall, but plant cells have both.
options.c: Both animal and plant cells have cell membrane and cell wall.
options.d: Only some cells of animals have cell wall and all plant cells have cell membrane.

solution:
(B) Animal cells have only cell membrane not cell wall, but plant cells have both
Plant cell have both cell wall and cell membrane for extra protection as they have to bear extra environmental stress. Animal cell only have cell membrane which is made up of protein, lipids and phospholipid.

subject: Biology
question_text: The digestive enzymes are present in [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(C) Lysosomes
Digestive enzymes of lysosomes called hydrolytic enzymes are formed in Rough endoplasmic reticulum. "Suicidal bag of cell".

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following will be resulted when an animal cell is surrounded by a medium with lower concentration of water? [NDA II 2022]

options.b: No change in movement of water

solution:
(A) Cell will lose water
As mentioned in the question, the medium around the cell have lower concentration of water so osmosis will start and the cell will lose water until the concentration of water become equal in cell and the given medium. So the cell will shrink.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following structures is not present in a prokaryotic cell? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(C) Nucleus
In prokaryotic cells, nucleus is replaced by nucleoid which is a nucleus like undefined structure and carries genetic material of cell, floating in cytoplasm.

subject: Biology
question_text: In a plant cell, which one of the following contains their own DNA? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(C) Mitochondria and chloroplast
Refer Question 11.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following structures or components is not always present in living cells? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(A) Cell Wall
Plasma membrane is a living structure and genetic material is present in all living cells. Cytoplasm is a jelly like fluid present in all cells. But Cell wall is a dead structure often present for protective functions. So the answer will be cell wall.

subject: Biology
question_text: During a laboratory experiment, a student immerses epidermal leaf peel in a hypertonic solution. After some time, the student examined the cells under a microscope and observed that: [NDA I 2023]

options.c: the cells built up turgor pressure.

solution:
(B) The cell were plasmolysed
Hypertonic solution is a solution where concentration of solution is less than the concentration of cell. So to achieve equilibrium, cell will lose water. Plasmolysis is the process where cell lose water in hypertonic solution. So answer will be B.

subject: Biology
question_text: Eukaryotic cells are much more complex as compared to prokaryotes. Which one of the following structures is exclusively present in a eukaryotic cell? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(D) Mitochondria
Mitochondria is membrane bound organelle and only present in eukaryotes because prokaryotic cells doesn't have membrane bound organelles. Nucleic acid is present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells but the location differs. Cell wall is present in bacteria and it is a Prokaryotic cells so #A is incorrect. Cell wall in bacteria is made up of peptidoglycan.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements regarding animal cell membrane is correct? [NDA I 2024]

options.a: They are composed of phospholipids only.
options.b: They are composed of proteins only.
options.c: They are composed of phospholipids and proteins only.
options.d: They are composed of phospholipids, proteins and cholesterol (lipid).

solution:
(D) They are composed of phospholipids, proteins and cholesterol (lipid)
Structural features of cell membrane is given by Fluid Mosaic Model of Singer and Nicolson in 1972.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. DNA replication takes place when chromatin is opened up.
2. Chromatin organises itself into rod-shaped chromosomes before cell division.
3. Both prokaryotes and eukaryotes have the same process for cell division.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(A) 1 only
Chromatin organise itself in chromosomes during cell division not before cell division. So #2 is eliminated. Process of cell division in Prokaryotic cells is simpler while in eukaryotic cells it's complex process like mitosis and Meiosis. #3 is incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: In most prokaryotes, the chromosome number is: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) 1
Prokaryotes are simple organisms so the genetic material and information is stored in a single chromosome. They generally reproduce through binary fission and divide into 2 daughter cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Bacterial DNA is referred to as naked because it is not associated with: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(B) Proteins
Protein in DNA is histone and around histone DNA is wrapped. In bacteria, as it is a Prokaryotic cell, the DNA is freely floating in cytoplasm without the histone proteins so it is referred as naked DNA.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following stages of the cell cycle is correctly matched with its characteristics? [GEOSCIENTIST 2025]

options.a: Inter phase: Growth of cell and replication of DNA
options.b: Inter phase: Cell begins to separate its DNA contents
options.c: Mitotic phase: Growth of cell and DNA replication
options.d: Mitotic phase: Both the daughter cells are provided with double the amount of DNA

solution:
(A)
Inter phase: Growth of cell and replication of DNA G1 phase, S phase and G2 phase are collectively called Inter phase. In G1 cell grows and copy organelles except DNA. In S phase DNA synthesis starts and in G2 phase the cells grows further and prepare for division. M phase is the phase where actual division takes place. Inter phase and Mphase are collectively known as Cell cycle.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Consider the following statements about stem cells:
1. Stem cells have the potential for self-renewal via mitotic cell division.
2. In 1908, James Thomas and his colleagues first derived human embryonic stem cells from donated blastocysts through 'in vitro fertilization'.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [CISF AC 2024]
solution:
(A) 1 only
Stem cells have the ability or self-renew is it true but James Thomson and his colleagues derived human embryonic stem cells in 1998 not in 1908. So statement 2 is incorrect. For donation, stem cells are extracted from blood or bone marrow.

subject: Biology
question_text: Microbodies found to be present in plant and animal cells contain [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(B) Enzymes
Microbodies also known as cytosomes are single membraned organelle present in cytoplasm of only eukaryotic cells. E.g. Peroxisomes and glyoxysomes. Enzymes are the protein that act as catalyst and speed up a chemical reaction.

subject: Biology
question_text: Photo 51 refers to an image of: [CDS I 2024]

options.d: the virus responsible for common cold

solution:
(B) DNA Molecules
Photo-51 is a X-ray image taken by Rosalind Franklin and Raymond Gosling in 1952. It played a major role in discovery of DNA double helical structure discovered by Watson and Crick in 1953.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is the correct chronology of invention/discovery in the field of Biotechnology? [CAPF 2024]

options.a: Discovery of Plasmid, Double Helical structure of DNA, DNA fingerprinting, cloning of Dolly
options.b: Double Helical structure of DNA, Discovery of Plasmid, DNA fingerprinting, cloning of Dolly
options.c: DNA fingerprinting, cloning of Dolly, Double Helical structure of DNA, Discovery of Plasmid
options.d: Double Helical structure of DNA, DNA fingerprinting, Discovery of Plasmid, cloning of Dolly

solution:
(B) Double helical structure of DNA, discovery of plasmid, DNA fingerprinting, cloning of dolly
Double Helical structure of DNA: Watson and Crick in 1953. Plasmid: DNA molecule found in bacteria. Plasmid are used in genetic engineering. Discovered by Joshua Lederberg. DNA fingerprinting: 1984 by Alec Jeffrey. Cloning of Dolly: Dolly is a sheep and first mammal to be cloned by using somatic cell nuclear transfer in 1996.

subject: Biology
question_text: Why does the Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum look 'rough' under a microscope? [CDS I 2025]

options.a: Due to the presence of ribosomes on its surface
options.b: Due to the absence of ribosomes on its surface
options.c: Due to the presence of lysosomes on its surface
options.d: Due to the presence of mitochondria on its surface

solution:
(A) Due to the presence of ribosomes on its surface
Ribosomes perform protein synthesis. They are attached to rough endoplasmic reticulum that makes it a site for protein synthesis. Formation of ribosome/ribogenesis takes place in the nucleolus. Formation of hydrolytic enzymes also takes place in RER.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is the correct base pairing found in a normal DNA molecule? [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(A) Adenine pairs with Thymine
Molecule of DNA was discovered by FRIEDRICH MIESCHER in 1869. Double helical structure of DNA was discovered by Watson and Crick in 1953. According to this double helical structure, Adenine pairs with Thiamine (A-T) and Guanine pairs with Cytosine (G-C). In RNA, thymine is replaced by uracil.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which type of muscles do the uterus, iris of the eye, and bronchi contain? [SSC PHASE XII 20/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Smooth muscles
Smooth muscles also called visceral muscles are spindle-shaped fibres. Centrally located single nucleus. They controls the function of peristalsis. Peristalsis is the downward movement of food from food pipe to stomach. Skeletal muscles: Help in body movement and maintain posture.

subject: Biology
question_text: How big are red blood cells in diameter? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(C) 7.5 μm
RBCs are also called erythrocytes and have lifespan around 4 months. Biconcave disc shaped cells. Normal range: 4.5-6.5M per mcL. They contain haemoglobin which has affinity for oxygen. RBCs are red due to iron present in haemoglobin.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which tissue among the following is a protective tissue in the animal body? [SSC MTS 20/10/2021]
solution:
(D) Epithelial tissue
It covers all internal and external organs and are of 4 types. A. Squamous epithelial: thin, flat and irregularly shaped. E.g. skin and mouth B. Cuboidal epithelial: mechanical tissue, cube like cells e.g. sweat, salivary and thyroid glands, lining of kidney tubules. C. Columnar epithelial: pillar like, taller than wider, performs absorption and secretion. E.g. intestine, gallbladder D. Ciliated: have cilia (hair-like) on free surface which facilitates locomotion. E.g. respiratory tract, urinary tubules of kidney. Helps in movement of mucus, urine, egg by rhythmic beating of cilia.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a type of blood cell made by the bone marrow? [SSC MTS 05/10/2021]
solution:
(A) Osteoclasts
Osteoclasts are cells that dissolve old and damaged bone and help remodel bone. Blood cells: RBC, WBC and platelets are blood cells. Blood cells move into liquid matrix called blood plasma which constitute 55% of blood and rest are cells. In plasma, 92% is water and rest 8% is enzymes, proteins and other solids.

subject: Biology
question_text: The blood platelets are also known as ________ [SSC MTS 16/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Thrombocytes
Blood platelets: Also called thrombocytes have a lifespan of about 7-10 days. They are smallest blood cells. Normal range: 150k-450k per microlitre of blood. Platelets helps in clotting of blood. Erythrocytes is a name for RBCs and Leucocytes for WBCs.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is the largest dental tissue? [SSC CHSL 11/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Dentin
Tooth is divided into 3 parts: root, neck and crown. Dentin is present in the interior of tooth and is a major component. Enamel: Outer covering of crown of teeth and is the hardest part of whole body. Made up of Calcium phosphate. Pulp- Soft tissues by which blood vessels and nerves are connected. Cement: protective covering of root.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following microscopic cells are of an amoeboid shape with a distinctive central membrane? [SSC CHSL 06/06/2022]
solution:
(B) White blood cells
White blood cells are irregular in shape and so is amoeba. That's why in question it is mentioned as amoeboid. Their lifespan is around 12-20 days and normal range is 4k-10k. They are also called leucocytes and soldier of body as they provide protection from infection. RBC is further divided into a) Granulocytes b) Monocytes c) Lymphocytes.

subject: Biology
question_text: How do the muscle cells help in movement? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]

options.a: The lining of the vessels inside help in movement.
options.b: The contraction and relaxation of these cells result in movement.
options.c: The blood flow of the cells helps in movement.
options.d: The thickness of the cell layer helps in movement.

solution:
(B) The contraction and relaxation of these cells result in movement.
Muscle cells are elongated and large sized. Contractile protein present which helps in the movement. Calcium ions responsible for muscle contraction and magnesium ions are responsible for muscle relaxation.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a large bean-shaped lymphoid organ in the human body? [SSC CHSL 16/04/2021]
solution:
(B) Spleen
It is a part of lymphatic system. It produce WBCs and antibodies. Also known as graveyard of RBCs as it destroys damaged RBCs from blood. *Enlarged spleen is a symptom of Kala Azar or Black fever. Thymus: produce T-cells and B-cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: The fluid present between joint that makes the movement of joints easy, is called: [SSC MTS 08/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Synovial
Synovial fluid makes movement easy by providing lubrication and reducing friction between joints. Tendons: Fibrous tissue. It connects muscle to bone. Provide great strength but limited flexibility. Ligament connects bone to bone. Cartilage: flexible, widely spaced out cells present in ear pinna, nose tip, trachea and larynx. It provides support and flexibility to body parts.

subject: Biology
question_text: Adipose tissue is a type of ________ tissue in the human body. [SSC GD 14/12/2021]
solution:
(C) Connective tissue
Adipose tissue: aggregation of fat cells called adipocytes. Present below skin and between internal organs. Helps in insulation and regulation of body temperature. Other connective tissues are Skeletal fluid and areolar. Areolar tissues are of two types dense (ligament, tendon) and loose (present between skin and muscles).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is a reversible, fluorescent red, acidic dye used to stain cytoplasm, red blood cells, collagen and muscle fibres for histological examination? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Eosin
Eosin is generally used combined with hematoxylin. Safranin: used to dye cell nuclei red. Crystal Violet: used to differentiate between gram negative and positive bacteria. Alizarin: used to stain calcium deposits.

subject: Biology
question_text: Muscles are of three types. Which of the following is NOT one of them? [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Sesamoids
Smooth, cardiac and skeletal muscles are types of muscle tissues. Cardiac muscles are involuntary in nature and uninucleated. Have striations and branches. They do not fatigue easily. Helps in contraction and relaxation in wall of heart.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following is a type of connective tissue?
I. Blood
II. Bone
III. Ligament
[SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(C) I, II and III
Blood is a fluid connective tissue composed of plasma (55%) and blood cells and some other solids. Plasma contains proteins, salts and hormones. Bone: Skeletal connective tissue. Hard, rigid and non-flexible. Made of hard matrix (Calcium + Phosphorus). Provides shape and protect organs like brain and heart. Ligament: dense connective tissue which connects bone to bone.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is not a connective tissue? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(C) Smooth muscle
Smooth muscle is a muscular tissue not a connective tissue => C is correct.

subject: Biology
question_text: In some viral infections, number of platelets are reduced quickly. Platelets are essential because [CAPF 2023]

options.a: they along with red blood cells carry oxygen.
options.b: platelets are important to carry food to cells through blood.
options.c: platelets remove carbon dioxide from blood.
options.d: platelets have a role in blood clotting.

solution:
(D) Platelets have a role in blood clotting
Having a low platelet count can increase the risk of excessive bleeding if the person gets injured. Having high count of platelet can also increase risk of blood clotting within the body and halt the blood flow and can cause stroke or heart attack.

subject: Biology
question_text: The lymph is mainly formed from the plasma of blood and it surrounds the cells. The main difference between blood and lymph is: [CAPF 2023]

options.a: Lymph does not contain red blood cells.
options.b: Lymph does not contain white blood cells.
options.c: Lymph contains both red blood cells and white blood cells.
options.d: Lymph does not contain any cells.

solution:
(A) Lymph does not contain red blood cells
Lymph is a colourless fluid that carries WBCs, oxygen and nutrients. It's a type of fluid connective tissue in which RBCs and blood proteins are absent. It is present in intercellular spaces. It resembles plasma as water is the primary component for both of them and both acts as transport mediums.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is the structure of a cardiac muscle cell? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(D) Cylindrical, branched and uninucleate
Cardiac muscle cells are present in wall of heart and helps it to pump and distribute blood throughout the body. These muscles are involuntary in nature.

subject: Biology
question_text: Suppose, due to an accident, the delicate lining of the mouth and the oesophagus of a person is injured. In this case, which one of the following tissues will be affected? [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(C) Squamous epithelium
Squamous epithelium tissues are divided into two different types a) SIMPLE SQUAMOUS: single layered and have delicate lining e.g. mouth and lining of oesophagus. b) STRATIFIED SQUAMOUS: stratified is a word for layer so these are layered tissues and prevent wear and tear. E.g. Skin.

subject: Biology
question_text: Fat in human body is stored in [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(A) Adipose tissue
It provides cushioning and protects body parts. It also regulates insulin sensitivity and immune response.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which cells in human body have no nucleus? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) Red blood cell
When RBCs first produced by bone marrow, they have nucleus but later on macrophages swallow the nucleus and provide RBCs with more space for haemoglobin and oxygen. After losing nucleus, RBCs doesn't undergo any further cellular division.

subject: Biology
question_text: From which among the following are blood clotting factors released? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) Platelets
Refer question 2.

subject: Biology
question_text: The tissue which is responsible for movement in our body is [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(B) Muscular tissue
Muscular tissue have contractile proteins that's why they are responsible for movement. Nervous tissue: Highly specialised tissues made up of neurons and glial cells. They can sense stimuli and send impulses across the body through electrical signals.

subject: Biology
question_text: Tendons connect: [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]
solution:
(C) Muscle to bone
Tendon and ligament are dense connective tissue. Tendon connects muscle to bone (remember by MBT). It is a fibrous tissue which provide great strength but limited flexibility. Ligament provides considerable strength. Little matrix is present. Connects bone to bone.

subject: Biology
question_text: Tissue is formed from cells in which: [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]

options.a: most cells are similar in structure and function.
options.c: all cells are different in structure.
options.d: all cells are different in both structure and function.

solution:
(A) Most cells are similar in structure and function
A group of similar cell which perform similar function is known as tissue. In all the options there is a keyword "different" so they can be eliminated easily.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following sets of elements is essential for the production of red blood cells in human body? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(B) Iron and zinc only
Use elimination. Arsenic is a toxicant and exposure of it can disrupt the process of RBC development. It can also decrease the cell viability. Zinc is essential for formation of RBC. Process of formation of RBCs is known as erythropoiesis.

subject: Biology
question_text: Squamous epithelial cells are found in the inner lining of [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(A) Oesophagus
The lining of oesophagus is delicate so simple squamous epithelial tissue are present there. Columnar epithelial are present in small intestine. Cuboidal epithelial in salivary glands and lining of kidney tubules.

subject: Biology
question_text: Lymph is a tissue fluid present in intercellular spaces. It resembles to [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(D) Plasma
Refer question 3.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is the correct sequence in increasing complexity? [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) Protein - Tissue - Organ - Organism
Tissues are a group of cells and organs are a group of similar functioning tissues example heart, lungs and kidney. These organs together form organ system e.g. respiratory system, reproductive system. All the organ system together forms an organism.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following cells produces antibodies against a foreign antigen? [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(A) Lymphocytes
Lymphocytes are one of the types under white blood cells. They include T-cells and B-cells. They are a major component of Lymph. They fight against infection in our body and low levels can increase the risk of infections. Other two types of white blood cells are granulocyte and monocyte.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following tissues help to connect two bones? [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(B) Ligaments
Cartilage: Skeletal connective tissue. They have extensive Matrix of proteins and sugars. Cartilage is flexible tissue. They provide support and protection to bones and joints. Present in ear pinna, larynx and * Rings of cartilage: trachea. Ligaments connect bone to bone.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Consider the following cell types:
1. Monocyte
2. Chondrocyte
3. Basophil
4. Lymphocyte
How many of the above belong to animal cell types? [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) 4
Monocyte, basophil and lymphocytes are type of white blood cell. Chondrocyte are present in cartilage. Basophil produce histamine and heparin. All these are present in animal cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Clotting of blood involves which one among the following clotting proteins? [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(B) Fibrinogen
Fibrinogen is a soluble protein in blood plasma that produce fibrin which is a thin, fibrous and sticky protein that helps the blood in clotting. It forms a mesh-like structure and traps platelets and blood cells to form clot in response to bleeding. Pathogens are disease causing organisms e.g. virus, bacteria and protozoa. Macrophages and phagocytes are a type of white blood cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which plant tissue does photosynthesis primarily occur? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Mesophyll
Mesophyll tissues are present in the leaves. They contain chloroplast which have chlorophyll pigment essential for photosynthesis. Mesophyll have 2 types: a) Palisade: chloroplast containing. b) Spongy- have very loosely compact cells which facilitates gas exchange efficiently.

subject: Biology
question_text: Husk of a coconut is made of: [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Sclerenchyma
Sclerenchyma: long, narrow, hard and stiff. These are dead cells as they lack protoplasm. They have lignified thick walls and no intercellular space. It provides rigidity and toughness. Inside of a coconut is made up of endosperm tissue.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which part of xylem tissue in plants stores food? [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Xylem parenchyma
Xylem parenchyma are only living structure in xylem. It provides food storage and mechanical support. Thin and rectangular in shape. Cells have cellulose walls. All the other components of like tracheids, vessels and fibres are dead.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following are plant tissues? [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]
I. Meristematic tissue
II. Permanent tissue
solution:
(A) Both I and II
Meristematic tissues are often present in growing regions of plants like root, stem and shoots. They are spherical, oval or rectangular with no intercellular space. They have dense cytoplasm and thin cellulose walls. When meristematic tissues stops growing then they go through differentiation and convert into Permanent tissues. Permanent tissue have thick cellulose walls and a large central vacuole.

subject: Biology
question_text: Cells of meristematic tissue lack: [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Vacuoles
Meristematic tissues lacks vacuoles because they need to divide often as they have active cells. They need to grow the roots, shoots and stem. Meristematic tissues are of 3 types. A. Apical- grow roots and shoots B. Intercalary- present in base of leaves and increase length of leaves and internodes. C. Lateral- they increase the girth of stem.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a part of the apical meristem found in plants? [SSC CHSL 04/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Protoderm
Protoderm go through differentiation in order to form epidermis of root, root hairs. Primordium: present at growing tips of shoots and develops a leaf. Axillary bud: develop branches and flowers. Present at leaf axil (where leaf meets stem).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following plant tissues has large air spaces? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(D) Aerenchyma
Aerenchyma is a type of parenchyma tissue. They are generally present in hydrophytes (submerged aquatic plants). Provide buoyancy to these plants and help them float. They also serve as air channel for the exchange of gases. Chlorenchyma is also a parenchyma tissue which is involved in photosynthetic activities as it have chloroplast and makes up mesophyll cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which layer of the plant tissue Guard cells are present? [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(C) Epidermis
Epidermis is a single layered and outermost layer of all plant parts. Provide protection, prevents water loss. Epidermis secrete a waxy layer called cuticle made up of cutin, which is water resistant and helps to protect from injury and fungus infection. In leaves, it forms stomata. Guard cells guard the stomata and regulate gaseous exchange.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the location of intercalary meristem in plants? [CDS II 2021]

options.a: Base of the leaves or internodes

solution:
(A) Base of the leaves or internodes
Intercalary meristems increase the length of leaves and internodes. Apical meristem is present in the tips of leaves.

subject: Biology
question_text: Xylem is a type of complex tissue in plants for upward conduction of water. Which one of the following xylem tissues consists of living cells? [CDS I 2023]
solution:
(C) Xylem Parenchyma
