Xylem is a complex permanent tissue which performs the conduction of water and mineral from roots to shoot. Movement of xylem is one way. It consists of a) tracheids b) xylem fibres c) xylem parenchyma d) vessels. Except xylem parenchyma all of the component are dead.

subject: Biology
question_text: Water goes up through xylem vessels in tall trees. The process in plants and properties of water that help in upward movement are: [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(B) Transpiration pull, cohesion and adhesion
Transpiration is the loss of water in the form of water vapour into atmosphere by the leaves through stomata. Transpiration pull is a suction pull that generates inside xylem tissue. Cohesion: it forms a continuous column of water in xylem vessels and helps water to move upward. Adhesion: it prevents the water column from breaking by countering the force of gravity.

subject: Biology
question_text: In ovules of Angiosperms, which one of the following is not a haploid cell? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(A) Nucellus
Nucellus is a diploid cell which narration is the embryo sac. When embryo sac forms there are two ends micropylar and chalazal. Synergid and egg cell are situated towards micropylar end and antipodals are towards chalazal end. Two polar nuclei are situated in the middle. Synergids guide pollen tube toward the egg. Antipodals provide nutrition. Egg when fertilized, develops into embryo.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following has non-nucleated cells at maturity? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(B) Xylem Fibre
In tall trees, xylem fibres provide mechanical support. They are a dead component of xylem. Helps in movement and protection of water carrying tissue. Xylem fibres contain lignin and are thick walled. Generally located between vessels and tracheids.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following correctly shows the pathway of water movement in a root? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(D) Epidermis → Cortex → Endodermis → Pericycle
In roots, epidermal cells are present as root hairs which absorb water from soil. Cortex is situated between epidermal cells and conducting tissue and aids in water transport. Endodermis is the inner layer of roots which helps in maintaining the flow of water. Pericycle is made of parenchyma or sclerenchyma and present inside endodermis. It encircles the vascular tissues.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which one of the following statements is/are correct for a vascular bundle in which xylem and phloem are jointly situated along the same radius?
1. These are called conjoint vascular bundles.
2. Cambium may be present in such vascular bundles.
3. Conjoint vascular bundles are always of closed type.
Select the answer using the code given below. [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(B) 1 and 2 only
In Conjoint vascular bundles xylem and phloem are jointly situated along the same radius. These vascular bundles can be divided into two types on the basis of presence of cambium: open (cambium present) and closed (cambium absent). Hence statement 3 is incorrect as they are not always closed. Open vascular bundle are generally present in dicots while closed are in monocots.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following is living and non-nucleated at maturity? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(A) Sieve tubes
Sieve tubes are a part of phloem along with companion cells, phloem parenchyma (food storage) and phloem sclerenchyma. Phloem parenchyma is a dead structure and provide rigidity and support. Phloem conduct food and the moment of phloem is two way.

subject: Biology
question_text: Transformation of meristematic cells into specific permanent tissues occurs by the process of [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(A) Cell differentiation
Cell differentiation is a process by which unspecialised cells become mature and take on specialised form. Meristematic cells generally perform the function of increasing length and girth but once they become mature they undergo differentiation and become permanent tissue which have a centrally located large vacuole.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is the main driver in explaining the cohesion-tension-transpiration pull model of water transport in plants? [GEOSCIENTIST 2025]
solution:
(B) Transpiration
It is firstly explained by Henry Dixon and John Joly. They explain the upward movement of water against the gravity. For explanation of cohesion-transpiration refer question Q5.

subject: Biology
question_text: Cambium is an example of: [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(A) Lateral Meristem
Meristematic plant tissues are present in growing region of plants. They are active and doesn't have any vacuoles or intercellular space. They have dense cytoplasm and thin cellulosic cell wall. (refer question Q5)

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following sequences is correct, according to taxonomy? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]

options.a: Class - Phylum - Family - Order - Genus - Species
options.b: Class - Phylum - Order - Family - Genus - Species
options.c: Family - Phylum - Class - Order - Genus - Species
options.d: Phylum - Class - Order - Family - Genus - Species

solution:
(D) Phylum - Class - Order - Family - Genus - Species
Taxonomy: Greek work taxis means arrangement. It is the scientific study of identifying, naming and classification. Carl Linnaeus: Father of modern taxonomy.

subject: Biology
question_text: The numerical taxonomy of plants is based on which of the following? [SSC CGL 11/04/2022]
solution:
(A) All observable characteristics
The concept of numerical taxonomy is given by Robert R. Sokal and Peter Sneath in 1963. It focuses more on numerical algorithms using computer. They give equal weight to all the characteristics of organisms.

subject: Biology
question_text: Who published Systema Naturae in 1735, classifying the three kingdoms of nature and outlining the sexual system for the classification of plants? [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Carolus Linnaeus
He developed binomial nomenclature. Systema Naturae provided the foundation for modern taxonomy. He described 3 kingdoms in this book named stones, plants and animals.

subject: Biology
question_text: Plants that do not have a well-differentiated body design fall into this group. The plants in this group are commonly called algae and are predominantly aquatic. [SSC CGL 06/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Thallophyta
Study of Algae: Phycology. Some of them are edible. Chlorella is a green algae which is high in protein content. Spirulina is a blue green algae used as superfood by astronauts. Sargassum is a brown algae which is also edible.

subject: Biology
question_text: ________ are the amphibians of the plant kingdom. [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Bryophyta
They have a more differentiated body than thallophytes but no vascular tissues. Generally found in damp, moist and shady areas. They have rhizoids (tiny hair like structure). E.g. Riccia, Moss/Funaria, Marchantia, liverwort and hornwort.

subject: Biology
question_text: Ferns, horsetails, and lycophytes belong to which of the following groups? [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Pteridophyta
First terrestrial plants. Well differentiated body. Pteridophytes are called cryptogams as they have hidden reproductive organs. They have vascular tissues. Generally found in crack of rocks. They reproduce by spore formation, example: Marsilea.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following is an example of Angiosperm in the Plant Kingdom? [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Tomato
Angiosperms are flowering plant and have covered seeds. These are the most common type of plants. At maturity, ovary become fruit and ovules become seeds. These plants provide many useful products like fruits, vegetables, flowers and grains. *Chinchona tree bark is used to make quinine - a medicine for malaria treatment.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following are non-vascular flowerless plants in the taxonomic division of Bryophyta? [SSC CHSL 09/06/2022]
solution:
(A) Mosses
Mosses have a little differentiated body and prefer wet and moist surroundings. They form dense clumps. They produce spores for reproduction and have unbranched stalk with a single capsule which have spores at the top of it. Their size is generally around 0.2 to 10 cm. However some are taller. *Tallest moss-Dawsonia (50cm).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is a multicellular filamentous green alga consisting of thin unbranched chains of cylindrical cells and is found in floating masses near the surface of streams and ponds? [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Spirogyra
Green algae are also known as chlorophycae. Spirogyra are free floating algae and prefer freshwater. Spirogyra is single-celled but these cells are connected from each other and form multicellular filaments as they live in colonies. Ectocarpus is a multicellular brown algae.

subject: Biology
question_text: Sporophyte stage is dominant in which plant group? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Vascular plants
Vascular plants are those which have specialised tissues (xylem, phloem) for conducting water and food. They include pteridophytes, gymnosperms and angiosperms. In bryophytes, gametophytic stage is more dominant and they do not have vascular tissues.

subject: Biology
question_text: The structure that produces and holds sperm cells in bryophytes (non-vascular plants) and ferns is called: [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Antheridium
Archegonium: Female reproductive organ in bryophytes. Archegonium is non motile while antheridium are motile and need water to swim towards archegonium for fertilization. Megasporangia: A name for ovule. A part of female reproductive part of plant which produce megaspores. They are protected by integuments.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following are cryptogams? [SSC CHSL 07/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Pteridophyta
Pteridophytes are also called cryptogams as they have hidden reproductive organs and no true flowers or seeds.

subject: Biology
question_text: The blue-green alga Anabaena lives in the leaves of a freshwater fern, forming which of the following relationships? [SSC MTS 12/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Symbiotic
Symbiotic relationship is a type of interaction in which both the organisms get benefits from each other. Anabaena provides nitrogen to azolla and it provides habitat to anabaena. This relationship is extremely useful for the cultivation of rice due to its nitrogen fixation characteristic. Parasitic relationship benefits one organism and harm other one. E.g lice in hairs. Saprophytic organisms derive their food from dead and decaying matter. E.g yeast and rhizopus (bread mould).

subject: Biology
question_text: The organisms that do not have a defined nucleus or organelles are classified into ________ Kingdom. [SSC CGL 14/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Monera (written as (C) Monera in solutions text, but matches option B/Monera)
Monera are prokaryotic and microscopic organisms. They can be autotrophic (cyanobacteria) or heterotrophic (vibrio). Monera are further divided into two types. a) Archaebacteia b) Eubacteria. All Eubacteria have cell wall while some of Archaebacteia doesn't.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the reason for the similar size of the vascular bundles in monocot leaves? [SSC CGL 11/08/2024]
solution:
(B) Parallel venation (written as (C) Parallel venation in solutions text)
Venation is arrangement of veins in a leaf. Monocots and dicots are further division of angiosperms based on number of cotyledons. Monocots have single cotyledon and parallel venation with fibrous roots. E.g. wheat, corn and sugarcane. Dicots have two cotyledon and branched venation with tap roots. E.g. pea, apple and cashew.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the cottony growth over a stale and moist bread known as? [SSC CHSL 12/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Fungi (written as (B) Fungi in solutions text)
Study of Fungi: Mycology. It is a fungi named rhizopus or bread mould that grows over a stale bread as it is saprophytic and absorb nutrients from decaying matter. They have mycelium: branched filaments network. Uses of fungi: penicillium: used as medicine. Yeast: used in bakery. Mushroom: edible fungi.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is a flowerless spore-producing plant that is usually small, horn-like, or needle-like capsule with spores that develop from a flat green leaf? [SSC PHASE XI 30/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Hornwort
Hornwort is a bryophyte. Chara: it is a green algae that comes under thallophyta as it doesn't have a differentiated leaves, root and stem. Celandine: it is an angiosperm. It is a herbaceous flowering plant. Riccia: it is also a bryophyte but it has a flat and thallus like structure. Hornworts have developed stomata while Riccia lacks stomata.

subject: Biology
question_text: Flowerless plants, naked seeds, needle-like leaves, and cone-like reproductive structures are the characteristics of ________ [SSC PHASE XII 20/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Gymnosperms (written as (B) Gymnosperms in solutions text)
Gymnosperms: A type of phanerogams. Gymnosperms are generally evergreen and Woody plants. In gymnosperms, xylem vessels and phloem companion cells are absent. E.g. Pine, Deodar and Cycas. *1st gymnosperm garden: Uttarkashi, Uttrakhand

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is/are a 'Gymnosperm'? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Cycas (written as (B) Cycas in solutions text)
Cycas bear naked seeds that's why they are categorised under gymnosperms. It is a dioecious plant. It is used to cure skin diseases, snake bite :) and hypertension. Rose is an angiosperm. Mosses are bryophytes and Fern is a pteridophyte.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is/are Pteridophyta? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Fern (written as (C) Fern in solutions text)
Fern are categorised under pteridophyte as they don't have seeds or flowers and reproduce through spores. Lichen is a symbiotic relationship between fungi and blue green algae. Algae provide food to fungi by photosynthesis while fungus provide habitat and protection to algae. Lichens are natural indicator of air pollution. *1st lichen park: Munsyari, Uttrakhand.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is the middle layer in the root apical meristem that gives rise to the cortex? [SSC CGL 21/04/2022]
solution:
(A) Periblem
Calyptrogen: It is a layer of rapidly dividing cells present at the root tip and forma root cap. Dermatogen: It is a layer of cells that forms outermost layer i.e. epidermis in plant. Plerome: Central core of primary meristem of plant. Also called procambium. It gives rise to stele.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a characteristic of fibres in relation to plant anatomy? [SSC CGL 13/04/2022]

options.c: Responsible for conduction and also mechanical support

solution:
(A) Possess only pitted thickenings
Fibres are elongated cells with thick walls of lignin and cellulose. They support stem and root tissues in plants. They are made up of scelrenchyma cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following pairs is INCORRECTLY matched? [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Pteridophyta - Cycas
Cycas is a gymnosperm not a pteridophyte because they have exposed reproductive organs and highly evolved than pteridophytes. Gymnosperms are commonly found in cool, dry and highly elevated regions. Pteridophytes have hidden reproductive organs.

subject: Biology
question_text: Plants which are grown under shade are known as: [SSC CHSL 27/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Sciophytes
Sciophytes are also known as photophobic or shade-loving plants. They have thinner leaves with more chlorophyll. E.g. orchids, money plant. Helophytes: Bog plants/ plants that grows in wet or marshy area. They are generally partially submerged. E.g. Pickerelweed, Sweet Flag. Psammophytes generally grow in unstable sand like desert, beach and dunes. E.g. sand rice and grey hair grass.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Fungi can grow on which of the following items?
I. Bread
II. Pickles
III. Leather
IV. Clothes
[SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(C) I, II, III and IV (written as (C) in solution but matches option D)
Fungi can grow in any habitat in soil, land or plant materials. They need a optimum temparature around 20-30°C and prefer low moisture and pH Conditions. They can be unicellular or multicellular. They are heterotrophic. Can be parasitic or saprophytic.

subject: Biology
question_text: Plants with green and tender stems are called: [SSC MTS 04/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Herbs
Example of herbs: Basil, tulsi and mint. Shrubs are bigger than herbs and have a bushy appearance. They have multiple stems. E.g. rose. Trees: big,tall and Woody plants with a single stem. E.g. Banyan tree. Canopy: It is a overhead roof life structure made by tree branches and crowns. It is visible when looked from above. Canopy gives shelter to many forest animals. Generally seen in tropical rainforest.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following is Phanerogams, flowering and seed-bearing plants? [SSC MTS 16/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Gymnosperms
Refer question 18.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which phylum of the plant kingdom consists of vascular plants, leaves (known as fronds), roots, and sometimes true stems and entire trunks in tree ferns? [SSC CHSL 02/06/2022]
solution:
(B) Pteridophyta (written as (B) in solutions text, but matches option C)
Refer question 6.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following plant kingdoms are divided into liverworts and mosses? [SSC MTS 19/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Bryophytes
Bryophyte is divided into 3 classes: hornwort, liverwort and mosses. Hornworts are found near wet and moist land and have elongated horn like structure. E.g. Anthoceros. Liverwort live near water sources and moist rocks and soil. E.g. Riccia, Marchantia. Mosses have a distinguished feature called 'rhizoids'. E.g. Funaria.

subject: Biology
question_text: What do you call the water holding compounds produced in large quantities by marine brown and red algae? [SSC CHSL 26/05/2022]
solution:
(C) Hydrocolloids (written as (C) in solutions but matches option B)
Hydrocolloids are widely used in food industry as emulsifying and thickening agents. They can be natural or synthetic. Some algae derived hydrocolloids are agar, alginate and carrageenan. Gelatin: It's a type of protein derived from animal collagen.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following plant kingdoms are divided into Dicotyledons and Monocotyledons? [SSC MTS 16/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Angiosperms
Angiosperms are divided into monocots and dicots on the basis of number of cotyledon they bear. Monocots have single cotyledon, complex arrangement of vascular bundles and petals in multiples of 3. E.g. tulips, ginger and cardamom. Dicots have two cotyledons and ring arrangement of vascular bundles and tap roots, having 4 or 5 petals. E.g. mango, papaya and neem.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is not a subgroup of the plant kingdom? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Hemichordata
Hemichordata is a sub-phylum under phylum chordata in animal kingdom. In hemichordates, notochord is not fully developed. E.g. balanoglossus.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the correct statement about green algae. [SSC CGL 07/12/2022]

options.a: Green algae usually have a soft cell wall made up of an inner layer of pectose.
options.b: Green algae usually have a rigid cell wall made of an inner layer of protein and an outer layer of fibre.
options.c: Green algae usually have a rigid cell wall made of an inner layer of cellulose and an outer layer of pectose.
options.d: Green algae usually have a rigid cell wall made of an inner layer of fat and an outer layer of protein.

solution:
(C) Green algae usually have a rigid cell wall made of an inner layer of cellulose and an outer layer of pectose
Green algae also known as chlorophycae as they contain chlorophyll which help them to carry out photosynthesis. They act as food for humans and aquatic animals. E.g. spirulina, chlamydomonas, spirogyra.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match column A with column B.
Column A (Type of algae) | Column B (Proper Name)
a. Blue-green algae | i. Sargassum
b. Red algae | ii. Chlamydomonas
c. Green algae | iii. Rhodophyta
d. Brown algae | iv. Cyanobacteria
[SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(C) a-iv, b-iii, c-ii, d-i (written as (C) but matches option B/b)
Blue-green algae is also known as cyanobacteria. Red algae also known as rhodophycae. E.g. agar- used to make jelly and also as thickening agent. Green algae: chlorophycae. Brown algae: Sargassum - edible algae found in north Atlantic sea also known as sargasso sea.

subject: Biology
question_text: Select the incorrect statement about Gymnosperms. [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]

options.a: Gymnosperms are found as woody shrubs, trees, or lianas and include no true aquatics and few epiphytes.
options.c: Gymnosperms are typically slow in terms of reproduction: up to a year may pass between pollination and fertilization, and seed maturation may require 3 years.
options.d: Gymnosperms exhibit cones or strobili and naked seeds, but not flowers.

solution:
(B) Gymnosperms are seedless flowering plants
Gymnosperms is a type of phanerogams that means they have seeds so they are not seedless and they don't have flowers. So statement B is incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements is correct? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]

options.a: The transport of male gametes in bryophytes and pteridophytes occurs through water.
options.b: The transport of male gametes in angiosperms occurs through water.
options.c: The transport of male gametes in bryophytes and pteridophytes occurs through air.
options.d: The transport of male gametes in Gymnosperms occurs through water.

solution:
(A) The transport of male gametes in bryophytes and pteridophytes occurs through water
Male reproductive part: Antheridium Female reproductive part: Archegonium. Female gametes are non-motile and male gametes are motile so male gametes need a medium to reach female gamete for fertilization. Here, water serves as medium for carrying male gametes to females.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which of the following groups, are all plants monoecious? [SSC CGL 09/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Maize, cucumber, fig and melon
Monoecious: plants that have male and female reproductive part in both in same plant. Also called bisexual plants. E.g. coconut, corn, fig, walnuts. Dioecious: plants that have female and male reproductive part in different plants. Also known as unisexual plants. E.g. papaya, hemp, kiwi and asparagus. #papaya is a dioecious plant so statements A, B and D can be easily eliminated.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify a marine alga that is used as food. [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Sargassum
Sargassum is brown algae found floating in sargasso sea. It consists of 3 parts: holdfast, frond and stipe. It is rich in nutrients such as vitamins, proteins and fibres and widely used as a food for animals and humans. It helps control blood pressure. It has anti-inflammatory and anti-microbial properties. Spirogyra, volvox and ulothrix are green algae.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a diverse group of aquatic organisms capable of photosynthesising? [SSC CPO 09/11/2022]
solution:
(D) Algae
Algae doesn't have a well differentiated body. They produce 50% of oxygen present in earth's atmosphere for photosynthesis. Yeast: yeast is an eukaryotic unicellular organisms belongs to kingdom Fungi. Used in bakery for bread making. They reproduce through budding. Protozoa: unicellular and mostly aquatic. E.g. euglena, amoeba and plasmodium.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a type of bryophyte that lives in many environments and is characterised by its small, flattened leaves, root-like rhizoid, and peristome? [SSC CHSL 24/05/2022]
solution:
(A) Funaria
Funaria is a type of moss commonly known as "cord moss". Rhizoids helps funaria to absorb water and nutrients from ground. Cladophora, ulothrix and Ulva are green algae.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following organisms belongs to the kingdom Monera? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(C) Anabaena
Anabaena is a blue-green algae. Filamentous, photosynthetic and bead like structure. They perform nitrogen fixation and used in rice fields. Euglena and diatom belongs to protista. They are unicellular and eukaryotic. Cell wall is present in euglena and they also possess flagella for locomotion. Ulothrix is a green algae.

subject: Biology
question_text: Yeast, which is used to make bread, belongs to the group: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(B) Fungi
Saccharomyces cerevisiae also known as baker's yeast or brewer's yeast. It converts sugars into carbon dioxide and ethanol. Penicillium is also a fungi used in medicine to make antibiotics.

subject: Biology
question_text: Lichens represent the symbiotic relationships between algae and fungi. The algae component present in lichen is best known as [CISF AC 2024]
solution:
(B) Phycobiont
Algae have chlorophyll and performs photosynthesis and makes food for itself and fungus. While fungus being a mycobiont component provide habitat and absorb nutrition from soil. Due to this symbiotic relationship they can survive in extreme conditions. Prion: It's a brain disease occurs due to protein abnormalities in mammals. Symbiont is an organism that lives in association with another organism.

subject: Biology
question_text: Certain species of which one of the following organisms are well known as cultivators of fungi? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(A) Ant
Along with ants some other organisms helps in the cultivation of fungus like beetles, wasps and termites. Ants cultivate fungus for food and fungus helps to decompose organic matter. It can be seen as mutualism or symbiosis.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is defined as a natural biological unit tied together by the sharing of a common gene pool? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(D) Species
Species is a basic unit or lowest taxonomic rank. It can also be defined as a fertile offspring. Ranks of taxonomy (lowest to highest): Species - Genus - Family - Order - Class - Phylum - Kingdom. It can be remembered by a trick: Some Girls Of Our Class Play Kabbadi.

subject: Biology
question_text: Microscopic, branched and thread-like structures, which can grow and absorb nutrition from organic matter, are found in [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(C) Fungi
Hyphae is a branched and filamentous structure of fungi. A mass or colony of hyphae is called mycelium, which is the vegetative structure of fungi and absorb nutrients from organic matter. Fungi doesn't have chlorophyll so they are heterotrophic.

subject: Biology
question_text: In a dicot pot herb, vaseline/vegetable oil was applied on the upper surface of one leaf (Experimental leaf 1) and on the lower surface of another leaf (Experimental leaf 2). Vaseline/Vegetable oil was not applied on the control leaf. The plant was deliberately not watered for several days. Which leaf will dry up last? [NDA I 2021]

options.d: All the leaves will dry up simultaneously

solution:
(B) Experimental leaf 2
Stomata are structure that present on the surface of leaves and they carry out transpiration (loss of water). Stomata are abundant on the lower surface rather than upper surface of leaves. In leaf 1, there's a layer on the upper portion but not on lower so it will dry out faster than control leaf which is first to dry. Whereas in leaf 2 there's a layer at lower surface so transpiration rate will get comparatively slower and it'll remain fresh a long time.

subject: Biology
question_text: During seed germination, the part of the 'embryo' which grows into root is [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(A) Radicle
Seed germination is a process by which a seed grows into a new plant. During seed germination, ovary become enlarged and turn into fruit, ovules that are inside ovary turns into seeds and protected by integuments. Radicle become root and plumule become shoot. This process is fueled by the nutrients stored in the seed called endosperm.

subject: Biology
question_text: How are evergreen plants with woody stems having naked seeds classified? [NDA II 2020]
solution:
(D) Gymnosperms
Gymnosperms are the flowerless, naked seeds bearing plants. They are perennial and woody. In gymnosperms, vessels and companion cells are absent. They are most commonly found in cold and temperate forests. Wind pollinated plants. Conifers, pines, deodar and hemlocks.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following categories does not have photosynthetic pigments? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(B) Fungi
Algae, gymnosperms and angiosperms all have chlorophyll pigment but fungi doesn't. In fact, algae produce around 50% of the oxygen present in earth's atmosphere. Algae are very useful economically e.g. biofuel, food source and removing coliform bacteria from wastewater. Some algae also have iodine content and used in medicine for thyroid treatment.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is not a characteristic feature of fungi? [NDA I 2022]

options.a: Cell wall is made of chitin.

solution:
(C) Can carry out photosynthesis
Fungi doesn't have chlorophyll pigment which is essential for photosynthesis so they can't carry out photosynthesis. Hence statement C is incorrect. Fungi does have a cell wall which is made up of complex sugar chitin. Mycelium is a mass of hyphae and fungi reproduce asexually.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Consider the following statements regarding cell wall composition:
1. Bacterial cell wall is made of peptidoglycan.
2. Fungal cell wall is made of cellulose.
3. Animals lack cell wall and have extracellular matrix made up of sugar and proteins.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(C) 1 and 3 only
Cell wall is a protective and outermost covering, which is not present in animals. In plants, it is made up of cellulose. In bacteria, it is made up of peptidoglycan. But in fungi, it is made up of chitin not cellulose. Hence only Statement 1 and 3 are correct.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements about bryophytes is correct? [NDA I 2023]

options.a: The plant body is a gametophyte.
options.b: They are also called the amphibians of plant kingdom.
options.c: The plant body is attached to the substratum by rhizoids.

solution: (D) Specialised water-conducting tissue are present (written as incorrect statement in solutions text to explain that statement D is the incorrect one, matching the question 'which is NOT correct' or 'which is correct' depending on textbook typo. The solution explains: 'Bryophytes and Pteridophytes are divided on the basis of presence of conducting tissues or vascular bundles. In Pteridophytes, vascular bundles are present but bryophytes are non-vascular plants that means they doesn't have specialised tissues for conducting water. #D is incorrect.')

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following statements is correct?
1. When any plane passing through the central axis of the body divides the organism into two identical halves, it is called radial symmetry.
2. Where the body can be divided into identical left and right halves in only one plane, it exhibits bilateral symmetry.
[SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(C) Both I and II
Symmetry is a basis of classification in the animal kingdom. Organism with radial are generally Coelenterates and Ctenophores. Some organisms doesn't have symmetry i.e. their body can't be divided into 2 equal parts. Such organisms are called Asymmetric. E.g. amoeba, sponges and snails.

subject: Biology
question_text: The animals or plants which can inbreed successfully must belong to the same ________ [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Species
Species can be defined as a group of organisms that can interbreed and produce a fertile offspring. Species is lowest taxonomic rank. A group of closely related species is called genus.

subject: Biology
question_text: The body of the phylum ________ is unsegmented and dorsoventrally flattened. [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Platyhelminthes
They are acoelmate, triploblastic and have organ level organisation. They are flat like ribbons hence called Flatworms. They have common opening for both anus and mouth. E.g. liverfluke, tapeworm, taenia.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which phylum of the animal kingdom is made up of segmented insects like earthworms? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Annelida
Annelida have organ level organisation, bilateral symmetry, true coelom and are triploblastic. They have soft and elongated body with segments. They can be hermaphrodite (both male and female reproductive organs) or unisexual.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which species of sponges is commonly called glass sponge due to the presence of silica spicules? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Euplectella
It belongs to phylum porifera which is commonly known as sponges. They are asymmetric, diploblastic and non-motile. They have cellular level of organisation and no coelom.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is an example of Phylum Arthropoda? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Butterfly
Arthropoda is the largest phylum in Kingdom animalia. The classification of five kingdoms is given by Robert Whittaker in 1969. The five kingdoms are - Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae and Animalia. These kingdoms are further divided into phylums.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following organisms belong to the Phylum Protozoa? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Amoeba, paramecium, plasmodium
Protozoa under protista are unicellular eukaryotes. They have appendages for locomotion. They can be autotrophic or heterotrophic. They are mostly aquatic and can live in colonies as well as solitary.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the largest phylum in the animal kingdom. [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Arthropoda
Arthropoda is the largest phylum in Kingdom Animalia. They have jointed legs and open circulatory system. They are generally bilaterally symmetric, coelmate and have organ level organisation. E.g. cray fish, beetle and butterfly.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following classes has the largest number of animals? [SSC PHASE XII 20/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Insects
Insecta is the largest class and comes under phylum arthropoda. Their body is divided in head, thorax and abdomen. Some of them have wings e.g. mosquito. They are invertebrates i.e. do not have backbone. They are generally hexapods (6 legs).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following does NOT belong to the category of Porifera? [SSC CGL 17/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Hydra
Porifera are known for pores that lead to large body cavity called spongocoel. They act as water canal system. Most porifiers have power of regeneration. They are non-motile and mostly aquatic. E.g. sycon, spongilla and Euplectella.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements is correct with respect to chordates? [SSC CGL 11/04/2022]

options.b: Heart is dorsal or laterally placed or absent

solution:
(A) Notochord is present
Chordates have a rod like structure called notochord. A dorsal, hollow and tubular nerve cord and a ventral heart. They have a post anal tail and thyroid gland. They can be divided into two types based on the development of notochord. a) Protochordata b) Vertebrate

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT an animal of Phylum Chordata? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Earthworm
Earthworm belongs to phylum annelida as it has segmented, soft and elongated body. It does not have a notochord at any stage of its life. So it is not considered as chordate.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following symmetries is exhibited by arthropods? [SSC CGL 18/04/2022]
solution:
(D) Bilateral
Arthropods have bilateral symmetry that means their body can be divided into two identical halves by only one plane. Humans also show this kind of symmetry. Some organisms can be divided into identical halves by multiple planes, it is called radial symmetry. E.g. starfish, sponges and snails.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which group of animals has a segmented spinal column together with a few primitive forms in which the backbone is represented by a notochord? [SSC CHSL 01/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Vertebrata
Vertebrates have a fully developed notochord. It is a sub phylum under chordates. It is further divided into five classes: a) Cyclostomata b) Amphibians c) Reptiles d) Aves e) Mammals.

subject: Biology
question_text: Arachnids belong to which subphylum of the phylum Arthropoda? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Chelicerata
Arachnids have four pair of legs and mandibles. They don't have any antennae and wings. They have jointed head and thorax called cephalothorax. E.g. spiders, ticks.

subject: Biology
question_text: Select the incorrect statement about Phylum Arthropoda. [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]

options.a: Class Insecta is the largest class of Phylum Arthropoda.
options.b: Lignin is the main component of the exoskeletons of arthropods.
options.c: Over 85% of all the known animal species are arthropods.
options.d: Phylum Arthropoda comprises a wide diversity of animals with hard exoskeletons and jointed appendages.

solution:
(B) Lignin is the main component of the exoskeletons of arthropods
Arthropods are unisexual organism that have open circulatory system and respire through gills and trachea. They excrete through malpighian tubules. All arthropods have a hard exoskeleton which is made up of chitin instead of lignin hence statement B is incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which phylum includes organisms with radial symmetry, such as sea anemones and jellyfish? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Cnidaria
They are generally aquatic and soft organisms but they become hard because of calcium and mineral deposition. This hard deposition acts as exoskeleton and generally made up of calcium carbonate. They have common opening for anus and mouth. E.g. coral, box jellyfish, purple jelly.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following choices is related to the phylum Coelenterata? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Aurelia
They are commonly known as moon jelly or jellyfish. They have tentacles. They fall under cnidaria/coelentreta which have tissue level organisation, radial symmetry and diploblastic in nature. Sycon, spongilla and euspongia belongs to porifera.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following characters are correct about Phylum Echinodermata? [SSC CHSL 03/06/2022]
solution:
(B) Coelom present, bilateral symmetry
Echinodermata have spiny skin and they are free living. They does not have segmentation. They are shape can be spherical, elongated or star shaped. They are aquatic organisms with organ level organisation.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following classes includes the family of turtles? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Reptilia
Reptiles are cold blooded animal with a three chambered heart except crocodile (4 chambers). They are vertebrates as they have fully developed notochord. They have scales (hard plate like structure) on their body.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following characteristics is NOT of Aves? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]

options.a: They give birth to live young ones with some exceptions those lay eggs.

solution:
(A) They give birth to live young ones with some exceptions those lay eggs
Aves are birds and they are warm blooded with a complete four chamberd heart. They do not give birth to live young ones instead they lay eggs and the eggs hatch after sometime and the offspring come out. Hence statement A is incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which organisms are classified as Aves? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Birds
Refer question 21 (printed as 'Refer question 22' in solutions page which is a typo).

subject: Biology
question_text: To what phylum does a blood-sucking leech belong? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Annelida
Annelida have soft elongated body which is differentiated into segments. They also have paired locomotory appendages - parapodia e.g. nereis. There respire through their body surfaces. Excretion occurs through nephridia. They also have well developed circulatory and digestive system.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following options is associated with the class of cold-blooded animals? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Chameleon
Chameleon belongs to class reptiles. Refer question 20.

subject: Biology
question_text: How many chambers are there in the heart of fishes? [SSC CGL 11/09/2024]
solution:
(B) 2
Fishes are vertebrates. They comes under class pisces and are cold blooded. They have streamlined or elongated body. They use gills for respiration and have closed circulatory system. E.g. tuna, shark.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following pairs of 'Animal - Phylum of animal kingdom they belong' is incorrect? [SSC CHSL 13/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Liver fluke - Nematoda
Liver fluke does not belong to phylum Nematoda. It is a platyhelminthes hence A is incorrect. Platyhelminthes have dorsoventrally flat body like ribbon. Suckers and hooks are present. They are hermaphrodite. E.g. Planaria, tapeworm.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a characteristic of both mammals and birds? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Warm-blooded
Warm blooded animals have the ability to control their body temperature with respect to the outer surroundings. Both birds and mammals are warm blooded. They also have four chamberd heart in common. Pneumatic (hollow) bones are found in only birds so they can fly.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a member of the Arthropoda phylum? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Pila
Pila belongs to phylum mollusca. They have soft body and hard exoskeleton (snails). Their body is divided into head, foot and visceral mass. Their entire body is covered by mantle (thin skin). It is the second largest phylum in Kingdom animalia. E.g. octopus, helix, limax.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match the points under Column A with those under Column B. Which of the following is the correct match? [SSC CGL 12/09/2024]
Column A | Column B
i. Annelida | a. Tapeworm
ii. Arthropoda | b. Sea Star
iii. Echinodermata | c. Ant
iv. Platyhelminthes | d. Earthworm
solution:
(A) i-d, ii-c, iii-b, iv-a
Earthworm belongs to annelida as it have soft and elongated body. Ant is an insect so it belongs to arthropoda. Sea star have spiny skin so it belongs to echinodermata. Tapeworm have flat body like ribbon so it belongs to platyhelminthes.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements most accurately describes the Cyclostomata group? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]

options.a: They are characterised by having an elongated eel-like body, circular mouth, slimy skin and are scaleless.
options.b: They are ectothermic animals having mucus glands in the skin, and a three-chambered heart.
options.c: They are warm-blooded, oviparous, bipedal, feathered, winged and toothless vertebrates.
options.d: Their skin is covered with scales/plates and their hearts have only two chambers.

solution:
(A) They are characterised by having an elongated eel-like body, circular mouth, slimy skin and are scaleless
They are vertebrates. Cyclostomatas are jawless organisms which are cold blooded. They do not have scales on their body. They have paired fins and 6 to 15 pair of gill slits. E.g. hog fishes

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a Protozoan? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Scypha
Euglena, plasmodium and amoeba all are protozoa that belongs to Kingdom Protista. They all are without cell wall except euglena. Euglena has flagella, plasmodium have cilia and amoeba have pseudopods for locomotion. Scypha belongs to phylum porifera.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the third germinal layer found in triploblastic animals. [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Mesoderm
All the organisms have two layers i.e. endoderm and ectoderm. Some organisms have a third layer called Mesoderm and they are called triploblastic. Mesoglea is a jelly like structure that is present in absence of Mesoderm.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a roundworm? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Ascaris
Ascaris is a roundworm that belongs to phylum Nematoda / Aschelminthes. They have cylindrical body and rudimentary digestive and excretory system. They have common opening for both anus and mouth. Ascaris is a pathogen found in the intestine of animals.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following organisms belongs to phylum Apicomplexa? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Plasmodium
Apicomplexa is a group of protozoa. They are obligate intracellular parasites. Plasmodium is responsible for causing malaria. It is spread by female anopheles mosquito which is a blood feeding host for plasmodium.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following pairs is INCORRECTLY matched? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Ancylostoma - Pinworm
Ancylostoma is a roundworm and belongs to phylum Nematoda. They have organ level organisation, bilateral symmetry and are pseudocoelomates. They are triploblastic, unisexual and mostly parasitic in nature. (Note: Ancylostoma is hookworm, while Enterobius is pinworm).

subject: Biology
question_text: What is Typhlops? [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Blind snake
Typhlops belongs to class reptilia, phylum chordata as they have notochord. They are non-venomous and terrestrial but they live near water sources. They have a ring like structure around their neck.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match the Animals in Column A with the Phylum they belong to in Column B. [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
Column A | Column B
I. Jellyfish | a. Mollusca
II. Crayfish | b. Mammal
III. Whale fish | c. Insect
IV. Devil fish | d. Coelenterate
solution:
(D) I-d, ii-c, iii-b, iv-a
Whale is a mammal and warm blooded animal which is viviparous. Crayfish is not a true fish, it is an insect. Devil fish is another name for octopus and they belongs to mollusca. Jellyfish also known as moon jelly belongs to cnidaria/coelentreta.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which from the following is a phylum of animals found in freshwater ponds, lakes, and swamps that provides setae or parapodia for locomotion? [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Annelida
Refer question 4 and 23.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify an organism that has a well-defined muscular pharynx. [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Ascaris
Refer question 33.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which type of liverworts form umbrella-shaped structures that raise gametangia above the main gametophyte body and sporophytes develop below these structures? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Marchantia
Marchantia belongs to Kingdom - Plantae, Phylum - Bryophyta. They have a differentiated body but vascular tissues are not present. They are generally found in damp or moist areas. Rhizoids are present in bryophytes.

subject: Biology
question_text: Meandrina is the generic name of which Coelenterata animal having soft tube-shaped bodies that live together in large groups? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Brain coral
Brain coral are spheroid in shape and slowly growing species. They acts as base of reefs. They do not have brain but their structure looks like brain hence the name. They are often referred as coral polyps.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the animal that does NOT have bilateral symmetry. [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Cnidarian
Cnidarian have radial symmetry that means their bodies can be divided into two equal halves by multiple planes. They are diploblastic and acoelmates. E.g. sea anemone and coral.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which group of Kingdom Animalia has unsegmented, bilaterally symmetrical, soft bodies, with a triploblastic construction (comprising three layers of cells) and lacking a coelom, skeleton, and anus? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Platyhelminthes
Platyhelminthes have flame cells for excretion purpose. Porifera have collar cells for excretion and digestion.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which of the following animal phyla is the body divided into head, thorax, and abdomen? [SSC CGL 26/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Arthropoda
Refer question 8 and 13.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify an endoparasite found in animals. [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Flatworms
Endoparasites are organism that live in the internal organ of a host. Flatworms belong to phylum platyhelminthes that have soft and unsegmented body. They are generally parasite so they feed on other organisms for nourishment like humans and animals.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match the columns. [SSC PHASE XI 27/06/2023]
Column A | Column B
I. Protozoa | a. Roundworm
II. Nematoda | b. Amoeba
III. Arthropoda | c. Star Fish
IV. Echinodermata | d. Cockroach
solution:
(C) i-b, ii-a, iii-d, iv-c
Amoeba is a protozoa and have pseudopods for locomotion. Roundworm lives in intestine of humans and belongs to Nematoda. Cockroach belong to phylum arthropoda as they have jointed legs. Starfish have spiny skin and belongs to echinodermata.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match the points under Column A with those under Column B. [SSC CHSL TIER-II 26/06/2023]
Column A (Phylum) | Column B (Example)
1. Porifera | a. Sponge
2. Cnidaria | b. Squid
3. Mollusca | c. Centipede
4. Arthropoda | d. Jellyfish
solution:
(B) 1-a, 2-d, 3-b, 4-c
Porifera are commonly known as sponges as only option B have matched porifers to sponges so all other options are easy to eliminate.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following does not belong to class Insecta? [SSC PHASE XI 30/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Scorpion
Scorpion belongs to arachnids. They have 8 legs and two mandibles to catch prey. They have two segmented body: cephalothorax (head + thorax) and abdomen.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a group of primitive vertebrates having a large jawless sucking mouth, no limbs or paired fins and a completely cartilaginous skeleton with a persistent notochord? [SSC CHSL 26/05/2022]
solution:
(A) Cyclostomata
They belongs to phylum chordata and have a jawless, sucking circular mouth. They have elongated body with 6 to 15 pairs of gill slits which helps them to respire. They have close circulatory system. They die after spawning.

subject: Biology
question_text: The ________ is an embryonic midline structure common to all members of the phylum Chordata that serves as a source of midline signals to surrounding tissues and as a major skeletal element of the developing embryo. [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Notochord
Notochord is a rod like structure present in all chordates. In some organisms, notochord is not fully developed, they are called Protochordata, e.g. doliolum, salpa. Organisms which have fully developed notochord are called vertebrates.

subject: Biology
question_text: Balanoglossus belongs to which of the following phylums of the animal kingdom? [SSC MTS 04/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Hemichordata
Hemichordata have false notochord. They are marine organisms and have gill slits and a dorsal nerve cord. They are triploblastic and have open circulatory system and true coelom. E.g. acorn worms.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following is an example of Phylum Mammalia of Animal Kingdom? [SSC MTS 16/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Rat
Crocodile belongs to class reptilia and have complete four chambered heart which is an exception. Hen and pigeon belongs to class aves which have four chambered heart and warm blood.

subject: Biology
question_text: Mosquito belongs to ________ phylum of the animal kingdom. [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Arthropoda
Refer question 8 and 13 for Arthropoda. Mosquitoes have jointed legs and are transmitter for many diseases like malaria and dengue. Female anopheles mosquito cause malaria and dengue is caused by Aedes aegypti.

subject: Biology
question_text: Snail is an example of which phylum of the animal kingdom? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Mollusca
Snails have a soft body but hard exoskeleton. They have open circulatory system like arthropods. Mollusca is second largest phylum in the kingdom animalia and includes organisms like octopus, unio and dentalium.

subject: Biology
question_text: Hydra is an example of which of the following phylum of the animal kingdom? [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Coelenterata (written as (C) in solutions text but matches Option B/Mollusca? Wait: Hydra is Coelenterata, but options here are A/B/C/D without Coelenterata. In 27.jpg, option C is Annelida, B is Mollusca, D is Arthropoda, A is Protochordate. The solution 55 in 31.jpg says '(C) Coelenterata
Hydra is a freshwater cnidaria and they have stinging cells...' So there was a typo in the options in the book, where Coelenterata was missing or replaced. We will record the correct answer as 'c' (or other) and document it.)

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following comes under the Platyhelminthes classification in the Animalia? [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Tapeworm
Leech belongs to phylum annelida. They have segmented body. Only Annelida, arthropoda and chordata have segmented body. Sycon belongs to porifera and wuchereria belongs to Nematoda. Wuchereria are infectious parasites that cause lymphatic filariasis.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which group of animals are named because of their spiny skin and have a peculiar water-driven tube system that they use for moving around? [SSC CGL TIER-II 03/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Echinodermata
Echinodermata are spiny skinned organism that are free living and marine. They are triploblastic and coelmate. At larval stage, they have bilateral symmetry but when they get mature they have radial symmetry. They are unisexual and reproduce by external fertilization.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify an example of organisms that are free-swimming and bottom-dwelling forms. [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Zooplankton
Zooplanktons are primary consumer in aquatic food chain and they feed on phytoplankton (producer in aquatic food chain). Phytoplankton are microscopic algae that carry out photosynthesis. Small fishes or secondary consumers eat zooplanktons and big fishes eat small fishes and form a food chain.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the organism that is NOT a pseudo coelomate. [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Scorpions
Refer question 48. Coelom is a body cavity filled with fluid in which internal organs of an organism are present. (Scorpions have true coelom, hence not pseudo coelomate).

subject: Biology
question_text: The body of the ________ is circular in cross-section, hence the name roundworms. They may be free-living, aquatic and terrestrial or parasitic in plants and animals. [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Aschelminthes
Roundworms are bilaterally symmetric, triploblastic and pseudocoelmate. They have a well developed muscular pharynx. They are unisexual and female are often longer than males. They perform internal fertilization. E.g Ascaris, ancylostoma.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify an example of organisms that do NOT belong to the third trophic level. [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Cows
Trophic level is the position of an organism in a food chain. First trophic level is occupied by producers (plants, grass and algae) in every food chain. Producer are eaten by primary consumers such as cows. Second trophic level is occupied by secondary consumers. Cow is a primary consumer hence it can't be placed at 2nd trophic level.

subject: Biology
question_text: Salamander belongs to which of the following classes? [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Amphibian
Amphibians can live in water as well as land. They are body is divided into head and trunk. They are scaleless and have moist skin. They have tympanum as ear. Three chambered heart and unisexual. External fertilization and oviparous in nature.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following cells line the spongocoels and canals of members of the phylum Porifera? [SSC CGL 09/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Collar cells
Collar cells are also called choanocytes. Porifers are hermaphrodites and asymmetrical. They reproduce sexually by fragmentation and have internal fertilization. Generally marine. E.g. sycon.

subject: Biology
question_text: "There are holes or 'pores' all over the body. They are commonly called sponges, and are mainly found in marine habitats". The above statement signifies which of the following phylum? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(A) Porifera
Refer question 63.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify an example of diploblastic animals. [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Coelenterates
Coelenterates have two basic body forms called polyp and medusa. Polyp is sessile and cylindrical e.g. hydra, adamasia. Medusa is umbrella shaped and free swimming. E.g. aurelia. Polar animals are the animals that live in polar region. These animals have thick fur and layers of fats to survive in the harsh and cold conditions. Lemurs are generally found in dry regions and dense forests.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following animals does NOT have a backbone? [SSC MTS 15/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Spider
Spider belongs to phylum arthropoda and class arachnids. They are invertebrate and have 8 legs. Human, pigeon and frog all are vertebrates that means they have a backbone.

subject: Biology
question_text: To which of the following classes do parrots belong? [SSC MTS 18/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Aves
Aves is a name for bird's class. They are warm blooded organisms with complete four chamberd heart and hollow bones which allow them to fly. They are oviparous i.e. lays eggs.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following is/are example(s) of cold-blooded animals?
I. Turtle
II. Snakes
[SSC MTS 25/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Both I and II
Snake and turtle both belongs to class reptilia. So they both have cold blood and three chambered heart. Blind snakes are called typhlops. Cold blooded animals can't adjust their body temperature with respect to their surroundings while warm blooded can.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following organisms does not possess notochord at any stage of its life cycle? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(A) Holothuria
Herdmania belongs to sub-phylum protochordata and have a under developed notochord. Salamander and exocoetus are vertebrate and have true notochord. Holothuria belongs to echinodermata and does not have a notochord at any stage of its life.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following animals has a four-chambered heart? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(D) Crocodile
Crocodile have four chamberd heart as an exception in class reptilia. They are called blooded animals with three chambered heart. Other e.g. lizards, snake and turtle.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following is a free living animal? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(D) Planaria
It belongs to platyhelminthes and free living organisms. They have flame cells for osmoregulation and excretion. Planaria have high regeneration capacity and is hermaphrodite.

subject: Biology
question_text: Animals which are marine, bilaterally symmetrical, have a coelom and a notochord, but never form a vertebral column are placed under which one of the following groups? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(B) Protochordata
They are called Protochordata because they do not have a fully developed notochord. Urochordata and cephalochordates are collectively known as protochordates. While chordates have true or fully developed notochord.

subject: Biology
question_text: Rupa and Sachin observed an animal in their school garden. Rupa called it an insect while Sachin identified it as an earthworm. Which one of the following characteristics confirms that it is an insect? [CDS II 2021]

options.b: Body of the animal had very little segmentation.
options.c: Body of the animal was cylindrical.
options.d: Body of the animal was bilaterally symmetrical.

solution:
(A) The animal had jointed legs
Insect is the biggest class under phylum arthropoda. They are known to have jointed legs and a segmented body. They have open circulatory system and an exoskeleton made up of chitin. Their body is divided into head, thorax and abdomen. E.g. butterfly, mosquito.

subject: Biology
question_text: Cell wall is absent in which one of the following organisms? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(D) Tapeworm
Cell wall is not present in animal cells. Tapeworm is an organism that belongs to Kingdom animalia. Cell wall in bacteria is made up of peptidoglycan.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following animals can perform respiration either through gills or through lungs? [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(B) Frog
Frog is an amphibian which can live on both land and water. When frogs are in water, there respire through their skin. When they are on land, they respire through lungs. Tadpoles stage of frog respire through their gills.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following have species that can establish symbiotic relationship with other organisms?
1. Cnidarians
2. Fungi
3. Protozoa
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [IAS 2021]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Symbiotic relationship is the association between two organism in which both the organism gets the benefit from the interaction. Cnidaria form symbiotic relationship dinoflagellates (coral + dinoflagellates algae). Protozoa form symbiotic relationship with termites, bacteria or corals. Fungi for association with algae to form lichens.

subject: Biology
question_text: Reptilia include animals which are cold-blooded, have scales and breathe through lungs. These include: [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]
solution:
(D) Snakes and turtles
Refer question 68 Level I.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following 'algae' is not known identified partner to form lichen? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(D) Vaucheria
They are also known as "water felt". They are yellow-green algae (xanthophyceae). Lichen is the association between algae and fungi in which fungi is mycobiont component and algae is phycobiont component. Fungus from the outer layer and provide protection. Algae is photosynthetic and provide fungus with food and nutrition.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a true fish? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(D) Sea Horse
Jellyfish belongs to coelentreta and starfish belongs to echinodermata. Silverfish belongs to arthropoda. Only seahorse is a true fish as it belongs to class pisces.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following belongs to Pisces? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(A) Dogfish
Refer question 11.

subject: Biology
question_text: Organisms of which of the following phylum are called sponges? [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(C) Porifera
They are asymmetric, diploblastic and non-motile. They have cellular level of organisation and no coelom. Collar cells are present for circulation and reproduction. Porifers are also known as sponges as they have force that lead to large body cavity called spongocoel. E.g. sycon and spongilla.

subject: Biology
question_text: Sponges belong to which one among the following phylum? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(A) Porifera
Porifera have spores and develop water canal system. They have specialised collar cells for respiration and circulation. Pores lead to large body cavity called spongocoel. They are diploblastic, acoelmates and asymmetric. Other e.g. Sycon, Euplectella, Euspongia.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the type of cell that is long and branched. [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Nerve cell
Nerve cell or neuron is the longest cell in the human body and basic unit of nervous system. Neuron receive and transmit signals throughout the body in form of electrical impulses. These electrical impulses release a chemical "neurotransmitter" that carries the information to brain cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: The vertebrae are a part of which of the following body systems in humans? [SSC CGL 17/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Spinal cord
Spinal cord is made up of neurons which helps it to carry information from body to brain and commands from brain to body. Spinal cord is protected by vertebrae (small bones). These vertebrae form spine or backbone which consists 33 vertebrae and provide structure and support to body. Spinal cord is located inside backbone. Coccyx bone is located at the end of spine.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which part of the brain is instrumental in making it possible for a person to perform an activity such as picking up a pencil from the floor? [SSC CPO 24/11/2020]
solution:
(B) Cerebellum
Cerebellum is a part of hind brain. It is located between cerebrum and base of the brain (brain stem). It is also known as "little brain". It's main function is to maintain posture and balance of the body. It also controls precise muscle movements.

subject: Biology
question_text: Glucagon, a peptide hormone, is produced by the ________. [SSC CGL 20/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Pancreas
Pancreas is a mixed gland. It secretes glucagon by alpha cells. It also secrets insulin hormone (by beta-cells) and regulates sugar (glucose) level in blood. Low glucose level can cause hypoglycemia and higher glucose level can cause hyperglycemia.

subject: Biology
question_text: "Goitre" is a disease of which of the following glands? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Thyroid Gland
Thyroid is the largest endocrine gland which is located in neck region. It is in the shape of butterfly. Thyroid gland produce thyroxine hormone and deficiency of this hormone can cause hasimoto disease. Goitre is caused due to the deficiency of iodine. Endocrine glands are also called ductless glands.

subject: Biology
question_text: Our brain mainly depends on which of the following foods? [SSC PHASE X 02/08/2022]
solution:
(D) Only glucose
Brain takes upto 20% of oxygen and glucose that is consumed by our body. Brain consists of 60% fat. It is protected by skull which have 22 bones (14 facial+8 cranial). Brain weighs around 1300gm in females and 1400gm in males. Between meninges (a protective layer) and skull duramatter is present.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which part of the human brain is responsible for walking in a straight line? [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Cerebellum
As cerebellum maintains the balance and posture of body. Walking in a straight line requires balance. Pons: it is also a part of hind brain along with medulla oblongata and cerebellum. It acts as a bridge between brain and spinal cord. It also regulates sleep, breathing and facial movements.

subject: Biology
question_text: The sensation of feeling full is because of a center associated with hunger, which is in a separate part of the ________ [SSC CHSL 21/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Forebrain
Forebrain is divided into a) Cerebrum b) Thalamus and c) Hypothalamus. Hypothalamus mainly controls hunger. It also regulates body temperature. Thalamus is responsible for pain and other sensations related to taste, touch and smell.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is a degenerative disorder of the brain and one of several conditions that cause dementia, a progressive decline of mental functions resulting in memory loss and confusion? [SSC CHSL 04/07/2019]
solution:
(B) Alzheimer's disease
Dementia is a bigger term that includes many symptoms of brain dysfunction. Alzheimer's disease is a disease that leads to memory loss and other functional difficulties. It is caused by damage in nerve cells of brain. It is irreversible. Amnesia is usually a temporary loss of memory.

subject: Biology
question_text: In 1873, who published the first photograph of nerve tissue stained with the black reaction technique, describing a whole nerve cell? [SSC MTS 01/09/2023]
solution:
(B) Camillo Golgi
According to this photograph, neuron is divided into 4 parts a) Dendrites - branch like structure which recieve signals from other neuron and body. b) Cell body - it contains nucleus and nissl Bodies which helps in protein synthesis. c) Axon- axon connects cell body to terminal. It contains schwann cells, myelin sheath and node of ranvier (gap between myelin sheath). d) Axon terminal - it's the ending of a neuron where electrical signals converts into chemical to reach another neuron through synapse (gap between 2 neurons).

subject: Biology
question_text: With whom did Camillo Golgi receive the Nobel Prize in 1906 for his work on the structure of the nervous system? [SSC CHSL 07/06/2022]
solution:
(D) Santiago Ramony Cajal
The reaction used to describe structure of neuron by staining is called black reaction or Golgi staining. It uses potassium dichromate (to harden) and silver nitrate. Camillo Golgi also discovered Golgi apparatus in 1898.

subject: Biology
question_text: The hypothalamus, a small region of the ________, plays a crucial role in many important functions of the human body. [SSC CPO 13/03/2015]
solution:
(D) Brain
Brain is a part of Central nervous system along with spinal cord. It is divided into 3 parts: A. Forebrain- control voluntary actions. It's further divided into cerebrum, thalamus and hypothalamus. B. Midbrain-regulate eye movement, pupil dilation and transmit information for vision and hearing. C. Hind brain - Cerebellum, pons and medulla Oblongata.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a part of the hind-brain? [SSC MTS 07/10/2021]
solution:
(D) Hypothalamus
Hind brain consists of three parts a) cerebellum: controls posture and coordination b) pons- connects brain and spinal cord c) Medulla Oblongata: part of ANS and regulated respiration, digestion and involuntary functions.
Temporal lobe is a part of Cerebrum. Hippocampus is a part of temporal lobe that processes memory. Occipital lobe: It is responsible for vision. Parietal lobe: It is related to body temperature, touch and movement. Frontal lobe: It's the largest lobe of cerebrum and contains motor controlling centre.

subject: Biology
question_text: Reflex arcs are evolved in animals for quick and efficient responses. Which one of the following sequences correctly represents a reflex arc? [CDS I 2023]

options.a: Receptor - Sensory neuron - Relay neuron in spinal cord - Brain - Motor neuron - Effector
options.b: Receptor - Sensory neuron - Brain - Relay neuron in spinal cord - Motor neuron - Effector
options.c: Receptor - Motor neuron - Relay neuron in spinal cord - Sensory neuron - Effector
options.d: Receptor - Motor neuron - Brain - Sensory neuron - Effector

solution:
(A) Receptor - Sensory neuron - Relay neuron in spinal cord - brain - Motor neuron - Effector
Reflex arc is an involuntary sequence of action that protects body from damage by sensing stimuli. Receptor is an organ by which stimuli is sensed. Then the information is send to spinal cord by sensory or afferent neuron. Spinal cord carries the information to brain and command from brain to neuron and motor neuron transfer the command to the organ by which stimulai is sensed. It is a very rapid and instant process. (Note: in the simple spinal reflex arc, the signal goes through the spinal cord directly to the motor neuron bypassing conscious brain processing, but the solution text states this sequence as matching the key).

subject: Biology
question_text: The cells associated with receiving and conducting impulses are [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]
solution:
(A) Neuron
Neuron is the longest cell in the body and it sense stimuli and send impulses across the body through electrical signals. These electrical signals are converted into chemical at the end of neuron and transferred to other neuron through synapse. Neurons are protected by glial cells. Schwann cells are also a type of glial cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: The transfer of electrical signals by nerve cells in human body is enabled by [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(D) Sodium and potassium
Neurons carries the information from one to another then spinal cord and brain by electrical impulses. These electrical or nerve impulse is excited or triggered by the sodium and potassium pump and makes it to travel from one neuron to other.

subject: Biology
question_text: Insufficient levels of parathyroid hormone and low amount of calcium in the blood causes which of the following conditions? [SSC MTS 04/09/2023]
solution:
(D) Hypocalcemia
Parathyroid hormone (PTH) is secreted by parathyroid gland present near thyroid glands in the neck region. It controls the calcium level in the blood. PTH is released when calcium level is low and it make bones release more calcium. Calcium is important for muscular contraction and nerve activities.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which gland produces Cortisol often known as a 'stress hormone' in humans? [SSC MTS 26/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Adrenal gland (written as Adernal gland in solutions)
Adrenal gland is located on the top of kidneys and it also release adrenaline, aldosterone. Cortisol regulates blood pressure, blood sugar and stress. Low-level of cortisol can cause addison's disease. Adrenaline is also called "fight or flight hormone".

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following hormone is secreted by the brain that helps to regulate sleep-wake cycles? [SSC CHSL 05/02/2021]
solution:
(B) Melatonin
It is secreted by pineal gland located in brain. It also regulates circadian rhythm (body's internal clock). Melatonin is secreted in response to darkness which is detected by retina of our eye. More darkness leads to more melatonin and leads to a better sleep cycle.

subject: Biology
question_text: ________ is associated with the production and metabolism of fats and steroid hormones. [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Smooth endoplasmic reticulum
Smooth endoplasmic reticulum lacks ribosomes and mainly involved in the synthesis of lipids and fats. They also perform detoxification of poison and drugs. They are made up of large network of tubules and sheets.

subject: Biology
question_text: 'Somatotropin' is another name for ________. [SSC CHSL 05/07/2019]
solution:
(D) Growth hormone
Growth hormone is secreted by pituitary gland/ master gland. Deficiency of growth hormone can cause Dwarfism and surplus can cause Acromegaly. Pituitary gland also secrete Anti-diuretic hormone or vasopressin that helps kidney to control the amount of water.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following mineral is essential for the synthesis of thyroid hormones (thyroxine)? [SSC MTS 13/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Iodine
Thyroid is largest endocrine/ductless gland. It is Butterfly shaped and located in neck region. Iodine is essential for secretion of thyroxine and deficiency of iodine can cause "Goitre" disease and Cretinism.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following hormones is known to be helpful in coping with stress? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2019]
solution:
(A) Serotonin
Serotonin hormone controls mood, behaviour and acts as a messenger between body and brain. It is secreted by pineal gland in the brain. Pineal gland also secrete melatonin. Serotonin also controls gastrointestinal issues. Lack of serotonin can lead to depression, anxiety and other mental disorders.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which hormone leads to the expulsion of milk from the breast during sucking? [SSC CGL 04/06/2019]
solution:
(B) Oxytocin
Oxytocin is secreted by pituitary gland and produced by hypothalamus. Prolactin is also secreted by pituitary gland but it is involved in the production of milk while oxytocin helps to release milk. Progesterone is involved in menstrual cycle and pregnancy.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is not a plant hormone? [RRB NTPC 07/03/2021]
solution:
(D) Prolactin
Prolactin is a hormones secreted by pituitary gland in humans after pregnancy for the formation of milk. While Ethylene, Abscisic acid and Gibberellins are plant hormone involved in growth promotion and inhibition.

subject: Biology
question_text: Glucagon, a peptide hormone, is produced by the ________. [SSC CGL 20/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Pancreas
Pancreas is called mixed gland as it performs as both endocrine and exocrine gland, produce both hormones and enzymes. Glucagon is secreted by alpha cells while insulin is secreted by beta cells of pancreas. They both controls level of sugar in bloodstream. Increased level of glucose can cause hyperglycemia and decreased levels can cause hypoglycemia.

subject: Biology
question_text: The atrial wall of the human heart secretes a very important peptide hormone. What is the name of that hormone? [SSC CHSL 12/10/2020]
solution:
(C) ANF
ANF: Atrial Natriuretic Factor. Discovered by De Bold in 1981. It decreases blood pressure by decreasing water, sodium and fats that cause dilation of blood vessels. ADH- Anti-diuretic hormone, CCK-Cholecystokinin, GIP-gastric inhibitory polypeptide.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is termed as a hunger hormone as its stimulates appetite, increases food intake, and promotes fat storage? [RRB NTPC CBT-II 12/06/2022]
solution:
(D) Ghrelin
It works with hypothalamus to regulate hunger. Leptin: It is secreted in intestine to inhibit hunger and controls metabolism. Resistin is produced by adipose tissue.

subject: Biology
question_text: Growth hormone function: [RRB ALP 17/08/2018]

options.d: Sometimes as growth promoters and sometimes as growth inhibitors

solution:
(D) Sometimes as growth promoters and sometimes as growth inhibitors
Growth hormone is secreted by anterior lobe of pituitary gland which is also called master gland. It can promote or inhibit the growth of bone length and muscle growth. It regulates height. Excess level of growth hormone can cause Acromegaly and decreased level of growth hormone can cause dwarfism.

subject: Biology
question_text: Human growth hormone is secreted by which gland? [RRB NTPC 19/01/2017]
solution:
(B) Anterior lobe of pituitary gland
Refer question 13.

subject: Biology
question_text: Phytohormones are: [RRB ALP 21/08/2018]

options.a: Regulators synthesized by plants influencing physiological processes
options.b: Used to measure growth in plants

solution:
(A) Regulators synthesized by plants influencing physiological processes
Phytohormones another name of plant hormones. They regulate growth and development in plants. They can act as promoter as well as inhibitors based on their specific function.

subject: Biology
question_text: Plants require nutrients for their growth which can be supplied by: [SSC MTS 15/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Soil, water and air
Soil provides essential micro and macronutrients specially Nitrogen, Phosphorus and Potassium (NPK). By soil, plant also absorb the fertilizers supplements added synthetically. Some nutrients are water soluble hence absorbed by plant through water. Air provides carbon dioxide, taken by stomata which is essential for photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
question_text: Water comes out of the leaves in the form of vapour by a process called ________. [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Transpiration
Transpiration is the loss of water in form of water vapour through stomata at the daytime. It helps the plant to cool down and regulate temperature. Transportation is the movement of water and nutrients throughout the plant. Venation is pattern of veins in the plant leaf; can be parallel or branched. Creepers grows through adventitious roots, horizontally.

subject: Biology
