question_text: What happens to the guard cells when water flows into them? [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Stomatal pores opens
Guard cells are the protective cells around stomata. They help in opening and closing of stomata and regulate transpiration. When water flows into the guard cell they cause the stomata to open and when water flows out of guard cells it closes the stomata.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following plant hormones regulates growth particularly by stimulating cell elongation in stems? [SSC CGL 23/02/2024]
solution:
(A) Auxin
Auxin is a hormone that promotes growth of plant. It stimulates cell elongation in stem. It grows the tips of shoots and involve in phototropism. Ethylene: It is a gaseous hormone that helps in fruit ripening. It is a synthetically used in agriculture.

subject: Biology
question_text: Plants that are adapted to live under plenty of sunlight are called: [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Heliophytes
Heliophytes need a large amount of sunlight for their optimum growth. They have thick leaves and special pigments to protect them from negative effects of sunlight. E.g. cactus, sunflowers, sugarcane. Neophytes: introduced by humans. Protophytes: lower class plants such as algae and fungi.

subject: Biology
question_text: The leaves have a green pigment called ________, which helps leaves to capture the energy from the sunlight. [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Chlorophyll
Chlorophyll is present in chloroplast a type of plastid and carry out photosynthesis. Xanthophyll is a part of carotenoid group. It gives yellow colour to plants and protects them from intense sunlight. E.g. papaya, pumpkin and corn. Cladophyll is a flattened leaf or petiole. It performs photosynthesis in plants like cacti.

subject: Biology
question_text: The broad, green part of the leaf is called ________. [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Lamina
Lamina is also called leaf blade. Chlorophyll is present in the lamina of the leaf hence photosynthesis occurs here. Petiole is a long and cylindrical stalk. Midrib is thick vein in the middle of leaf and small veins emerge from it in a definite pattern called venation.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following does NOT figure among the main functions of the root system of a plant? [SSC CGL 17/08/2021]

options.a: Supply of food to plant parts
options.b: Providing a proper anchorage to the plant parts
options.c: Absorption of water from the soil
options.d: Absorption of minerals from the soil

solution:
(A) Supply of food to plant parts
Root does not supply food to plant parts as photosynthesis occurs in leaves and the food is supplied throughout the plant by phloem. Root absorb water from soil and supply throughout the plant by xylem. Along with water, minerals are also absorbed by roots from the ground.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following gases is released in the process of photosynthesis? [SSC CGL 12/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Oxygen
Photosynthesis is the process by which plants use water and carbon dioxide in presence of light to make energy and release oxygen. Light is absorbed by chlorophyll pigment. The energy/sugar is stored in form of starch in plants.

subject: Biology
question_text: The loss of water in the form of water droplets from leaves of plants is called ________. [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Guttation
Guttation occurs through hydathodes at night while transpiration occurs through stomata at day time. Translocation is the movement of nutrients throughout the plant by phloem. Plasmolysis is the process where cells lose water in hypertonic solution to attain equilibrium. Pressure gradient enables the movement of water and nutrient in plant tissue.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following pairs is not correctly matched? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(D) Hydrotropic : Flower
Hydrotropism is the movement of a plant part towards water. Flower grows against the stimulus of water hence water is negatively hydrotropic. Chemotropism is movement of plant part towards chemical stimulus. Pollen tube is directed towards ovary by a chemical interaction. Pollen tube is positively chemotropic.

subject: Biology
question_text: A student was doing an experiment on increasing the cell division among plants. She asked her supervisor to suggest the specific plant hormone for the same. Had you been her supervisor, which plant hormone would you suggest? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(C) Cytokinin
Cytokinin is a growth promoter hormone which is involved in cell division and branching. They are generally found in roots and shoots of plant. They also effect axillary bud growth and apical dominance. Gibberellins: It promotes fruit elongation, germination and flowering. It is present in stem.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is essential for thyroid gland to make thyroxin? [CDS I 2023]
solution:
(D) Iodine
Refer question 6 of Level I.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following hormones inhibits the growth activity in plants? [CDS II 2024]
solution:
(C) Abscisic acid
Among all the plant hormones, abscisic acid inhibits the growth activity. It also helps the plant to manage external stress. It is also known as stress hormone of plants. It regulates seed and bud dormancy and helps the plant to survive in unfavourable conditions.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is a plant hormone? [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(B) Cytokinin
Oestrogen is a female hormone secreted by ovaries. Insulin is secreted by pancreas to control blood sugar levels. Thyroxine is secreted by thyroid gland. They all are present in humans while cytokinin is a plant hormone that promotes cell division in plants and present in roots and shoots.

subject: Biology
question_text: After few days of sowing of maize seeds in the soil, irrespective of their position, the radicle always grows downwards, whereas the coleoptile always grows upwards. This phenomenon is known as: [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(D) Geotropism
Geotropism is movement of plant part in response to gravity. The downward growth of radicle is positive geotropism while the upward growth of coleoptile indicates negative geotropism.

subject: Biology
question_text: Shoots of plant show upward movement and it can be designated to be: [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(D) Negatively geotropic
Refer question 6.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following hormones is responsible for the development of female sexual characters? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(B) Estrogen
Estrogen is secreted by ovaries and it maintains female sexual characters. It is also known as "beauty hormone". Prolactin is secreted by pituitary gland and it enables the production of milk. Progesterone is involved in childbirth and pregnancy. Oxytocin is also secreted by pituitary gland. Also known as Love hormone.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following hormones increases the heartbeat in mammals? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(D) Adrenaline
Adrenaline also called fight or flight hormone is secreted by adrenal glands and controls blood pressure, heartbeat and response to stress. It prepares the body to face stress and crisis. Melatonin is secreted by pineal gland in response to darkness.

subject: Biology
question_text: Xerophytic plants adapt to hot arid regions by [GEOSCIENTIST 2025]

options.a: developing a thick cuticle on their leaf surfaces.
options.b: opening stomata only during the day.
options.d: growing leaves only during the winter.

solution:
(A) Developing a thick cuticle on their leaf surfaces
Xerophytic plants are found in dry regions like deserts. They are adapted to less water conditions such as low rainfall areas. They develop a thick cuticle on the leaf surface to minimise water loss through transpiration. They have Sunken stomata and deep roots. They store water in their tissues. E.g. Acacia, cactus.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following is not a plant hormone? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(D) Thyroxine
'Hormones' are named by EH Starling. Thyroxine is a hormone secreted by thyroid gland that is present in the neck region. It is a butterfly shaped gland and the largest endocrine/ductless gland. Auxins and gibberellins are growth hormone of the plant and stimulate growth of tips and elongation of fruit respectively. Abscisic acid is a growth inhibitor in plants.

subject: Biology
question_text: Who described the algae Spirogyra in 1674 and named the motile organisms Animalcules, meaning 'little animals'? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Antonie van Leeuwenhoek
Father of microbiology. He discovered free living cells in pond water. Also discovered sperm cells and RBCs. He is also known for the improvement of microscope. Maurice Wilkins: Received Nobel Prize in 1962 along with Watson and Crick.

subject: Biology
question_text: Who explained the structure and reproductive system of algae in 1935? [SSC CGL TIER-II 06/03/2023]
solution:
(B) F.E. Fritsch
Father of phycology. He published a book 'Structure and classification of Algae'. Algae are autotrophic as they have chlorophyll pigment. M.O.P. Iyengar: Father of indian Phycology.

subject: Biology
question_text: The reproduction process in which only a single parent is involved is called ________ [SSC MTS 11/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Asexual reproduction
Asexual reproduction is carried out by only one parent and in this type of reproduction there are more chances of a species to get extinct. It does not involve fusion of gametes and the offsprings are clone to their parent cell.

subject: Biology
question_text: The innermost part of a flower is called the ________. [SSC MTS 12/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Pistil
Pistil is female reproductive organ of flower. It consists of stigma, style and ovary. Stigma is the part where pollens land for fertilization. Style is a tube like structure which connect stigma and ovary. Ovary is the part where fertilization takes place. Stamen is the male reproductive organ made up of anther and filament.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which part of a flower is the sticky surface at the top of the pistil, it traps and holds the pollen? [SSC MTS 07/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Stigma
Refer question 4 for stigma explanation. Sepal are the outermost part which protect flower Bud. They are green in colour. Petals are bright coloured structure which attract birds and pollinators for pollination.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the yellow dust present in the middle of a flower called? [SSC CGL 09/03/2020]
solution:
(B) Stamen
Stamen is male reproductive organ of a flower made up of anther and filament. Filament is a long stalk like structure which connect the anther to the base of flower. Anther contains pollens inside them. When a plant contain both female and male part in same plant are called monoecious or bisexual plant. When male and female are present in separate plant then the plant is considered unisexual dioecious.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which type of reproduction occurs in hydra? [SSC MTS 02/02/2019]
solution:
(B) Budding
Budding is a type of a asexual reproduction where a bud arise from body of parent cell and develop into a new organism. It may or may not get detached after development. It allows the organism for rapid reproduction and produce a genetically similar offspring. E.g. hydra and yeast.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is considered unicellular green algae? [SSC MTS 16/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Chlamydomonas
Chlamydomonas is a unicellular green algae which is autotrophic. It can reproduced by both sexual and asexual process. Asexually it reproduce by spore formation and sexually by formation of isogamous gametes.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of microorganism type is correct?
I. Paramecium - Fungi
II. Penicillium - Protozoa
[SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
solution:
(C) Neither I and II
Paramecium is a protozoa which have cilia for locomotion while penicillium is a fungi that is used to make antibiotic. Both I and II are interchanged, hence, both of them are incorrect. *Paramecium reproduce by binary fission and penicillium reproduced by spore formation.

subject: Biology
question_text: How do Pteridophytes reproduce? [SSC CPO 12/12/2019]
solution:
(B) With the help of spores
Pteridophytes are the vascular plants which have hidden reproductive organs and they are also called cryptogams. They do not produce flower or seed, they reproduce by spore formation. The most dominant stage in their life cycle is sporophyte.

subject: Biology
question_text: The development of a fruit without fertilization is called? [SSC CGL 04/03/2020]
solution:
(B) Parthenocarpy
Fruits that are developed from parthenocarpy are seedless. E.g. Banana, pineapple. This process is first described by Fritz Noll in 1902. Hybridogenesis: It is the process by which two different species mates and form an offspring of desired or selective characters. E.g Mule (it is a hybrid of male donkey and female horse).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following are flowerless plants that produce cones and seeds in which seeds are not encased within an ovary? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2019]
solution:
(A) Gymnosperms
Gymnosperms are the flowerless plants that have naked seeds. They are generally evergreen and Woody plants. Xylem vessels and phloem companion cells are absent from gymnosperms. E.g. deodar and pine. Angiosperms are the flowering plant and have covered seeds.

subject: Biology
question_text: A type of asexual reproduction in which new plants are produced from roots, stems, leaves, and buds is known as ________. [SSC PHASE XI 27/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Vegetative propagation
Vegetative propagation is a form of asexual reproduction in plants which require different part of plant to form a new plant. Cutting, layering and grafting are some methods of vegetative propagation. Cutting - banana, sugarcane. Layering - lemon, strawberry and Grafting - Rose. Tissue culture is a scientific and artificial type of vegetative propagation.

subject: Biology
question_text: How do the red algae reproduce vegetatively? [SSC PHASE XI 27/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Fragmentation
Red algae is also known as rhodophycae. Fragmentation is breaking down of parent cell in different fragments and each fragment give rise to a new offspring. These fragments can be made by external factors. E.g. sea anemone. Fission: It is the process of dividing parent cell into daughter cell.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which chromosome is involved in the production of mRNAs for early development? [SSC PHASE XI 27/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Lampbrush Chromosome
Masked mRNAs are stored in cytoplasm and translated into protein at a later stage. Lampbrush chromosome allow them for transcription. These chromosome are not present in mammals. They are first discovered by Walther Flemming in 1882.

subject: Biology
question_text: Some simple multicellular organisms reproduce by breaking into several parts. Then each of these parts grows into a new organism. What is this method of reproduction? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Fragmentation
Refer question 14.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Select the option that shows the correct matching of Column I with Column II regarding Asexual Reproduction. [RRB GROUP D 24/09/2018]
Column A | Column B
a. Leishmania | i. Regeneration
b. Yeast | ii. Binary fission
c. Plasmodium | iii. Multiple fission
d. Hydra | iv. Budding
solution:
(A) A-ii, B-iv, C-iii, D-i
Leishmania divide by binary fission which is a process where parent cell divided into two daughter cells by elongation and division. Yeast reproduce by budding and plasmodium reproduced by multiple fission where parents are divided into multiple daughter cells. Hydra can divide by regeneration or budding but yeast can't divide by regeneration so #option A is appropriate.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify an algae that undergoes anisogamous fusion of gametes. [SSC PHASE XI 28/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Eudorina
Fusion of gametes can be of three types. A. Anisogamous: where the gametes are not similar in size. B. Isogamous: where the gametes are of similar size. E.g. spirogyra, ulothrix C. Oogamous: One gamete is large and non-motile while another is small and motile. E.g. volvox.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is not an asexual form of reproduction in plants? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Pollination
Spore formation, vegetative propagation and budding are asexual form of reproduction while pollination is a sexual reproduction which involve male and female part for fertilization. It can be self pollination or cross pollination. Cross pollination requires pollinators such as insects, wind or birds.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements regarding reproduction in flowering plants is FALSE? [RRB GROUP D 29/08/2022]

options.b: Reproduction may occur from fragments of stems.
options.d: Reproduction may occur from tissues separated from the plant and grown on an artificial medium.

solution:
(C) Reproduction may occur through spores
Flowering plants are called angiosperms. Reproduction through seeds is called apomixis and through fragment of stem is called cutting. Tissues separated from the plant and grown on artificial medium is termed as tissue culture which is a artificial form of vegetative reproduction. Angiosperms does not form spores. Hence statement C is incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: Reproduction in humans occurs through which type of fertilisation? [SSC MTS 14/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Internal Fertilisation
In internal fertilization, fusion of gametes takes place inside the body and the foetus takes nourishment from the mother's body. External fertilization takes place outside the organisms body and require a safe medium for fertilization. E.g. Frogs, starfish. Chances of survival are more in internal fertilization as the offspring is protected from prey.

subject: Biology
question_text: In humans, fertilisation usually takes place in the ________. [SSC CPO 12/03/2019]
solution:
(C) Fallopian tube
Fertilization is the process of fusion of male and female gametes and zygote. It takes place in fallopian tubes which are duct like structure that transfer the ovum from ovary to uterus. When the fertilization is successful the zygote is transferred from fallopian tubes to uterus and it get attached to uterus walls. Formation of egg/ovum takes place in ovary.

subject: Biology
question_text: During fertilisation in living beings, fusion of male and female gametes takes place to form a cell called ________. [SSC CPO 13/12/2019]
solution:
(D) Zygote
Zygote is formed when fusion of male and female gametes takes place. Ovum is the female gamete and sperm is male gamete. An embryo is a mass of cell that is transferred from fallopian tubes to uterus.

subject: Biology
question_text: ________ is the ability of a single cell to produce a fertile, adult individual. [SSC CGL 17/08/2021]
solution:
(B) Totipotency
Totipotency can give rise to a complete organism from a single cell while pluripotency can differentiate into other cell but cannot give rise to an organism. Cloning is the process of producing genetically similar organisms. Mutation is an alteration in the sequence of DNA or RNA.

subject: Biology
question_text: How many arteries are there in an umbilical cord? [SSC CGL 13/08/2021]
solution:
(B) Two
Umbilical cord is a three vessel tube like structure that connects the developing baby to placenta. It provides nourishment and oxygen to foetus and carries waste from foetus. It is made up of two arteries and one vein. Artery carries waste while vein transfer oxygen and nutrients.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is the primary sex organ in females? [SSC CGL 06/03/2020]
solution:
(B) Ovary
Vagina is the opening of female reproductive organ which connects uterus from outside of the body. For ovary, fallopian tube and uterus refer question 22.

subject: Biology
question_text: All human beings have ________ pairs of chromosomes in the nuclei of their cells. [SSC MTS 19/06/2023]
solution:
(D) 23
Walther Flemming discovered chromosome in 1882. Chromosome are made up of DNA and protein (histone). In humans there are 46 chromosomes or 23 pairs. 22 pairs are of autosomal chromosome and one pair is of sex chromosome that determines the sex of baby.

subject: Biology
question_text: The stage of the embryo in which all the body parts can be identified is called ________. [SSC MTS 09/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Foetus
Foetus is the last development stage of a fertilized egg. In the stage the foetus have developed all features and ready for survival. It starts after 10th week of pregnancy and last until birth. Stages of development - Fertilized egg -> Blastocyst -> embryo -> foetus.

subject: Biology
question_text: The phenomenon whereby the female gamete undergoes development to form new organisms without fertilisation in some organisms like honey bees, some lizards, and birds is called: [SSC CPO 25/11/2020]
solution:
(D) Parthenogenesis
It can help in rapid multiplication of organism. Parthenogenesis can decrease the variation in generation and can also decrease chances of survival. Apomixis- development of seed without fertilization is a type of a sexual reproduction. E.g. dandelions.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify an organism that exhibits metagenesis. [SSC CGL 26/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Obelia
Metagenesis is a life cycle in which organism can change between sexual and asexual reproduction. It is also called alternation of generations. Obelia is a member of Cnidaria. It is also known as sea-fur.

subject: Biology
question_text: The period of life, when the body undergoes changes leading to reproductive maturity is called: [SSC MTS 04/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Adolescence
It is a phase where body of an individual undergoes many physical and psychological changes. It starts from the age of 10-11 and ends at 18-19. It is a type of transition between childhood and adulthood. The changes include voice changes, development of reproductive organs and hair growth in certain areas.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT an example of 'viviparous' animals? [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Bird
Viviparous are the organisms in which development of an offspring takes place inside body and they give birth to a fully developed offspring. E.g. whale, dolphin and humans.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the creatures is oviparous? [SSC CGL 11/06/2019]
solution:
(A) Frog
Oviparous are the organisms that lays eggs. Rabbit, mouse and squirrel all are the example of viviparous organisms as they give birth to offsprings after internal development.

subject: Biology
question_text: The transformation of the larva into an adult through drastic change is called ________. [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Metamorphosis
Metamorphosis occurs in many insects, amphibians and fishes. This process is important for survival and reproduction. It involves structural changes such as breakdown and reorganisation of tissues and organs.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following statements refers to the process of 'Parthenogenesis'? [IB ACIO 18/02/2021]
1. It is a natural form of asexual reproduction in which growth and development of embryos occur without fertilization.
2. It is a mode of reproduction in which a male outside's sperm fertilizes a female organism's egg outside of the female's body.
3. It is a mode of reproduction in which a male organism's sperm fertilizes a female organism's egg inside of the female's body.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
solution:
(C) Only I
Mode of reproduction in which a male sperm and female egg fertilised outside the females body is known as external fertilization and if this fertilization occurs inside the female body it is known as internal fertilization. Hence, statement 2 and 3 does not have any relation to parthenogenesis.

subject: Biology
question_text: In biology, the term 'IVF' stands for ________. [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(B) In vitro fertilisation (written as (B) in solutions text but matches Option A)
In vitro fertilization is an artificial mode of reproduction which helps people who faces problems with conception and fertilization. In this egg is separated from female body and sperms from male body and the fertilization is induced in a laboratory. When the fertilization is successful, the embryo is transferred to female uterus.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which part of the human body has the growth stages divided into - Anagen, Catagen, and Telogen? [SSC CHSL 15/04/2021]
solution:
(A) Hair
Anagen is the starting for active phase of formation of hair follicle. Most of the hairs are in this phase. Catagen is the transition phase between anagen and telogen. In this phase cell division in active cell stops. Telogen is the resting phase.

subject: Biology
question_text: A flow chart of sex determination in human beings is shown below. Some conclusions that can be drawn from it are mentioned in the options. Select the INCORRECT option. [RRB GROUP D 26/08/2022]

options.a: All children inherit X chromosome from their mother.
options.b: The chances of a child being male or female is 50%.
options.c: The male decides the sex of the offspring.
options.d: The female decides the sex of the offspring.

solution:
(D) The female decides the sex of the offspring
Female sex chromosomes are XX and male sex chromosome are XY. The child receives an mandatory X chromosome from mother. The chromosome received from father can be an X or Y. If X is received from father then the child will be a female, while if Y is received from father the child will be male. Hence, gender is decided on the basis of chromosome given by father not mother.

subject: Biology
question_text: Buds produced in the notches along the leaf margin of Bryophyllum fall on soil and develop into new plants. This is an example of which one of the following types of reproduction? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(A) Vegetative propagation
Vegetative propagation is a type of asexual reproduction in plants which form new plant through stem, leaves and roots. It can occur naturally or can be induced artificially. Cutting, layering and grafting are the methods of artificial vegetative propagation. It forms genetically similar plants that have no variations among them and can be susceptible to diseases.

subject: Biology
question_text: A child receives a tall beautiful plant as a birthday gift from his father with a quiz. The father asked her how she would verify whether this tall plant was the progeny of both the tall parents or one tall and one short parent plant. She could verify this through: [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(B) Self-pollination
When Pollen gets transferred within the same flower or another flower of same plant then it is called self pollination. It does not need any pollinators. Cross pollination is the transfer of pollen from anther of a flower to stigma of another flower of another plant. It requires pollinators like wind, insects or water. As cross pollination occurs between two plants the chances of genetic variation are more than self pollination.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements about variations is not true? [CDS II 2021]

options.a: Variation is minimum in asexual reproduction.
options.b: All variations in a species have equal chances of survival.
options.c: Changes in genetic constitution result in variation.
options.d: Variants can be selected by environmental factors.

solution:
(B) All variations in a species have equal chances of survival
Mutation in genetic material cause variation. As asexual reproduction involves only one parent so the chances of variation are minimum. Change in the genetic makeup of an individual results in variation. Some variations happenes for making it easy to adapt in external environment. Hence, statement 1, 2 and 3 are correct. But all variations does not have equal chances of survival.

subject: Biology
question_text: Hyphae are: [CDS I 2022]

options.b: thin threads formed by many fungi
options.d: responsible for preventing the cell from losing water in wet conditions

solution:
(B) thin threads formed by many fungi
Hyphae are multicellular vegetative growth of fungi. They are thin filamentous structure that are connected end to end and network of hyphae is called mycelium. They breakdown dead matter into smaller parts. The cell wall of fungi is made up of chitin.

subject: Biology
question_text: Asexual reproduction takes place through budding in: [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(D) Yeast
Yeast is a unicellular fungi that is reproduced through budding. When a bud like outgrowth arise from parent cell and develops into a new organism, the process is called budding. Yeast is used in bakery or brewing activities. E.g. baker's yeast: Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Plasmodium reproduce through multiple fission and amoeba by binary fission.

subject: Biology
question_text: Fruit formation from the thalamus instead of ovary is known as false fruit. The most common examples are: [CISF AC 2024]
solution:
(C) Apple and strawberry
Fruits that are formed from other plant part instead of ovary without fertilization is called false fruit or pseudocarps. They can be form from thalamus (apple and strawberry), peduncle (cashew) or inflorescence (jackfruit).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is a 'false fruit'? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(C) Apple
Refer question 6.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is a 'drupe'? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(C) Coconut
Drupes are fruits that are developed from a single ovary and contain only a single seed. They are generally fleshy. E.g. peach, olive, mango, Amla.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the number of chromosomes found in human somatic cell? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(C) 46
Walther Flemming discovered chromosome in 1882. Chromosome are made up of DNA and protein (histone). In humans there are 46 chromosomes or 23 pairs. 22 pairs are of autosomal chromosome and one pair is of sex chromosome that determines the sex of baby.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following animals lays eggs to reproduce? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(C) Platypus
Animals that lays egg to reproduce are called oviparous. The eggs laid by the parent will hatch after sometime and the offspring come out. Animals that reproduce by giving birth to a live young ones that is developed inside the mother's body is called viviparous.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is the technique of joining parts of two plants to form a composite plant? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(B) Grafting
Grafting, cutting and layering are the artificial means of vegetative propagation. In cutting, a part of plant such as root or stem is cut from plant and placed in a suitable medium to grow. In layering, the part of plant is bent and put into the soil when still attached to the plant. Grafting is used for growing different varieties of same crop.

subject: Biology
question_text: Many fruits burst with a sudden jerk because of which the seeds are scattered a few yards from the parent plant. This type of dispersal of seeds is known as: [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(C) Autochory
When the fruit become mature it bursts open and disperse seeds around it and those seeds form new plant. This process is called Autochory. In anemochory, seeds are dispersed by wind and in hydrochory, seeds are dispersed by water. Similarly, zoochory means dispersal by animals.

subject: Biology
question_text: In a typical flower, germinating pollen grains pass through several parts of the gynoecium before they reach the ovule. A list of the parts of gynoecium is given below in different combinations. Choose the combination that represents the correct sequence of pollen tube pathway/journey: [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(B) Stigma, style, ovary
Stigma is the sticky part where the pollens land and the compatibility is confirmed by pollen-pistil interaction. Once the compatibility is confirmed, Pollen tube start growing through style towards ovary. This Pollen tube is guided by the chemical released by synergids cells present in the female gametophyte. Ovary is the part where ovules are present and fertilization takes place.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is the mechanism of action of oral contraceptive pills? [NDA I 2022]

options.d: They inhibit the release of egg.

solution:
(D) They inhibit the release of egg
Contraception is the process or devices which helps to avoid unwanted pregnancy. They are also called birth control. They are of many types such as barrier e.g. condoms, cervical cap, hormonal, e.g. pills, IUDs - intrauterine devices like copper T, or sterilization - vasectomy (male) and tubal ligation (female).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following plants has unisexual flowers? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(A) Papaya
Unisexual flowers are those which have separate plant for separate sexes. They are also called dioecious. E.g. papaya, cucumber, water-melon, muskmelon. Bisexual or monoecious flowers have both male and female part in one flower. E.g. hibiscus, sunflower, rose, mustard, tomato.

subject: Biology
question_text: After fertilization, the fruit and the seed are produced by [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(B) ovary and ovule, respectively
Fertilization is the fusion of male and female gametes. When it occurs, different part of the flower undergoes different changes. Ovule turn into seed, ovary into fruit, radicle into root and plumule into shoot.

subject: Biology
question_text: One advantage of sexual reproduction over asexual reproduction is that it helps species to survive over long evolutionary time. This is because sexual reproduction produces: [NDA I 2023]

options.a: more offspring in each reproductive cycle.

solution:
(D) more variation in offspring
Sexual reproduction involves two parents which have different genetic makeup. The fusion of gametes will result in more genetic variations and the offspring will have more chances of survival.

subject: Biology
question_text: The two important features of sexual reproduction in higher organisms that create genetic diversity in offspring are [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(B) Meiosis and fertilization
Meiosis occurs in sex cells or gametes. In it, nucleus divides two times and chromosome number reduced to half to allow more genetic variations. The malfunction in meiosis can result in disorders like down syndrome and Turner syndrome. Fertilization is the fusion of male and female gametes inside the female body. Together they produce a more genetic diverse offspring.

subject: Biology
question_text: In angiosperms, pollen grain germinates to produce two male gametes. Which one of the following functions is carried out by these gametes? [NDA II 2023]

options.a: Both the gametes fuse with a single egg cell.
options.b: Both the gametes fuse with two different egg cells.
options.c: One gamete fuses with the egg cell and the other one eventually degenerate.
options.d: One gamete fuses with the egg cell and the other one fuses with a diploid secondary nucleus.

solution:
(D) One gamete fuses with the egg cell and the other one fuses with a diploid secondary nucleus
This process is called double fertilization. One gamete fuse with egg cell (syngamy) to form zygote and the other game to fuses with secondary nucleus to form triploid cell which give rise to endosperm. This endosperm provide nutrition to the developing zygote.

subject: Biology
question_text: In a sexually reproducing organism, which one of the following statements is appropriate both for the parent and offspring? [NDA II 2023]

options.a: Chromosome number increases but DNA content remains constant.
options.b: Both chromosome number and DNA content remains constant.
options.c: Chromosome number decreases but DNA content remains constant.
options.d: Both chromosome number and DNA content decreases.

solution:
(B) Both chromosome number and DNA content remains constant
In humans, the chromosome number is always 46 or 23 pairs. If not, it result in disorders like Down syndrome - extra copy of chromosome 21, Turner syndrome-missing X chromosome in female and Klinefelter syndrome - extra X chromosome in males.

subject: Biology
question_text: Relationship between a flower and honey-bee will help the flower for/in [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(B) Pollination
Honey Bee acts as a pollinator for flower as it helps to carry out cross pollination. In return, it gets nectar from flower. It is called mutualism as both Honey Bee and flower gets benefit.

subject: Biology
question_text: In flowering plants, DNA content of the parent plant gets halved during [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(D) Pollen formation
Pollen formation occurs through meiosis which is a method of cell division in sex cells. The DNA content gets half and form haploid pollen grains so that it can allow genetic variation in next generation. Pollen formation takes place in the anther of the flower.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is the correct sequence during sexual reproduction in Angiosperms? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(A) Egg -> zygote -> embryo -> seed
Egg is the female gamete produced by ovary. When fertilization takes place, the male and female gamete together forms zygote. This zygote develop further into embryo and get its nutrition from endosperm. Seed is the last development stage.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following combinations of events represents the correct sequence during reproduction flowering plants? [NDA II 2023]

options.a: Ovule -> fruit; egg -> embryo; zygote -> seed; ovary -> egg
options.b: Embryo -> egg; ovary -> fruit; ovule -> zygote; zygote -> seed
options.c: Ovary -> fruit; zygote -> egg; embryo -> ovule; seed -> fruit
options.d: Egg -> zygote; zygote -> embryo; ovule -> seed; ovary -> fruit

solution:
(D) Egg -> zygote; zygote -> embryo; ovule -> seed; ovary -> fruit
Refer question 16.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which one among the following the vegetative propagation takes place through 'Eye Buds'? [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(A) Potato
Vegetative propagation is the asexual method of reproduction where a new plant arise from the vegetative part of a plant. In potato, it occurs through eye bud for eye of tuber. In ginger, it occurs through rhizome. In sugarcane, by cutting of cane or stalk. In onion, by bulbs.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is able to regenerate our digestive system with good microbes that will neutralize the harmful ones? [SSC PHASE X 05/08/2022]
solution:
(C) Probiotics
Probiotics are live microorganisms that provide health benefits when consumed, especially by improving or restoring the gut flora (good microbes).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which part of the digestive system receives secretions from the liver and the pancreas? [SSC MTS 02/11/2021]
solution:
(D) Small intestines
Small intestine receives bile juice from the liver (which is stored in gallbladder) and pancreatic juice from the pancreas. The small intestine is the site of complete digestion of carbohydrates, proteins, and fats.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the function of lipase in the process of digestion. [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Breaking down emulsified fats
Lipase is an enzyme that catalyzes the breakdown of fats into fatty acids and glycerol. It digests emulsified fats.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is the indigestible portion of our diet essential to the health of the digestive system? [SSC MTS 08/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Roughage
Roughage, also known as dietary fiber, is the indigestible portion of plant food. It helps in the proper functioning of the digestive system by adding bulk to the food and assisting in bowel movement.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which juice is released in our gall bladder that facilitates the digestion of fats? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Bile
Bile juice is secreted by the liver and stored in the gallbladder. It contains bile salts which help in the emulsification of fats (breaking down large fat globules into smaller ones).

subject: Biology
question_text: Food digested in the stomach passes through the intestines so that the blood vessels can absorb essential nutrients for the functioning and growth of the body. What is this process known as? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Assimilation
Assimilation is the process by which digested food nutrients are absorbed by blood vessels and transported to different cells of the body where they are utilized for energy, growth, and repair.

subject: Biology
question_text: The pouch connected to the junction of the small intestine and the large intestine is called: [SSC CPO 09/12/2019]
solution:
(B) Caecum
Caecum is a pouch-like structure that forms the beginning of the large intestine. It connects the small intestine (ileum) to the colon.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct regarding the liver? [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]

options.a: It is situated in the upper part of the abdomen on the left side.
options.b: It secretes bile juice that is stored in a sac called the gallbladder.
options.c: It is the largest gland in the body.

solution:
(A) It is situated in the upper part of the abdomen on the left side
The liver is situated in the upper right side of the abdominal cavity. Hence, statement A is incorrect. It is indeed the largest gland, reddish-brown in color, and secretes bile stored in the gallbladder.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which food requires a longer small intestine to digest food? [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Grass
Herbivores eating grass need a longer small intestine to allow the cellulose to be digested completely. Meat is easier to digest, hence carnivores have shorter small intestines.

subject: Biology
question_text: In humans, the digestion of carbohydrates like starch begins in which of the following parts? [SSC CHSL 14/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Buccal cavity
Digestion of carbohydrates (starch) begins in the mouth (buccal cavity) by salivary amylase (ptyalin) which converts starch into maltose.

subject: Biology
question_text: In ruminants like cattle, bacteria present in ________ helps in digestion of cellulose. [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Rumen
Ruminants have a specialized stomach with a chamber called Rumen containing symbiotic bacteria and protozoa that secrete cellulase enzyme to ferment and digest cellulose.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a medium-sized globular protein that acts as a pancreatic serine protease found in the digestive system of many vertebrates? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Trypsin
Trypsin is a serine protease from the exocrine pancreas that hydrolyzes proteins in the small intestine.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following muscles regulates the exit of food from the stomach into the small intestine? [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Sphincter
Pyloric sphincter is a circular muscle that controls the release of chyme from the stomach into the duodenum (small intestine).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a function of calcium in our body? [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]

options.a: It plays an important role in blood clotting.
options.c: It is associated with healthy bones and teeth.

solution:
(B) It helps the digestive system
Calcium is crucial for bone/teeth structure, blood clotting, and muscle contraction, but it does not directly play an active role in digestive processes. Hence, statement B is incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: In human beings, the small intestine is coiled and is about ________ metres long. [SSC MTS 12/07/2022]
solution:
(D) 7 to 7.5
The small intestine is highly coiled and measures about 7 to 7.5 meters (approx 20-25 feet) in length.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which type of carbohydrate CANNOT be broken down by the GI tract, does not provide energy, but helps the body get rid of wastes and keeps the intestinal tract healthy? [SSC CHSL 27/05/2022]
solution:
(A) Fibre
Fiber/Roughage cannot be digested by human enzymes. It adds bulk to feces and assists excretion, maintaining bowel health.

subject: Biology
question_text: A large, muscular sheet called ________ forms the floor of the chest cavity. [SSC MTS 02/10/2024]
solution:
(A) Diaphragm
The diaphragm is a dome-shaped muscular partition separating the thorax from the abdomen, playing a major role in respiration.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following organs secretes hydrochloric acid that helps our body in killing pathogenic bacteria? [SSC CGL 16/02/2021]
solution:
(D) Stomach
Stomach secretes hydrochloric acid (HCl) as part of gastric juice. HCl provides an acidic medium for pepsin activation and kills bacteria in ingested food.

subject: Biology
question_text: What helps make the acidic food alkaline that is coming from the stomach? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Bile Juice
Bile juice is alkaline and contains sodium bicarbonate which neutralizes the acidity of chyme from the stomach, making it alkaline for pancreatic enzyme activity.

subject: Biology
question_text: The inner walls of the small intestine have thousands of finger-like outgrowths. These are called: [SSC CHSL 14/10/2020]
solution:
(A) Villi
Villi are finger-like projections on the mucosal surface of the small intestine that increase surface area for nutrient absorption.

subject: Biology
question_text: The enzyme that is essential for fat digestion is: [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Lipase
Lipase is the key enzyme responsible for breaking down fats (triglycerides) into monoglycerides and free fatty acids.

subject: Biology
question_text: Through which of the following body parts does food need to pass before reaching the intestines? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Oesophagus -> Stomach
The correct pathway of food in the alimentary canal before reaching intestines is: Mouth -> Pharynx -> Oesophagus (food pipe) -> Stomach.

subject: Biology
question_text: Steapsin, the enzyme which converts fats into fatty acids and glycerol, is secreted by ________ [SSC MTS 10/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Pancreas
Steapsin, also known as pancreatic lipase, is secreted by the pancreas into the duodenum to digest fats.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify a protein-digesting enzyme. [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Pepsin
Pepsin is secreted in an inactive form (pepsinogen) by chief cells of gastric glands in the stomach and digests proteins into peptones.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following statements about enzymes in the human body is/are correct? [SSC CHSL TIER-II 26/06/2023]
Statement A: Maltase is present in the saliva and breaks the sugar maltose into glucose.
Statement B: Amylase is present in the saliva and helps change starches into sugars.
solution:
(D) Only B
Salivary amylase (ptyalin) is present in saliva and converts starch to sugars. Maltase is secreted by the small intestine (intestinal juice), not in saliva. Hence, only Statement B is correct.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following options correctly matches column-A and column-B? [SSC MTS 01/10/2024]
Column-A (Food Component) | Column-B (Enzyme involved in digestion)
i. Protein | a. Amylase
ii. Carbohydrates | b. Lipase
iii. Fats | c. Trypsin
solution:
(D) i-c, ii-a, iii-b
Proteins are digested by trypsin. Carbohydrates are digested by amylase. Fats are digested by lipase.

subject: Biology
question_text: Select the correct arrangement of the parts of the food canal in humans from the starting point to the ending point. [SSC JE 07/06/2024]

options.a: The buccal cavity -> Oesophagus -> Stomach -> Large intestine -> Small intestine -> Rectum -> Anus
options.b: The buccal cavity -> Oesophagus -> Stomach -> Small intestine -> Large intestine -> Anus -> Rectum
options.c: The buccal cavity -> Oesophagus -> Stomach -> Large intestine -> Small intestine -> Anus -> Rectum
options.d: The buccal cavity -> Oesophagus -> Stomach -> Small intestine -> Large intestine -> Rectum -> Anus

solution:
(D) The buccal cavity -> Oesophagus -> Stomach -> Small intestine -> Large intestine -> Rectum -> Anus
This defines the correct anatomical path of the human alimentary canal.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match the terms in column A with their respective properties in column B. [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
Column A | Column B
i. Glucose | a. Intermediate substance breakdown of glucose
ii. Yeast | b. Glucose is converted into pyruvic acid
iii. Glycolysis | c. Uses nutrients for fermentation process
iv. Pyruvic | d. Best organic substrate for acid respiration
solution:
(A) i-d, ii-c, iii-b, iv-a
Glucose is the organic substrate for respiration. Yeast utilizes nutrients for fermentation. Glycolysis is the pathway converting glucose to pyruvate. Pyruvate is the intermediate breakdown product.

subject: Biology
question_text: In boys, the growing 'larynx' is visible as a distinct bulge at the front of the throat called ________ [SSC MTS 19/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Kanthamani (Adam's apple)
The Adam's apple (Kanthamani) is the protrusion of the thyroid cartilage in the larynx seen primarily in adolescent and adult males.

subject: Biology
question_text: Breakdown of pyruvate into lactic acid and energy in lack of Oxygen takes place in which of the following? [SSC CHSL 13/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Human muscle cells
During vigorous exercise when oxygen is scarce, pyruvate undergoes anaerobic respiration in muscle cells, converting to lactic acid and causing muscle fatigue.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following is correct regarding respiration?
I. Regular traditional breathing exercise (pranayama) can increase the capacity of lungs to take in more air.
II. During inhalation, ribs move up and outwards and diaphragm moves down.
[SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Both I and II
Both statements accurately describe the physiological mechanics of respiration and lung capacity expansion via pranayama.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the percentage of carbon dioxide in the air exhaled by human lungs? [SSC CHSL 21/03/2023]
solution:
(D) 4.4%
Exhaled air consists of about 4.4% carbon dioxide, which is a byproduct of cellular aerobic respiration.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the network of air tubes for gas exchange in insects? [SSC MTS 27/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Tracheae
Insects breathe through a tracheal system consisting of network of air tubes (tracheae) which open out via spiracles.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is present between the lungs? [SSC CGL 03/03/2020]
solution:
(A) Thymus
The thymus gland is located in the chest cavity between the lungs, behind the sternum.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the reason for the formation of dental plaque. [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]

options.a: Masses of bacterial cells together with food particles stick to the teeth.
options.c: When bacteria acting on sugars produce acids.
options.d: When acids stick to the teeth.

solution:
(A) Masses of bacterial cells together with food particles stick to the teeth
Dental plaque is a biofilm of microorganisms (primarily bacteria) that forms and grows on surfaces within the mouth, sticking to teeth surfaces alongside food debris.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following demineralises the enamel of the teeth? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Acids produced by bacteria
Bacteria in dental plaque ferment sugars to produce organic acids which lower pH and demineralize the enamel of the teeth.

subject: Biology
question_text: What part of the respiratory system prevents the air passage from collapsing? [RRB GROUP D 17/08/2022]
solution:
(B) Rings of cartilage
The trachea and bronchi are lined with C-shaped rings of cartilage that prevent them from collapsing during inhalation when air pressure decreases.

subject: Biology
question_text: During the process of respiration, how many oxygen molecules combine with one molecule of glucose? [RRB GROUP D 08/09/2022]
solution:
(C) Six
The balanced equation for aerobic respiration is: C6H12O6 + 6O2 -> 6CO2 + 6H2O + Energy (ATP). Thus, 6 oxygen molecules combine with 1 glucose molecule.

subject: Biology
question_text: Some fishes breathe by gulping water. These fish gulp water ________ [RRB GROUP D 06/10/2022]

options.a: More times per minute than an animal breathing air
options.b: More rapidly when they are near the surface of the water
options.c: Less times per minute than an animal breathing air
options.d: Less rapidly when they live in a turbulent river

solution:
(A) More times per minute than an animal breathing air
Since the concentration of dissolved oxygen in water is much lower than in air, aquatic organisms have to breathe much faster to meet their oxygen requirements.

subject: Biology
question_text: Frogs have a pair of lungs like human beings but they can also breathe through their: [RRB GROUP D 06/10/2022]
solution:
(C) Slippery and moist skin
Frogs show cutaneous respiration, breathing through their thin, highly vascularized, moist skin when in water or damp environments.

subject: Biology
question_text: The labelling for the parts a, b, and c of the human respiratory system shown below are given as options. Identify the correct option. [RRB GROUP D 09/09/2022]

options.a: a - Trachea, b - Larynx, c - Pharynx
options.b: a - Larynx, b - Pharynx, c - Trachea
options.c: a - Pharynx, b - Larynx, c - Trachea
options.d: a - Pharynx, b - Trachea, c - Larynx

solution:
(C) a - Pharynx, b - Larynx, c - Trachea
The pharynx is the common chamber, the larynx is the voice box below it, and the trachea is the windpipe leading to bronchi.

subject: Biology
question_text: All the statements given below regarding respiration in humans are correct except one. Identify the incorrect statement. [RRB GROUP D 26/08/2022]

options.a: The ribs sink in, and the diaphragm rises upwards during inhalation.
options.b: The ribs rise up, and the diaphragm flattens during inhalation.
options.d: Air is taken in by the lungs.

solution:
(A) The ribs sink in, and the diaphragm rises upwards
During inhalation, the diaphragm contracts and moves down while ribs move up and out, expanding the thoracic cavity. Statement A describes exhalation/incorrect mechanics.

subject: Biology
question_text: Where are villi present in the human alimentary canal? [SSC MTS 07/07/2022]
solution:
(D) Small intestine
Refer question 20. Small intestine lining contains villi for maximum nutrient absorption.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is not a breakdown product of glucose during respiration? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(D) Methanol
Respiration breaks down glucose into ethanol/CO2 (yeast fermentation), lactic acid (muscle cells), or CO2/H2O (aerobic respiration). Methanol is toxic and not a natural respiration product.

subject: Biology
question_text: In humans, urea is mainly formed from the metabolism of which one of the following components of food? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(C) Amino acids
Deamination of excess amino acids in the liver produces ammonia, which is then converted into urea via the ornithine (urea) cycle and excreted by the kidneys.

subject: Biology
question_text: Urea is produced by metabolism of [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(A) Proteins
Proteins are made of amino acids. Metabolism of these proteins/amino acids leads to nitrogenous waste (urea).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following pairs of enzymes do not work at acidic pH? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(C) Trypsin and Amylase
Pepsin works at highly acidic pH (1.5-2) in the stomach. Trypsin and pancreatic amylase function in alkaline pH (7.8-8.5) of the small intestine. Hence, trypsin and amylase do not function in acidic pH.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following parts of human alimentary canal can be of maximum length? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(B) Small intestine
The small intestine (~7 meters) is significantly longer than the large intestine (~1.5 meters), stomach, and rectum.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements regarding bile secreted by liver is not correct? [CDS I 2023]

options.a: Bile contains enzymes for digestion of lipids.
options.c: Bile neutralizes the acidic pH of the food coming from stomach.
options.d: Bile makes the pH of the food alkaline and facilitates action of pancreatic enzymes.

solution:
(A) Bile contains enzymes for digestion of lipids
Bile does not contain any digestive enzymes. It contains bile salts that emulsify lipids and alkaline components to neutralize stomach acids.

subject: Biology
question_text: An antibacterial enzyme present in saliva and tears is called: [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) Lysozyme
Lysozyme acts as an antibacterial agent by cleaving the peptidoglycan cell walls of bacteria, providing protection in tears and saliva.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following is not a feature of Xerophytes? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(B) Large number of stomata
Xerophytes minimize water loss by having smaller/fewer leaves, thick waxy cuticles, sunken stomata, and reduced number of stomata. Large number of stomata is not a xerophytic adaptation.

subject: Biology
question_text: Animal fats generally contain: [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]
solution:
(B) saturated fatty acids
Animal fats are generally rich in saturated fatty acids, making them solid at room temperature.

subject: Biology
question_text: Molecules made up of carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen and sometimes sulphur that synthesise protein are known as: [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]
solution:
(A) Amino acids
Amino acids are the building blocks of proteins, consisting of amine and carboxyl groups alongside side chains containing C, H, O, N, and S.

subject: Biology
question_text: The protein-digesting enzyme secreted by the stomach wall in case of mammals is called [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(C) pepsin
Pepsin is the primary gastric enzyme responsible for initiating protein digestion in the stomach.

subject: Biology
question_text: The digestion of fat in human intestine is performed by [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(B) bile and lipase
Fat digestion requires emulsification by bile salts (secreted by liver) followed by enzymatic hydrolysis by pancreatic lipase.

subject: Biology
question_text: The breakdown of glucose in cytoplasm results in the formation of [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(A) pyruvate and energy
Glycolysis occurs in the cytoplasm, breaking down 1 molecule of glucose (6C) into 2 molecules of pyruvate (3C) with a net yield of 2 ATP (energy).

subject: Biology
question_text: When yeast cells are O2 starved, fermentation serves as the source of energy. This results in the production of: [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(A) ATP + CO2 + Ethanol
Anaerobic fermentation in yeast converts pyruvate into ethanol and carbon dioxide, regenerating NAD+ and producing 2 ATP.

subject: Biology
question_text: In the human digestive system, which one among the following is the role of the pancreas? [NDA I 2024]

options.a: Secretion of surfactants to break up lipid droplets.
options.b: Storage and regulated release of bile.
options.c: Secretion of lipase, amylase and protease.

solution:
(C) Secretion of lipase, amylase and protease
The pancreas secretes pancreatic juice containing enzymes: pancreatic amylase, lipase, trypsinogen, chymotrypsinogen, etc. to digest all food types.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which part or organ of human body cartilage is NOT found? [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(C) Bronchiole
Cartilage is found in the nose, trachea, bronchi, and joints. Bronchioles lack cartilage and are kept open by smooth muscles and surrounding lung tissue elastic recoil.

subject: Biology
question_text: Lactic acid is formed in the muscles during exercise or running. It is formed: [CAPF 2023]

options.a: to give extra energy from fat.
options.b: to give extra energy from lungs.
options.c: to give extra energy from glucose.
options.d: to give extra energy from vitamins.

solution:
(C) to give extra energy from glucose
When muscles require energy rapidly during intense exercise and oxygen supply is insufficient, glucose undergoes anaerobic glycolysis to form lactic acid to rapidly supply ATP.

subject: Biology
question_text: Lactose is made up of: [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(D) Glucose and Galactose
Lactose is a disaccharide sugar composed of galactose and glucose subunits joined by a beta-1,4-glycosidic linkage. It is found in milk.

subject: Biology
question_text: How many Carbon atoms are present in a Pyruvate molecule? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(B) 3
Pyruvate (pyruvic acid) is a 3-carbon organic acid (CH3COCOOH) formed as the end product of glycolysis.

subject: Biology
question_text: ________ are the vessels which carry blood away from the heart to various organs of the body. [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Arteries
Arteries are blood vessels that transport oxygen-rich blood from the heart to the body's tissues and organs. They have thick, elastic walls to handle the high pressure of blood pumped by the heart.

subject: Biology
question_text: ________ are the vessels which carry carbon dioxide-rich blood back to the heart. [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Veins
Veins are blood vessels that carry deoxygenated, carbon dioxide-rich blood from various parts of the body back to the heart. This blood is then sent to the lungs for oxygenation. They have thinner walls compared to arteries and often contain valves to prevent backflow of blood.

subject: Biology
question_text: The blood platelets are also known as ________. [SSC MTS 16/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Thrombocytes
Thrombocytes, commonly known as platelets, are cell fragments in the blood that play a crucial role in hemostasis (clotting) - the process that stops bleeding by forming clots. They aggregate at sites of blood vessel injury to form clots and prevent excessive bleeding. Thrombin is an enzyme that converts fibrinogen into fibrin during the clotting process.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the pigment that gives blood its colour. [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Haemoglobin
Haemoglobin is a protein in red blood cells responsible for transporting oxygen from the lungs to tissues throughout the body. The vital component of haemoglobin is iron, which binds to oxygen, giving blood its characteristic red colour. Ferritin is a protein that stores iron in the body but does not play a role in blood coloration.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the pH of human blood? [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(D) 7.4
The normal pH range of human blood is between 7.35 and 7.45, making it slightly alkaline. Maintaining this pH range is crucial for proper bodily functions.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is essential to form haemoglobin in blood? [SSC CGL TIER-II 03/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Iron
Refer solution to question 4. Iron is key to binding oxygen in the haemoglobin structure.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a type of blood cell made by the bone marrow? [SSC MTS 05/10/2021]
solution:
(A) Osteoclasts
Osteoclasts are specialized bone cells responsible for bone resorption (breaking down bone tissue). They are not blood cells in the traditional sense, as they do not circulate in the bloodstream. Red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets are all produced in the bone marrow and play essential roles in oxygen transport, immune response, and blood clotting, respectively.

subject: Biology
question_text: Lymph is a light clear fluid made up of white blood cells that attack harmful ________ in the blood. [SSC CGL 13/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Bacteria
Lymph contains white blood cells, particularly lymphocytes, which are part of the immune system. These cells are responsible for identifying and neutralizing harmful bacteria. Fungi and protozoa are less commonly targeted by the immune system through lymphatic mechanisms. Viruses are attacked by specific immune responses.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a large bean-shaped lymphoid organ in the human body? [SSC CHSL 16/04/2021]
solution:
(B) Spleen
The spleen is a bean-shaped organ that filters blood, removes old red blood cells, and helps fight infection by producing lymphocytes and storing white blood cells. Lymph nodes are bean-shaped but are smaller structures. Tonsils are lymphoid tissues, but they are not large or bean-shaped.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following statements about blood pressure is correct? [SSC CHSL 06/08/2021]

options.a: The normal systolic pressure is about 80 mm of Hg and diastolic pressure is 120 mm of Hg.
options.b: The normal systolic pressure is about 72 mm of Hg and diastolic pressure is 35 mm of Hg.
options.c: The normal systolic pressure is about 160 mm of Hg and diastolic pressure is 80 mm of Hg.
options.d: The normal systolic pressure is about 120 mm of Hg and diastolic pressure is 80 mm of Hg.

solution:
(D) The normal systolic pressure is about 120 mm of Hg and diastolic pressure is 80 mm of Hg
The normal blood pressure range is 120/80 mmHg, indicating the pressure during ventricular heart contractions (systolic) and during ventricular relaxation (diastolic).

subject: Biology
question_text: Swollen bluish veins resulting from the valves that do not close properly are called ________. [SSC CHSL 12/08/2021]
solution:
(B) Varicose veins
Varicose veins occur when vein valves fail, causing blood to pool and veins to become swollen, twisted, and bluish.

subject: Biology
question_text: In the case of reptiles, their heart is not properly divided into four chambers i.e., it consists of two auricles and an incompletely divided ventricle. Which of the following is an example of a reptile having a four-chambered heart? [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Crocodile
Crocodiles are an exception among reptiles, as they have a four-chambered heart, which is more efficient for separating oxygenated and deoxygenated blood. Snakes, Turtles, and Lizards have a three-chambered heart.

subject: Biology
question_text: A healthy individual has 12-16 grams of hemoglobin in each ________ ml of blood. [SSC CPO 25/11/2020]
solution:
(A) 100
Haemoglobin concentration is measured in grams per 100 ml (also called decilitre) of blood. For a healthy adult, it ranges from 12-16 g/dL for females and 13.5 - 17.5 g/dL for males.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following blood groups is called the "Universal Receiver"? [SSC CHSL 14/10/2020]
solution:
(A) AB+
Individuals with AB+ blood type have all antigens (A, B, and Rh) so they can safely receive blood from any blood group. O+ can donate to all Rh+ blood types but is not a universal receiver. O- can donate to all blood types and is a universal donor.

subject: Biology
question_text: The sino-atrial node is located in the ________. [SSC CHSL 20/10/2020]
solution:
(B) Heart
The sino-atrial (SA) node is the natural pacemaker of the heart, located in the upper wall of the right atrium. It generates electrical impulses that regulate the heartbeat.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which part of the body is responsible for the manufacturing of red blood cells? [SSC CGL 04/03/2020]
solution:
(D) Bone Marrow
Refer solution to question 7. Bone marrow is where hematopoiesis occurs, producing RBCs, WBCs, and platelets.

subject: Biology
question_text: How do humans acclimatize at high altitudes where atmospheric pressure is low for normal breathing? [SSC CHSL 03/06/2022]

options.a: By decreasing red blood cell production, decreasing the binding affinity of hemoglobin and by increasing breathing rate.
options.b: By increasing red blood cell production, decreasing the binding affinity of hemoglobin and by increasing breathing rate.
options.d: By increasing white blood cell production, decreasing the binding affinity of hemoglobin and by increasing breathing rate.

solution:
(B) By increasing red blood cell production, decreasing the binding affinity of hemoglobin, and by increasing breathing rate
At high altitudes, the body compensates for low oxygen by: Producing more RBCs to carry oxygen. Decreasing haemoglobin's oxygen-binding affinity to enhance oxygen release to tissues. Increasing breathing rate to take in more oxygen. Humans cannot hibernate.

subject: Biology
question_text: Blood clot is formed because of the presence of certain cells in blood called ________. [SSC CPO 13/12/2019]
solution:
(D) Platelets
Refer solution to question 3. Platelets/thrombocytes aggregate to form blood clots.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which component of blood fights against the germs entering into the human body? [SSC MTS 09/08/2019]
solution:
(C) WBC
White blood cells (WBCs) are part of the immune system and help fight infections by attacking bacteria, viruses, and other harmful pathogens.

subject: Biology
question_text: The process of blood clotting is called ________. [SSC CPO 15/03/2019]
solution:
(A) Coagulation
Coagulation is the process of blood clotting, which prevents excessive blood loss during injury. Thrombocytopenia is a condition characterized by low platelet levels. Haemophilia is a genetic disorder where blood doesn't clot properly.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following instruments is used to measure blood pressure? [RRB GROUP D 01/09/2022]
solution:
(C) Sphygmomanometer
A sphygmomanometer is specifically designed to measure blood pressure. Haemoglobinmeter measures haemoglobin levels. Urinometer measures the specific gravity of urine.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the primary function of the excretory system in animals? [SSC MTS 18/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Removal of waste products
The excretory system removes metabolic waste products such as urea, carbon dioxide and excess salts from the body.

subject: Biology
question_text: Filtration of waste products in humans happens in the kidneys. There is a large number of filtration units present inside the kidneys to help them in doing this job. These filtration units are called: [SSC CGL 18/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Nephrons
Nephrons are the functional filtration units of the kidneys that remove waste from blood and produce urine. Alveoli are found in the lungs and are responsible for gas exchange.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is a specialized excretory cell found in Platyhelminthes that acts like a kidney, removing waste material through filtration? [SSC CGL TIER-I 06/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Flame cell
Flame cells are specialized excretory cells in Platyhelminthes (flatworms) that filter waste and remove it from the body.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following chemicals is responsible for the yellow colour in urine? [SSC CHSL 13/04/2021]
solution:
(B) Urobilin
Urobilin, a byproduct of the breakdown of haemoglobin, is responsible for the yellow colour of urine. Bilirubin gives bile its yellow colour.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of these is an excretory organ of the human body? [SSC CHSL 19/10/2020]
solution:
(B) Skin
The skin excretes waste products like sweat, which contains water, salts, and some urea.

subject: Biology
question_text: Identify the organ which is located on both sides in the human body. [SSC CPO 09/12/2019]
solution:
(C) Kidney
The kidneys are paired organs located on both sides of the spine in the lower back. Spleen: There is only one spleen, located on the left side of the body. The bladder is a single organ located in the pelvic region.

subject: Biology
question_text: Sweating or perspiration is nature's way of ________. [SSC CPO 09/12/2019]

options.a: Releasing excess salt from the body.
options.b: Wardng off heredity illness through the skin.
options.c: Releasing excess water from the body.
options.d: Lowering the internal temperature of the body.

solution:
(D) Lowering the internal temperature of the body.
Sweat evaporation from the skin surface helps regulate body temperature by cooling the body.

subject: Biology
question_text: With regard to kidneys and its functions, which of the following statements is NOT correct? [SSC CGL 11/12/2019]

options.a: They maintain water balance and excrete metabolic waste.
options.b: Reptiles have relatively more nephrons than birds.
options.c: Each nephron has two parts - the glomerulus and the renal tubule.
options.d: Their basic functional units are called nephrons.

solution:
(B) Reptiles have relatively more nephrons than birds
Birds generally have more efficient kidneys with a higher number of nephrons to conserve water due to their adaptation to flight and dry environments. A, C, and D are correct.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which organ is responsible for removing the nitrogenous waste from the human body? [SSC MTS 19/08/2019]
solution:
(B) Kidneys
The kidneys filter nitrogenous waste products like urea, uric acid, and ammonia from the blood and excrete them in urine.

subject: Biology
question_text: Dialysis is advised to the patient in case of which human organ is damaged or not functioning properly? [SSC MTS 20/08/2019]
solution:
(A) Kidneys
Dialysis is a medical procedure that replaces kidney function by filtering waste products and excess fluids from the blood.

subject: Biology
question_text: Before urination, in which part of the excretory system in the human body does the urine get stored? [SSC MTS 21/08/2019]
solution:
(D) Urinary Bladder
Urine is stored in the urinary bladder before it is excreted through the urethra. The ureter transports urine from the kidneys to the bladder.

subject: Biology
question_text: In the human body, blood flows through a process of double circulation. Which one of the following statements is true in this regard? [CDS II 2021]

options.a: Oxygenated blood reaches the left side of the heart from the lungs.
options.b: Blood in the left side of the heart is poor in oxygen and is brought to the right side of the heart.
options.c: Deoxygenated blood from the left side of the heart is brought to the lungs for oxygenation.
options.d: Oxygenated blood from the right side of the heart is sent around the body.

solution:
(A) Oxygenated blood reaches the left side of the heart from the lungs
In double circulation, oxygenated blood from the lungs enters the left atrium of the heart via the pulmonary vein. Blood in the left side of the heart is oxygen-rich. Deoxygenated blood from the right side of the heart is sent to the lungs for oxygenation. Oxygenated blood is pumped from the left ventricle to the body.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists: [CDS I 2022]
List I (Blood Vessel) | List II (Function)
A. Pulmonary artery | 1. Carries blood from lungs to heart
B. Capillaries | 2. Carries blood to heart muscle
C. Coronary artery | 3. Connection between arteries and veins
D. Pulmonary vein | 4. Carries blood from heart to lungs
solution:
(A) A4 B3 C2 D1
Pulmonary artery - 4 (Carries blood from heart to lungs). Capillaries - 3 (Connection between arteries and veins). Coronary artery - 2 (Carries blood to heart muscle). Pulmonary vein - 1 (Carries blood from lungs to heart).

subject: Biology
question_text: The blood pressure of a normal human being is found to be 120/80 mmHg. These numbers represent the blood pressure at [CDS I 2023]

options.a: ventricular contraction and ventricular relaxation, respectively.
options.b: ventricular relaxation and ventricular contraction, respectively.
options.c: auricular contraction and auricular relaxation, respectively.
options.d: ventricular contraction and auricular contraction, respectively.

solution:
(A) Ventricular contraction and ventricular relaxation, respectively
Refer to solution to question 10 level 1. 120 mmHg represents systolic pressure (ventricular contraction) and 80 mmHg represents diastolic pressure (ventricular relaxation).

subject: Biology
question_text: Pulse is felt due to the rhythmic contraction and relaxation of the [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) Aorta and main arteries
The pulse is created by the expansion and recoil of arteries as blood is pumped out of the heart during each heartbeat.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is synthesised in human body that dilates blood vessels and increases blood flow? [IAS 2024]
solution:
(A) Nitric oxide
Nitric oxide (NO) is a gas molecule synthesized by the vascular endothelium that acts as a potent vasodilator to relax blood vessels and increase blood flow.

subject: Biology
question_text: pH of blood is: [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]
solution:
(C) 7.4
Refer solution to question 5 level 1.

subject: Biology
question_text: Arteries are the blood vessels which: [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]

options.d: carry the blood away from the heart.

solution:
(D) carry the blood away from the heart
Arteries are defined as vessels that carry blood away from the heart. Although most carry oxygenated blood, the pulmonary artery carries deoxygenated blood. So statement D is the most accurate definition.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which part of nephron of the human kidney is responsible for filtration of blood for excretion? [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) Bowman's capsule
Ultrafiltration occurs in the glomerulus which is enclosed by Bowman's capsule in the nephron.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which instrument is used to measure the blood pressure manually? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(A) Sphygmomanometer
A manual sphygmomanometer (BP cuff and mercury manometer) is used alongside a stethoscope to measure blood pressure.

subject: Biology
question_text: The normal systolic pressure in human beings is: [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(C) 120 mm of Hg
Systolic blood pressure represents the maximum pressure exerted when the heart contracts, normally around 120 mmHg.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following gases is released in the process of photo-synthesis? [SSC CHSL 12/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Oxygen
Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen in the presence of light. Oxygen is released into the atmosphere.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following grains contain oryzenin? [SSC CHSL 17/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Rice
Oryzenin is a major protein found in rice (forming about 80% of its protein content). It is a glutelin protein.

subject: Biology
question_text: What happens to the guard cells when water flows into them? [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Stomatal pores open
When water flows into the guard cells, they swell, become turgid, and bow outward, which causes the stomatal pore to open.

subject: Biology
question_text: Plants that are adapted to live under plenty of sunlight are called: [SSC CGL 28/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Heliophytes
Heliophytes are sun-loving plants adapted to high-intensity light environments.

subject: Biology
question_text: The leaves have a green pigment called ________, which helps leaves to capture the energy from the sunlight. [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Chlorophyll
Chlorophyll is the green pigment in chloroplasts that absorbs light energy required for photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is the ultimate source of energy for all living organisms? [SSC MTS 11/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Sun
The sun is the primary source of energy for life on Earth, driven by photoautotrophic energy capture in plants.

subject: Biology
question_text: The mode of nutrition in which organisms make food themselves from simple substances is called ________. [SSC MTS 11/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Autotrophic nutrition
