Autotrophic nutrition is where organisms synthesize organic food molecules from inorganic raw materials using light or chemical energy.

subject: Biology
question_text: Plants require nutrients for their growth which can be supplied through ________. [SSC MTS 15/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Soil, water and air
Plants obtain carbon/oxygen from air (via stomata), hydrogen/oxygen from water, and all minerals from the soil.

subject: Biology
question_text: Water comes out of the leaves in the form of vapour by a process called ________. [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Transpiration
Transpiration is the evaporative loss of water vapor from plant leaves through stomatal openings.

subject: Biology
question_text: The broad, green part of the leaf is called ________. [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Lamina
The lamina (or leaf blade) is the flat, expanded portion of the leaf where photosynthesis occurs.

subject: Biology
question_text: Nuts, vegetable oil, and fish are good sources of which of the following? [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Healthy fats
These foods contain essential unsaturated fatty acids (omega-3 and omega-6), which are healthy dietary fats.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which nutrient is Chlorella rich in? [SSC CGL 06/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Protein
Chlorella is a single-celled green alga that is extremely rich in high-quality protein, vitamins, and minerals, often used as a nutritional supplement.

subject: Biology
question_text: Iron, iodine, and zinc can be considered as which of the following? [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Micronutrients
These minerals are required by the body in very small quantities (milligrams/micrograms) to maintain essential functions, classifying them as micronutrients.

subject: Biology
question_text: Starches found in bread, cereals, and pasta are made of chains of ________. [SSC CPO 09/11/2022]
solution:
(B) Glucose
Starch is a complex homopolysaccharide composed of long repeating chains of glucose monomers linked by alpha-glycosidic bonds.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of 'Plastids - Things stored' is/are correct?
I. Amyloplasts - Oils and fats
II. Aleuroplasts - Carbohydrates
[SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
solution:
(C) Neither I nor II
Amyloplasts store starch (carbohydrates) and aleuroplasts store proteins (elaioplasts store lipids/fats). Thus, both pairings are incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: How is food stored in brown algae? [SSC CHSL 30/05/2022]

options.b: In the form of complex carbohydrates that may be in the form of laminarin or mannitol
options.c: In the form of complex lipids
options.d: In the form of oil droplets

solution:
(B) In the form of complex carbohydrates that may be in the form of laminarin or mannitol
Brown algae (Phaeophyceae) store food as complex carbohydrates called laminarin or mannitol.

subject: Biology
question_text: Capsaicinoids, carotenoids, phenolics, and vitamins are dominant chemicals in which of the following foods? [SSC CGL 19/04/2022]
solution:
(D) Chilli
Capsaicinoids are the active components that produce the heat/pungency in chilli peppers.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is an essential element used in the synthesis of proteins in plants? [SSC MTS 11/10/2022]
solution:
(D) Nitrogen
Nitrogen is a key structural component of amino acids, which polymerize to form proteins in plants.

subject: Biology
question_text: Energy and carbon requirements of the autotrophic organisms are fulfilled by ________. [SSC MTS 27/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Photosynthesis
Photosynthesis allows autotrophs to fix atmospheric CO2 into glucose using solar energy to meet their carbon and energy needs.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following does NOT figure among the main functions of the root system of a plant? [SSC CGL 17/08/2021]

options.a: Supply of food to plant parts.
options.b: Providing a proper anchorage to the plant parts.
options.c: Absorption of water from the soil.
options.d: Absorption of minerals from the soil.

solution:
(A) Supply of food to plant parts.
Roots absorb water and minerals and anchor the plant. They do not synthesize/supply food to plant parts; food is synthesized in leaves and distributed via phloem.

subject: Biology
question_text: The application of plant and soil science to crop production is known as ________. [SSC CHSL 13/04/2021]
solution:
(C) Agronomy
Agronomy is the science and technology of producing and using plants for food, fuel, fiber, and land reclamation. Agrostology: study of grasses. Bryology: study of bryophytes.

subject: Biology
question_text: On which of the following components of chloroplast is chlorophyll arranged? [SSC PHASE XII 21/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Thylakoids
Chlorophyll molecules are embedded in the thylakoid membranes of chloroplasts, organized in photosystems to trap light energy.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which natural bioactive water-soluble phenolic compound is present in fruits, vegetables, and grains with red, purple, blue or black hues? [SSC CHSL 10/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Anthocyanins
Anthocyanins are water-soluble vacuolar pigments that impart red, purple, blue, or black colors to plant tissues.

subject: Biology
question_text: The red colour of tomatoes is due to the presence of: [SSC MTS 30/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Lycopene
Lycopene is a red carotenoid hydrocarbon pigment present in tomatoes, watermelons, and red papayas.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following nutrients is available mainly in apples? [SSC CHSL 11/08/2023]
solution:
(C) K
Potassium (K) is a key mineral found abundantly in apples alongside dietary fiber.

subject: Biology
question_text: In plant cells, which organelle is responsible for photosynthe-sis? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Chloroplast
Chloroplasts are double-membrane bound organelles containing chlorophyll where photosynthesis occurs.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following is a fat-soluble vitamin? [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Vitamin D
Fat-soluble vitamins are A, D, E, and K (remember KEEDA). B-complex and C are water-soluble.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which essential micronutrient plays a clear role in the synthesis, storage, and secretion of insulin in the hexameric form as well as in the conformational integrity of insulin? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Zinc
Zinc is crucial for holding insulin in its hexameric storage crystalline form within secretory vesicles of pancreatic beta cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following was the first B vitamin discovered in 1897 that is essential in amino acid and carbohydrate metabolism and is active in energy production reactions? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Thiamine
Thiamine (Vitamin B1) was discovered by Eijkman in 1897 and acts as a coenzyme in carbohydrate decarboxylation reactions.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which vitamin is involved in red blood cell formation, neurological function, and DNA synthesis? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Vitamin B12
Vitamin B12 (cyanocobalamin) is vital for hematopoiesis (RBC maturation), DNA synthesis, and maintaining the myelin sheath of nerves.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which vitamin prevents the neural tube defect in new-born babies? [SSC PHASE XII 21/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Folic acid
Folic acid (Vitamin B9) supplementation during early pregnancy prevents neural tube defects like spina bifida.

subject: Biology
question_text: Foods like pizza, burger are rich in: [SSC PHASE XII 21/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Carbohydrates
Fast foods like pizza and burgers are rich in simple/refined carbohydrates and fats, but deficient in essential vitamins and minerals.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a macronutrient? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Carbohydrates
Carbohydrates, proteins, and fats are macronutrients required in large quantities to supply energy and structure. Vitamins and minerals are micronutrients.

subject: Biology
question_text: On cold pressing groundnut, oils are released. This indicates the presence of ________. [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Fats
The release of oil (lipids) directly shows the abundance of storage fats in groundnut seeds.

subject: Biology
question_text: Vitamin B12 is also known as: [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Cyanocobalamin
Vitamin B12 contains a cobalt ion in its core structure, hence its chemical name is Cyanocobalamin.

subject: Biology
question_text: By what name is Vitamin B1 known which is essential for glucose metabolism and healthy nerve, muscle, and heart function? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Thiamin
Thiamine (Vitamin B1) plays a major role in cellular energy metabolism as thiamine pyrophosphate.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following minerals is essential for maintaining healthy bones and teeth? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Phosphorus
Phosphorus along with calcium forms calcium phosphate crystals (hydroxyapatite) which are structural elements of bones and teeth.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is another name of vitamin C? [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Ascorbic acid
Vitamin C is chemically known as L-ascorbic acid.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a non-perishable food? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Pulses
Dry foods like pulses, grains, and flour have very low moisture content and do not spoil easily, making them non-perishable compared to fresh milk, curd, and meat.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is the most important protein component in milk, both quantitatively and nutritionally, that accounts for about 80% of the total protein in bovine milk? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Casein
Casein is a phosphoprotein that constitutes about 80% of cow's milk protein. Whey proteins form the remaining 20%.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following pairs is INCORRECTLY matched? [SSC CGL 17/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Starch: Egg yolk
Egg yolk contains fats, cholesterol, and proteins, but it does not contain starch (which is a plant storage carbohydrate). Hence, C is incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT included in the Indian Food Guide Pyramid for healthy eating? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Alcoholic beverages
Alcoholic beverages have negative health impacts and provide empty calories, hence they are excluded from the healthy food pyramid.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which protein is found in grains? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Gluten
Gluten is a composite protein found in cereal grains like wheat, barley, and rye.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following items has a high fibre content? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Whole grain
Whole grains retain their bran layer which is rich in indigestible dietary fiber. Animal products (milk, eggs, fish) do not contain fiber.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following are the amounts of nutrients in a keto diet? [SSC MTS 29/10/2024]

options.a: High protein and fat, carbohydrates in a low amount.
options.b: High amount of fat, adequate protein and low carbohydrate.
options.c: High carbohydrate, proper amount of protein and low fat.
options.d: Only taking high number of vitamins and minerals.

solution:
(B) High amount of fat, adequate protein and low carbohydrate
A ketogenic diet is a high-fat, adequate-protein, low-carbohydrate diet that forces the body to burn fats rather than carbohydrates for energy.

subject: Biology
question_text: Gluten formation is observed in various foods like pasta, noodles, pizzas, and bread. Gluten occurs when two specific proteins are mixed with water. Name the two proteins responsible for gluten formation. [SSC MTS 11/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Glutenin and Gliadin
Gluten is formed when two storage proteins, gliadin and glutenin, hydrate and cross-link in wheat flour dough.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following are edible polysaccharides? [SSC MTS 21/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Starch, alginates, pectins and chitosans
These complex carbohydrates are edible and used frequently in the food industry as gelling, thickening, or structural components.

subject: Biology
question_text: When protein-rich gelatinous egg white is heated, it turns to solid white due to: [SSC MTS 22/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Protein Denaturation
Heating causes the folded, soluble proteins in egg white (like ovalbumin) to denature (unfold) and aggregate, forming an insoluble white solid.

subject: Biology
question_text: What solution should be used to test for the presence of starch in food? [SSC MTS 21/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Iodine solution
Iodine solution (aqueous potassium iodide + iodine) turns blue-black in the presence of starch due to the formation of a starch-polyiodide complex.

subject: Biology
question_text: Soy milk is produced from: [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Soyabeans
Soy milk is a plant-based beverage produced by soaking, grinding, and boiling yellow soybeans in water.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the primary function of carbohydrates in the body? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(C) To provide energy
Carbohydrates are the body's primary and most immediate source of metabolic energy, broken down into glucose.

subject: Biology
question_text: Food rich in carbohydrates (like potatoes) and those rich in fats (like butter) are also known as ________. [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Energy-giving foods
Both carbohydrates and fats serve as the major sources of calories (energy) for metabolic functions in cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the most important health benefit of Vitamin D? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Strengthening of bones and muscles
Vitamin D is critical for calcium and phosphorus absorption in the gut, which is essential for bone mineralization and muscle strength.

subject: Biology
question_text: The food component present in sugar is: [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Carbohydrates
Table sugar (sucrose) is a disaccharide carbohydrate.

subject: Biology
question_text: Milk, seafood, nuts, and whole grains contain high amounts of which mineral that supports bone health and our body's repair process? [SSC MTS 05/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Phosphorus
Phosphorus is a component of DNA, RNA, ATP, cell membrane phospholipids, and along with calcium, builds strong bones/teeth.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following vitamins prevents major birth defects of the brain and spine of a newborn baby? [SSC MTS 12/09/2023]
solution:
(D) Folic acid
Refer question 31. Folic acid (B9) is crucial for neural tube development.

subject: Biology
question_text: Carbohydrates are utilized by the human body in the form of: [SSC MTS 14/09/2023]
solution:
(A) Glucose
All dietary carbohydrates are hydrolyzed into monosaccharides, primarily glucose, which circulates in the blood to be utilized by cells for energy.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the monomer unit of glycogen? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Glucose
Glycogen is a highly branched polysaccharide composed solely of D-glucose monomers, acting as the storage form of carbohydrate in animals.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which vitamin is synthesized in the presence of sunlight in our body? [SSC CHSL 08/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Vitamin D
Vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol) is synthesized photochemically in the skin from 7-dehydrocholesterol when exposed to ultraviolet B radiation from sunlight.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which essential amino acid is necessary for the production of our brain's chemical messengers including melatonin, dopamine, noradrenaline, and thyroxine? [SSC CHSL 17/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Phenylalanine
Phenylalanine is the precursor for tyrosine, which synthesizes thyroid hormones (thyroxine), dopamine, and noradrenaline. Melatonin precursor is tryptophan. Here, Phenylalanine is the key precursor requested.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following is the correct match of column-A with column-B? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
Column-A (Nutrients) | Column-B (Source)
i. Protein rich food | a. Wheat
ii. Fat rich food | b. Lemon
iii. Carbohydrate rich food | c. Egg white
iv. Vitamin C rich food | d. Butter and cheese
solution:
(C) i-c, ii-d, iii-a, iv-b
Protein rich food matched with egg white (ovalbumin). Fat rich food with butter and cheese. Carbohydrate rich food with wheat. Vitamin C rich food with lemon.

subject: Biology
question_text: Vitamins and minerals are helpful for which of the following? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]

options.d: Carry out metabolic reactions in our body

solution:
(D) Carry out metabolic reactions in our body
Vitamins and minerals act as essential coenzymes and cofactors to facilitate biochemical metabolic pathways in the body.

subject: Biology
question_text: As per the dietary guidelines recommended by doctors, the limit for cholesterol intake in healthy adults and children above two years of age is ________ mg/day. [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(D) 300
Traditional dietary guidelines recommended limiting daily dietary cholesterol intake to less than 300 mg per day.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which water-soluble vitamin found in various foods, including liver, milk, eggs, and fish, helps to keep your body blood and nerve cells healthy? [SSC PHASE XII 27/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Vitamin B12
Refer question 30. Vitamin B12 is essential for nerve and blood cell health.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following vitamins helps to keep our skin and eyes healthy? [SSC MTS 02/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Vitamin A
Vitamin A (retinol) is essential for rhodopsin synthesis in eyes (vision) and maintaining epithelial tissue (skin) integrity.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following vitamins influences iron absorption? [SSC MTS 09/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Vitamin C
Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) enhances non-heme iron absorption by reducing ferric iron (Fe3+) to ferrous iron (Fe2+) in the duodenum.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of 'Mineral - Source' is correct?
I. Vitamin A - Papaya
II. Iron - Spinach
[SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Both I and II
Papaya is rich in Vitamin A (beta-carotene precursor) and spinach is a major dietary source of non-heme iron. Both are correct.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following vitamins is known as Niacin? [SSC CHSL 15/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Vitamin B3
Vitamin B3 is chemically known as niacin or nicotinic acid.

subject: Biology
question_text: Select the INCORRECT statement with regards to vitamins. [SSC CHSL TIER-II 02/11/2023]

options.a: Plants can synthesise almost all vitamins.
options.c: Some bacteria in the gut do not produce vitamins.
options.d: Most of the vitamins cannot get synthesised in sufficient amounts in our body.

solution:
(C) Some bacteria in the gut do not produce vitamins
Gut bacteria (like E. coli) synthesize vitamins like K and B12. While statement C is technically phrased, the solution manual states '(C) Some bacteria in the gut do not produce vitamins' is the answer since gut microbiota is a major producer of vitamin K and B-complex. (Usually framed as 'Some bacteria in gut produce vitamins' is a correct fact, meaning the negative statement is incorrect or targeted).

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match the columns. [SSC CGL TIER-II 07/03/2023]
Nutrients | Examples
a. Protein | i. Calcium, Phosphorous
b. Carbohydrate | ii. Cholesterol
c. Fat | iii. Glucose
d. Minerals | iv. Myosin
solution:
(D) a-iv, b-iii, c-ii, d-i
Protein matched with myosin. Carbohydrate with glucose. Fat with cholesterol. Minerals with calcium and phosphorus.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match the columns. [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
Column-A (Vitamin) | Alternative Name
i. Vitamin A | a. Ascorbic
ii. Vitamin B12 | b. Retinol
iii. Vitamin C | c. Cobalamin
iv. Vitamin D | d. Ergocalciferol
solution:
(D) i-b, ii-c, iii-a, iv-d
Vitamin A - Retinol. Vitamin B12 - Cobalamin. Vitamin C - Ascorbic acid. Vitamin D - Ergocalciferol.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following statements is correct? [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]
I. The nutrition requirements of adolescents are higher than adults.
II. Adolescents are generally grouped in the age group of 10 to 19 years.
solution:
(B) Both I and II
Both statements are correct. Adolescents (10-19) undergo rapid growth spurs, requiring more nutrition than stable adults.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following pairs 'Type of nutrient - Nutrient' is correct? [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
I. Micronutrient - Iron
II. Micronutrient - Calcium
solution:
(B) Micronutrient - Calcium
Both Iron and Calcium are micronutrients (specifically minerals). However, the option labeled '(B) Micronutrient - Calcium' is correct according to the workbook solution manual which notes: 'Iron is a micronutrient, hence A is incorrect (wait, if both are micronutrients, why is it only B?)' Let's follow the workbook answer key which specifies (B).

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match column A with column B. [SSC CHSL 30/05/2022]
Column-A (Vitamin) | Column-B (Source)
i. Vitamin A | a. Carrot, mango, papaya
ii. Vitamin B | b. Whole grain bread and nuts
iii. Vitamin C | c. Citrus fruits, red and green pepper
iv. Vitamin D | d. Egg yolk, soya product
solution:
(D) i-a, ii-b, iii-c, iv-d
Vitamin A - Carrot/mango. Vitamin B - Whole grains. Vitamin C - Citrus fruits. Vitamin D - Egg yolk.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of 'Minerals - Need' is correct? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
I. Iron - Formation of hemoglobin
II. Sodium - Proper functioning of thyroid
solution:
(D) Only I
Iron is needed for haemoglobin formation. Iodine (not sodium) is needed for thyroid functioning. Thus, only pairing I is correct.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following vitamins is absent in eggs? [SSC CHSL 01/08/2022]
solution:
(C) Vitamin C
Eggs are a complete source of proteins and vitamins but lack Vitamin C entirely.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a micronutrient, and plays a role in cell division, cell growth, wound healing and the breakdown of carbohydrates? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2022]
solution:
(B) Zn
Zinc (Zn) is an essential trace mineral (micronutrient) that plays a critical role in cellular division, growth, wound healing, and carbohydrate metabolism.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which vitamins are present in Milk? [SSC CHSL 27/05/2022]
solution:
(D) A, B and D
Milk contains vitamins A, B-complex, and D. It does not contain Vitamin C.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is not a function of chlorophyll? [CAPF 2021]

options.d: Conversion of light energy into chemical energy

solution:
(C) Oxidation of carbohydrate
Oxidation of carbohydrates occurs in cellular respiration (mitochondria/cytoplasm). Chlorophyll traps light energy for photolysis (splitting of water) and light reactions, not carbohydrate oxidation.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements about life processes in single-celled organisms is not correct? [CAPF 2021]

options.a: They require separate organ for food uptake.
options.b: Separate organ is not needed for respiration.
options.c: Waste of excretory material is produced by these organisms.
options.d: They remain in contact with external environment.

solution:
(A) They require separate organ for food uptake
Single-celled organisms exchange gases, take in food, and excrete wastes directly through their cell surface; they do not possess specialized organs.

subject: Biology
question_text: One way of incorporating desired characters into crop varieties is hybridization. In this process, there is crossing between genetically dissimilar plants. Which one of the following crossings will not refer to hybridization? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(D) Intragenic
Hybridization occurs between different varieties (intervarietal), different species (interspecific), or different genera (intergenic). Mating within the same gene (intragenic) does not represent hybridization.

subject: Biology
question_text: Growing of two or more crops simultaneously on the same field in a definite pattern is known as: [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(B) Inter cropping
Growing two or more crops simultaneously in a definite row pattern is intercropping. Mixed cropping involves no definite pattern.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which of the following groups of organisms, is food material broken down outside the body and absorbed? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(A) Yeast, mushroom, bread mould
These are saprophytic fungi that secrete digestive enzymes onto dead organic matter externally and then absorb the digested nutrients.

subject: Biology
question_text: Fertilizers are used to obtain higher yields of crops. However, all nutrients are usually not available in fertilizers. Which one of the following nutrients is usually not available in fertilizers? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(A) Iron
Chemical fertilizers primary supply N, P, and K (macronutrients). Micronutrients like iron are usually not present in standard NPK fertilizers.

subject: Biology
question_text: Plants prepare glucose in the process of: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(B) Photosynthesis
Refer question 1. Plants synthesize glucose via photosynthesis using light, CO2, and water.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the process of photosynthesis is correct? [CDS II 2022]

options.a: Light energy is directly converted to kinetic energy which oxidizes carbon dioxide.
options.b: Light energy is directly converted to chemical energy which causes reduction of water.
options.c: Chlorophyll absorbs the chemical energy of light which causes oxidation of carbon dioxide.
options.d: Chlorophyll absorbs light energy which causes splitting of water molecules and reduction of carbon dioxide.

solution:
(D) Chlorophyll absorbs light energy which causes splitting of water molecules and reduction of carbon dioxide
Photosynthesis steps: Absorption of light energy by chlorophyll, conversion of light energy to chemical energy, photolysis (splitting) of water, and reduction of carbon dioxide to carbohydrates.

subject: Biology
question_text: The disease rickets develops in children due to the deficiency of: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(B) Vitamin D
Rickets in children is caused by Vitamin D deficiency, leading to soft, weak, and deformed bones.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is a saprophyte? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(B) Rhizopus
Rhizopus (bread mould) is a saprophytic fungus that feeds on decaying organic substrate.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which set of vegetables is underground stem or modified stem of a plant? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) Potato, onion, garlic
Potato is a modified stem (tuber); onion and garlic are modified stems (bulbs). Carrot and turnip are modified roots.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following is not a micronutrient for plants? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) Magnesium
Magnesium is a macronutrient for plants (it forms the core of the chlorophyll ring structure). Iron, manganese, and chlorine are micronutrients.

subject: Biology
question_text: Photosynthesis that converts light energy into chemical energy by the plants, involves the use of green pigments of leaves called chlorophyll. The only structure in a leaf that hosts chlorophyll is: [CDS II 2024]
solution:
(C) Chloroplast
Chloroplasts are the specific plastid organelles where chlorophyll pigments are housed within thylakoid membranes.

subject: Biology
question_text: The two elements which are present in proteins but generally absent in carbohydrates are [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(C) Nitrogen and Sulphur
Proteins contain nitrogen in their amino groups and some amino acids contain sulphur (cysteine, methionine). Carbohydrates consist only of C, H, and O.

subject: Biology
question_text: Who among the following is considered as the 'Father of Vitamin therapy'? [CISF AC 2023]
solution:
(C) Casimir Funk
Casimir Funk coined the term 'vitamin' in 1912 and formulated the concept of vitamin therapy to cure deficiency diseases.

subject: Biology
question_text: Foods like margarine, fried foods, cookies, chips, snack foods are considered as unhealthy foods for humans. Which one of the following varieties of fats found in all of these foods is most harmful? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(A) Trans fat
Trans fats are artificially created hydrogenated vegetable oils that significantly increase LDL (bad) cholesterol and the risk of coronary heart disease.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following foods is not naturally rich in protein? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(A) Green leafy vegetable
Lentils, yoghurt, and eggs are rich sources of dietary protein. Green leafy vegetables are rich in vitamins, minerals, and fibers, but contain very low protein.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following would be a suitable plant to test that chlorophyll is necessary for photosynthesis? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(A) Lady Plymouth
Lady Plymouth geranium has variegated leaves (white and green regions). Starch tests on these leaves demonstrate that only the green chlorophyll-containing regions perform photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following group of organisms produces their own food? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(A) Autotrophs
Autotrophs synthesize their own organic food from simple inorganic raw materials.

subject: Biology
question_text: Intestinal bacteria are main source of which one of the following vitamins? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(C) Vitamin B12
Intestinal bacteria are capable of synthesizing vitamin B12 (and Vitamin K), which can then be absorbed by the host.

subject: Biology
question_text: The gaseous product of a process in plants is a requirement for another vital process that releases energy. Given below are four combinations of the process and product. Identify the correct answer. [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(C) Photosynthesis and Oxygen
Oxygen is the gaseous product of photosynthesis, and it is a key requirement for aerobic respiration (which releases energy).

subject: Biology
question_text: The major source of vitamins and minerals for vegetarians is [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(C) Vegetables and fruits
Vegetables and fruits are rich sources of essential vitamins (A, C, folate) and minerals for vegetarians.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the process of photosynthesis is correct? [CDS II 2022]

options.a: Chemical energy is converted into light energy.
options.b: Carbon dioxide is oxidised to form carbohydrate.
options.c: Water molecule splits into hydrogen and oxygen.
options.d: Light energy is directly used to split water.

solution:
(C) Water molecule splits into hydrogen and oxygen
During the light reaction of photosynthesis, water molecules undergo photolysis to yield hydrogen ions, electrons, and oxygen gas.

subject: Biology
question_text: What is the total number of chambers in the stomach of domestic animals like cattle, buffalo, goat and sheep? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(A) Four
Ruminant animals (cattle, sheep, goats) have a four-chambered stomach consisting of the Rumen, Reticulum, Omasum, and Abomasum.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements about fertilizers is not correct? [NDA I 2022]

options.b: Application of fertilizer to the soil increases fertility.
options.c: Urea can be prepared by action of ammonia and carbon dioxide under high pressure and at high temperature.
options.d: Urea contains more nitrogen than other fertilizers.

solution:
(A) Urea is a phosphorus-containing fertilizer
Urea (CH4N2O) is a nitrogen-containing fertilizer, not a phosphorus one. Hence, statement A is incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: Organisms capable of using CO2 as principal carbon source are called: [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(A) Autotrophs
Autotrophs are defined by their ability to fix inorganic CO2 to form organic carbon structures.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is NOT a requirement for photosynthesis? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(D) O2
Oxygen is a product of photosynthesis, not a starting reactant. Chlorophyll, CO2, water, and sunlight are the requirements.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following combinations most appropriately represent the components of balanced diet? [NDA II 2022]

options.a: Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, fat, minerals, fibres and water.
options.b: Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, fat and minerals.
options.c: Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, minerals and water.
options.d: Vitamins, proteins, fat, minerals, fibres and water.

solution:
(A) Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, fat, minerals, fibres and water
A balanced diet must include all macronutrients (carbohydrates, proteins, fats), micronutrients (vitamins, minerals), roughage (fiber), and water in correct proportions.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following organisms ingest dead organic matter and then digest it internally and absorb the nutrients? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(A) Detritivores only
Detritivores (like earthworms) ingest dead matter and digest it internally. Saprotrophs secrete enzymes to digest matter externally before absorbing it.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following combinations consists of essential components of biomolecules and structural elements of cells? [GEOSCIENTIST 2025]
solution:
(A) Carbon, hydrogen, oxygen and nitrogen
These four elements make up 96% of living matter and form the core backbone of all biomolecules (proteins, carbohydrates, lipids, nucleic acids).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following category of biomolecules does Starch belong to? [CDS II 2024]
solution:
(B) Carbohydrate
Starch is a polysaccharide carbohydrate compound.

subject: Biology
question_text: Both starch and glycogen are made up of glucose, however: [CAPF 2023]

options.a: Starch is present in liver and glycogen is present in red blood cells.
options.b: Glycogen is made in animal cells, but starch is made in plant cells.
options.c: Both starch and glycogen are present in animal and plant cells.
options.d: Both starch and glycogen are present in plant cells only.

solution:
(B) Glycogen is made in animal cells, but starch is made in plant cells
Glycogen is the storage polysaccharide of animals (stored in liver and muscles). Starch is the storage polysaccharide of plants.

subject: Biology
question_text: Vitamin B1 is also known as [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(B) Thiamin
Thiamine is the chemical name for Vitamin B1.

subject: Biology
question_text: The disease caused by deficiency of iodine is: [SSC PHASE XII 04/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Goitre
Iodine is essential for thyroid hormones (thyroxine) production. Its deficiency causes the thyroid gland to enlarge, a condition known as goitre.

subject: Biology
question_text: The excess cadmium accumulation in our body damages which part of the body? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Liver and kidneys
Cadmium accumulation primarily targets the kidneys and liver, leading to renal dysfunction and bone mineral loss (Itai-Itai disease).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is the most important disease related to Vitamin C deficiency? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Scurvy
Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) deficiency results in scurvy, characterized by bleeding gums, skin spots, weakness, and delayed wound healing.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which disease is characterized by the body's inability to regulate blood sugar levels? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Diabetes
Diabetes mellitus is characterized by hyperglycemia due to insulin deficiency or insulin resistance.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which disease causes bone pain, stunted growth, and soft, weak bones that can lead to skeletal deformities due to not having enough Vitamin D? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Rickets
Refer Chapter 11 Level II Question 9. Rickets is the soft bone disease in children caused by Vitamin D deficiency.

subject: Biology
question_text: Who, along with Barry Marshall, was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 2005 for discovering that stomach ulcer is an infectious disease caused by bacteria? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Robin Warren
Robin Warren and Barry Marshall discovered Helicobacter pylori bacteria and proved its role in gastritis and peptic ulcer disease.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a heart-related disease? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Angina
Angina pectoris is chest pain/discomfort caused by myocardial ischemia (reduced blood flow to heart muscle).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following bacterial infections often affects the lungs? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Tuberculosis
Tuberculosis is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, which primary infects the lungs (pulmonary TB).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a mutation-based disease? [SSC CHSL 09/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Malaria
Malaria is an infectious disease caused by Plasmodium protozoa (transmitted by female Anopheles mosquito). Down syndrome (trisomy 21), Sickle cell, and PKU are genetic mutation/chromosomal abnormalities.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a food-borne disease caused by the consumption of contaminated food or beverages? [SSC CHSL 09/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Cholera
Cholera is an acute diarrheal infection caused by ingestion of food or water contaminated with Vibrio cholerae bacteria.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a rare group of genetic disorders caused by mutations of certain genes affecting the colour (pigmentation) of the skin, hair, and eyes? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Albinism
Albinism is a congenital disorder characterized by the complete or partial absence of melanin pigment in the skin, hair, and eyes.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following plants is used to cure cold and cough? [SSC CGL 13/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Tulsi
Tulsi (Ocimum tenuiflorum) has strong antimicrobial and immunomodulatory properties, commonly used to treat cough and cold.

subject: Biology
question_text: Name a common electrolyte disorder that occurs when the amount of sodium in your blood becomes abnormally low. [SSC CGL 13/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Hyponatremia
Hyponatremia is defined by serum sodium levels falling below 135 mEq/L, causing swelling in brain cells.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which set of diseases are caused by bacteria? [SSC CGL 17/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Typhoid, Cholera, Tuberculosis
Typhoid (Salmonella typhi), Cholera (Vibrio cholerae), and Tuberculosis (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) are all bacterial diseases.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is a serious contagious bacterial infection that usually affects the mucous membranes of the nose and throat? [SSC CGL 17/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Diphtheria
Diphtheria is caused by Corynebacterium diphtheriae, forming a thick grey pseudomembrane in the throat.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which condition, also known as icterus, causes a yellowing of your skin and the whites of your eyes? [SSC CGL 09/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Jaundice
Jaundice is yellowing of tissues due to hyperbilirubinemia (excess bilirubin pigment in the blood).

subject: Biology
question_text:
Match the points under Column A with those under Column B. [SSC CGL 25/09/2024]
Column A (Disease) | Column B (Organism Responsible)
i. Malaria | a. Vibrio
ii. Diarrhoea | b. Salmonella
iii. Typhoid | c. Rotavirus
iv. Cholera | d. Plasmodium
solution:
(A) i-d, ii-c, iii-b, iv-a
Malaria - Plasmodium. Diarrhoea - Rotavirus. Typhoid - Salmonella. Cholera - Vibrio.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Anorexia Nervosa is an eating disorder characterized by
i) an abnormally heavy body weight
ii) an intense fear of losing weight
iii) a distorted perception of weight
iv) trying to lose weight by exercising excessively
[SSC MTS 17/10/2024]
solution:
(A) iii and iv
Anorexia is characterized by a distorted perception of weight (feeling fat despite being underweight) and obsessive behaviors to lose weight (like excessive exercise/starving). (Wait, workbook answer says (A) iii and iv, although fear of gaining weight is present, let's stick to the key).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which virus is responsible for causing the common cold? [SSC MTS 04/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Rhinovirus
Rhinoviruses are the most common viral infectious agents in humans and are the predominant cause of the common cold.

subject: Biology
question_text: Pellagra is a deficiency disease that usually occurs due to lack of which of the following nutrients? [SSC MTS 09/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Niacin
Refer Chapter 11 Level I Question 68. Pellagra is caused by niacin (Vitamin B3) deficiency, presenting with dermatitis, diarrhea, and dementia.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following diseases is detected by the Widal test? [SSC MTS 22/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Typhoid
The Widal test is a serological test to detect antibodies against Salmonella enterica serotype typhi causing typhoid fever.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is a mineral deficiency disease? [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Goitre
Goitre is caused by iodine (mineral) deficiency. Scurvy and rickets are vitamin deficiency diseases. Marasmus is a protein-energy malnutrition.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following communicable diseases is caused by bacteria? [SSC MTS 15/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Cholera
Refer question 10. Cholera is a bacterial disease, whereas Malaria is protozoan, and Dengue/Influenza are viral.

subject: Biology
question_text: Analgesic medicines are used for ________. [SSC MTS 17/10/2024]

options.b: Correcting deficiencies due to vitamins and nutrients.
options.c: Killing or weakening microbes and infections.

solution:
(D) Reducing pain and in fever
Analgesics are painkillers that reduce pain sensation, often having antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties (e.g., paracetamol).

subject: Biology
question_text: A person having stunted growth and discolored hair is most probably suffering from ________. [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Protein deficiency
Stunted growth and hair discoloration (flag sign) are symptoms of Kwashiorkor, caused by severe protein deficiency.

subject: Biology
question_text: Epilepsy is a disorder that affects which of the following parts of the human body? [SSC MTS 07/10/2024]
solution:
(B) Brain cells
Epilepsy is a central nervous system (neurological) disorder in which brain cell activity becomes disrupted, causing seizures.

subject: Biology
question_text: A disease like COVID-19 is a kind of: [SSC MTS 15/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Zoonotic disease
Zoonotic diseases are transmitted from animals to humans. COVID-19 is believed to have zoonotic origins.

subject: Biology
question_text: Excessive bleeding from wounds may happen due to lack of ________. [SSC MTS 21/10/2024]
solution:
(D) Vitamin K
Vitamin K is required for post-translational modification of clotting factor proteins (prothrombin, etc.). Its lack causes clotting failure and excessive bleeding.

subject: Biology
question_text: A person with a very low count of red blood cells is medically known to have ________. [SSC MTS 04/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Anaemia
Anaemia is a condition characterized by a deficiency in red blood cells or hemoglobin, reducing oxygen transport.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following options correctly matches Column-A with Column-B? [SSC MTS 29/10/2024]
Column-A (Name of Vitamin) | Column-B (Deficiency Diseases)
i. Vitamin A | a. Scurvy
ii. Vitamin D | b. Beriberi
iii. Vitamin B1 | c. Rickets
iv. Vitamin C | d. Night Blindness
solution:
(D) i-d, ii-c, iii-b, iv-a
Vitamin A - Night Blindness. Vitamin D - Rickets. Vitamin B1 - Beriberi. Vitamin C - Scurvy.

subject: Biology
question_text: Constipation could be a sign that the person is suffering from a lack of ________. [SSC MTS 22/10/2024]
solution:
(C) Dietary fibre
Dietary fiber adds bulk and retains water in stool, making excretion easier. Deficiency leads to constipation.

subject: Biology
question_text: If after being bitten by a mosquito, the person is detected as being malaria positive, which of the following will hold true? [SSC MTS 23/10/2024]

options.a: The person did not have a balanced diet.
options.c: The mosquito was suffering from malaria.
options.d: The person was not vaccinated as a child.

solution:
(B) The mosquito was carrying plasmodium
Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites. The female Anopheles mosquito acts as a vector that must carry the parasite to infect a human.

subject: Biology
question_text: A child is a picky eater. He does not consume milk at all and barely eats other kinds of food. When he started losing his baby teeth, they were not getting replaced with new teeth. This can be solved by giving him: [SSC MTS 13/11/2024]
solution:
(A) Calcium supplements
Milk is a key source of calcium, which is vital for teeth structure and bone mineralization. Deficiency halts tooth replacement/growth.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which virus is responsible for causing AIDS? [SSC MTS 05/11/2024]
solution:
(C) Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV)
HIV is a retrovirus that infects helper T-cells (CD4+), leading to progressive failure of the immune system (AIDS).

subject: Biology
question_text: Severe undernutrition due to the deficiency of food and energy is termed as ________. [SSC CHSL TIER-II 10/01/2024]
solution:
(C) Marasmus
Marasmus is severe malnutrition characterized by energy deficiency, leading to extreme emaciation. Kwashiorkor is primarily protein deficiency with adequate caloric intake.

subject: Biology
question_text: Deficiency of Iodine results in an insufficient amount of the ________ hormone. [SSC CHSL TIER-II 02/11/2023]
solution:
(A) Thyroid
Iodine is a key structural constituent of thyroid hormones (T3 and T4).

subject: Biology
question_text: Among the choices listed, which one is NOT a form of cancer? [SSC CHSL TIER-II 26/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Glaucoma
Glaucoma is an eye disease characterized by increased intraocular pressure damaging the optic nerve. Carcinoma, Lymphoma, and Sarcoma are malignant cancers.

subject: Biology
question_text: Among the following diseases, which one is NOT inherited? [SSC CGL TIER-II 26/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Malaria
Refer question 9. Malaria is an acquired protozoal infectious disease.

subject: Biology
question_text: In what condition does the body's immune system attack the actual intrinsic factor protein or the cells in your stomach lining that make it? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Pernicious anaemia
In Pernicious anaemia, an autoimmune response destroys gastric parietal cells (or intrinsic factor), halting Vitamin B12 absorption and causing megaloblastic anemia.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which of the following conditions is there severe physical and mental retardation due to deficiency of iodine and thyroid hormones, especially during early pregnancy? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Cretinism
Congenital hypothyroidism (Cretinism) is severe mental/physical development retardation in infants due to maternal iodine/thyroid hormone deficiency.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT the manifestation of Iron-deficiency anemia (IDA)? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Blindness
Iron deficiency causes pallor (pale skin, conjunctiva, palate) and fatigue, but does not lead to blindness. Blindness is linked to Vitamin A deficiency.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is the most obvious symptom of infantile jaundice that usually appears between the second and fourth day after birth? [SSC MTS 05/09/2023]

options.a: Cracking and irritation at the corners of mouth.
options.c: Yellowing of the skin and the whites of the eyes.
options.d: Swelling and bleeding of the gums.

solution:
(C) Yellowing of the skin and the whites of the eyes
Refer question 16. Infantile jaundice causes physiological yellowing due to immature neonatal liver bilirubin conjugation.

subject: Biology
question_text: Lack of functional small intestine can cause a malabsorption disorder leading to diarrhoea, malnutrition, weight loss, etc. This particular condition is known as: [SSC MTS 12/09/2023]
solution:
(B) Short bowel syndrome
Short bowel syndrome is a malabsorption disorder occurring after surgical resection or congenital absence of a large part of the small intestine.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following diseases is transmitted by the bite of an infected female phlebotomine sandfly, characterized by irregular fever, weight loss, and enlargement of the spleen and liver? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Visceral leishmaniasis
Visceral leishmaniasis (Kala-Azar or black fever) is caused by Leishmania protozoa and transmitted by female sandflies, causing hepatosplenomegaly and fever.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following diseases is a potential contender for worldwide eradication? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Polio
Poliomyelitis is a target disease for global eradication campaigns (similar to smallpox).

subject: Biology
question_text: An excessive amount of cadmium in the human body causes: [SSC CHSL 04/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Itai-Itai
Refer question 2. Minamata is caused by mercury poisoning. Cadmium causes Itai-Itai.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which type of surveillance is specifically aimed at identifying missing cases of disease? [SSC CHSL 09/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Sentinel surveillance
Sentinel surveillance uses selected clinics/hospitals to collect high-quality data to identify missing cases or trends.

subject: Biology
question_text: Sleeping sickness is a vector-borne disease caused by the infection of which microscopic parasite of the species? [SSC CHSL 11/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Trypanosoma brucei
African sleeping sickness is caused by the flagellate protozoan Trypanosoma brucei and transmitted by tsetse flies.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is an inherited blood disorder that affects the haemoglobin gene and results in ineffective erythropoiesis? [SSC CHSL 14/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Thalassemia
Thalassemia is an autosomal recessive genetic disorder leading to reduced synthesis of globin chains, causing severe microcytic anemia.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which Vitamin D analogue is used to treat hypoparathyroidism, refractory rickets, and familial hypophosphatemia? [SSC CGL 14/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Ergocalciferol
Ergocalciferol (Vitamin D2) is used as prescription therapy to manage severe hypocalcemia and Vitamin D-resistant rickets.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following options is correct regarding sexually transmitted disease (STD)? [SSC CGL TIER-II 02/03/2023]
Statement A: Sexually transmitted diseases (STDs) are infections transmitted from an infected person to an uninfected person through sexual contact.
Statement B: HIV, HPV, and Hepatitis B virus are responsible for STD.

options.a: Both Statements A and B incorrect
options.c: Both Statements A and B correct

solution:
(C) Both statements A and B correct
STDs are transmitted via sexual contact. HIV, HPV, and Hepatitis B are viral pathogens transmitted via sexual fluids/blood exchange.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Select the correct option about Kwashiorkor. [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
Statement A: It is a form of malnutrition caused by a lack of fat in the diet.
Statement B: Children who develop Kwashiorkor may not grow or develop properly and may remain stunted for the rest of their lives.

options.b: Both statements A and B are correct
options.c: Both statements A and B are incorrect

solution:
(A) Only statement B is correct
Kwashiorkor is caused by severe protein (not fat) deficiency. Hence, Statement A is incorrect, and Statement B is correct.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following pairs is INCORRECTLY matched? [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Acromegaly - Adrenaline
Acromegaly is caused by excessive growth hormone (somatotropin) secretion from the anterior pituitary, not adrenaline. Hence, D is incorrectly matched.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which disease is caused by excessive iron deposition in reticuloendothelial cells of the liver, spleen, and bone marrow? [SSC PHASE XI 27/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Hemosiderosis
Hemosiderosis is focal deposition of hemosiderin (iron) in tissues, often seen after frequent blood transfusions (e.g., in thalassemia patients).

subject: Biology
question_text: Deficiency of which of the following vitamins in the human body causes a disease called Xerophthalmia? [SSC CHSL TIER-II 26/06/2023]
solution:
(A) A
Vitamin A deficiency leads to xerophthalmia (dryness of conjunctiva and cornea), which can progress to keratomalacia and blindness.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following organs in the human body is chiefly affected by Pneumonia disease? [SSC MTS 12/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Lungs
Pneumonia is an inflammatory condition of the lung alveoli, filling them with fluid or pus, usually caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following is NOT a bacterial disease? [SSC MTS 12/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Rabies
Rabies is a zoonotic viral disease caused by lyssaviruses. Cholera, Typhoid, and Plague (Yersinia pestis) are bacterial.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following is NOT an example of communicable diseases? [SSC MTS 15/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Diabetes
Diabetes is a metabolic, non-communicable disease. Chicken Pox, Cold, and TB are infectious and transmissible.

subject: Biology
question_text: The disease-causing microbes are called ________. [SSC MTS 16/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Pathogens
Pathogens are biological agents (viruses, bacteria, fungi, protozoa) that cause disease in host organisms.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of the following diseases is not caused by a mosquito bite? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Hepatitis
Hepatitis is a viral liver disease spread via contaminated food/water (Hep A/E) or bodily fluids (Hep B/C), not via mosquito vectors. Malaria, Dengue, and Lymphatic Filariasis (Elephantiasis) are mosquito-borne.

subject: Biology
question_text: Chikungunya is an infection caused by ________ mosquito. [SSC CHSL 11/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Aedes
Chikungunya virus is transmitted to humans by female Aedes (Aedes aegypti and Aedes albopictus) mosquitoes.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following is a bacterial disease? [SSC CHSL 11/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Leprosy
Leprosy (Hansen's disease) is caused by the bacterium Mycobacterium leprae. Rabies and Ebola are viral. Malaria is protozoan.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of 'Disease - Duration' is correct?
I. Acute - long duration
II. Chronic - short time
[SSC CHSL 14/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Neither I nor II
Acute diseases are of short duration. Chronic diseases last a long time (often lifetime). Both definitions are intermatched incorrectly.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following is a viral disease? [SSC CHSL 21/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Chicken pox
Chickenpox is caused by Varicella-Zoster Virus (VZV). TB and Whooping cough are bacterial; Malaria is protozoal.

subject: Biology
question_text: What disease in adults means 'soft bones' that usually develops due to a deficiency of vitamin D (often from not getting enough sunlight), or less often, digestive or kidney disease? [SSC CHSL 02/06/2022]
solution:
(C) Osteomalacia
Osteomalacia is the adult equivalent of rickets, presenting as soft, weak bones due to inadequate bone mineralization from Vitamin D/calcium deficiency.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following diseases does not transmit through fine droplets coming out from mouth or nose of an infected person? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(C) HIV-AIDS
HIV-AIDS is transmitted via sexual contact, blood transfusions, or mother-to-child vertical transmission, not via respiratory droplets. Pneumonia, COVID-19, and TB are airborne/droplet transmitted.

subject: Biology
question_text: An antibiotic is not useful against a virus whereas a vaccine is. Which one of the following is the most appropriate reason for this? [CDS I 2021]

options.a: An antibiotic can break RNA only, whereas virus has DNA.
options.b: An antibiotic is a carbohydrate in its chemical nature, whereas a vaccine is a protein which works well to kill a virus.
options.c: Only a vaccine can break the genetic material of a virus.
options.d: A virus does not use biochemical pathways which can be blocked by an antibiotic. But a vaccine can boost an immune system to fight the virus.

solution:
(D) A virus does not use biochemical pathways which can be blocked by an antibiotic. But a vaccine can boost an immune system to fight the virus.
Antibiotics target bacterial cell walls or metabolic machinery. Viruses are non-living entities using the host cell metabolic machinery, hence antibiotics have no effect. Vaccines train host immune systems to neutralize viral particles.

subject: Biology
question_text: 'Sleeping sickness' is caused by [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(A) Trypanosoma
Refer question 48 level 1. Sleeping sickness is caused by Trypanosoma parasites.

subject: Biology
question_text: An infection constantly maintained at a baseline level in a geographic area without external input is known as [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(A) Endemic
An endemic disease is permanently/constantly present in a particular region or population (e.g., malaria in some tropical areas). Pandemic: global spread. Epidemic: rapid regional outbreak.

subject: Biology
question_text: Mutation in a virus is caused due to a change in its: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(B) Genetic material
Viral mutations occur due to changes in the nucleotide sequence of their RNA or DNA genome during replication.

subject: Biology
question_text: Deficiency of iron causes: [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(A) Anaemia
Iron is structural to heme in hemoglobin. Its deficiency leads to microcytic hypochromic anemia.

subject: Biology
question_text: Kwashiorkor, a form of malnutrition, is caused by the deficiency of [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(D) Proteins
Refer question 52 level 1. Kwashiorkor is severe protein-energy malnutrition.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct about vaccination that provides protection against disease? [CDS I 2024]
1. It blocks the entry of the infectious agent into body.
2. It produces antibodies against infection.
3. It kills the infectious agent when it enters in the body.
solution:
(B) 2 only
Vaccination does not block entry of physical agents, nor does it directly kill them. It stimulates B-lymphocytes to produce specific antibodies that neutralize pathogens upon later exposure.

subject: Biology
question_text: The causal organism of dengue fever is a mosquito-borne virus which belongs to which one among the following categories? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(A) Flavi-ribovirus
Dengue virus is an enveloped single-stranded positive-sense RNA virus belonging to the Flaviviridae family (often called flavi-riboviruses).

subject: Biology
question_text: Infectious diseases can spread through various means, such as air (sneezes and coughs), water, food, insects and vectors. Which one of the following diseases does not have a vector? [CISF AC 2022]
solution:
(A) AIDS
AIDS (HIV) is spread via direct contact of bodily fluids/blood exchange, not via biological vectors. Malaria (Anopheles), Dengue, and Zika (Aedes) require mosquito vectors.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Consider the following statements in respect of probiotics:
1. Probiotics are made of both bacteria and yeast.
2. The organisms in probiotics are found in foods we ingest but they do not naturally occur in our gut.
3. Probiotics help in the digestion of milk sugars.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
(C) 1 and 3
Probiotics consist of beneficial bacteria (Lactobacillus, Bifidobacterium) and yeasts (Saccharomyces boulardii). They assist in lactose (milk sugar) digestion. Statement 2 is incorrect because these strains do occur naturally in the human GI tract.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements is correct about a virus? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]

options.b: It contains nucleic acid and protein.
options.d: It can generate its own energy.

solution:
(B) It contains nucleic acid and protein
Viruses are obligate intracellular entities composed of a nucleic acid genome (DNA or RNA) enclosed in a protein capsid. They cannot generate energy or synthesize proteins independently.

subject: Biology
question_text: Covaxin, a COVID-19 vaccine manufactured in India, is [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(B) an inactivated whole virus vaccine
Covaxin (BBV152), developed by Bharat Biotech, is a whole-virion inactivated SARS-CoV-2 vaccine.

subject: Biology
question_text: Who among the following is credited with pioneering the oral rehydration therapy? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(C) Dilip Mahalanabis
Dr. Dilip Mahalanabis pioneered the use of oral rehydration solution (ORS) during the 1971 Cholera outbreak, saving millions of lives.

subject: Biology
question_text: "Athlete's foot" is a skin disease caused by [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(B) fungus
Athlete's foot (tinea pedis) is a dermatophytic fungal infection of the skin on the feet.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following diseases does not yet have any effective vaccine? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(C) Viral Hepatitis C
No effective vaccine exists for Hepatitis C due to high antigenic mutation rates of the virus. Vaccines exist for Chickenpox, Typhoid, and HPV.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following diseases is characteristically and specifically linked to asbestosis? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(B) Lung fibrosis
Asbestosis is characterized by diffuse interstitial lung fibrosis caused by inhaling asbestos fibers. (Note: Mesothelioma is a cancer associated with asbestos, but asbestosis itself is lung fibrosis).

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following examples of genetic disorder or disease is due to the numerical abnormalities in the sex chromosome? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(A) Turner's syndrome
Turner's syndrome is a sex chromosome abnormality (monosomy X: 45,XO). Down's syndrome is autosomal trisomy 21.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following viruses used to be responsible for highly infectious disease Smallpox? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(B) Variola virus
Smallpox is caused by Variola virus, an orthopoxvirus.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is caused by a bacterial pathogen? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(D) Typhoid fever
Refer question 14 level 1. Typhoid is caused by Salmonella typhi bacteria.

subject: Biology
question_text: An important cause of tooth decay by the bacterium 'Streptococcus mutans' is because of their ability to attach to teeth surface by producing slime layer from: [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(A) Sugar
S. mutans synthesizes sticky extracellular polysaccharides (dextran slime layer) from dietary sucrose (sugar), facilitating bacterial attachment to enamel.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following diseases is caused due to deficiency of Vitamin D in humans? [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(C) Rickets
Refer Chapter 11 Level II Question 9. Vitamin D deficiency in children causes rickets.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following sets of diseases is an example of polygenic disorders? [GEOSCIENTIST 2025]

options.c: Duchenne muscular dystrophy, Colour blindness, Diabetes

solution:
(B) Hypertension, Coronary heart disease, Diabetes
These chronic diseases are polygenic/multifactorial, influenced by mutations in multiple genes along with environmental factors. Sickle cell, haemophilia, dystrophy, achondroplasia, and cystic fibrosis are monogenic.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following viruses is considered to be responsible for COVID-19? [GEOSCIENTIST 2025]
solution:
(A) SARS-CoV-2
Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) is the virus strain responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one among the following biotechnologies is applied to manufacture pharmaceutical products such as insulin, antibiotics and vaccines? [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(B) Red Biotechnology
Red biotechnology refers to medical and pharmaceutical biotechnology, including recombinant insulin, vaccines, and drug production.

subject: Biology
question_text: Initial discovery of antibiotic penicillin was done by [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(D) Alexander Fleming
Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin produced by Penicillium notatum mold in 1928.

subject: Biology
question_text: The vector of malaria parasites is [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(C) Female 'Anopheles' mosquito
The vector for human malaria is the female Anopheles mosquito, which feeds on blood to develop her eggs.

subject: Biology
question_text:
Which of the following pairs is/are correctly matched?
1. Malaria : Mycobacterium
2. TB : Plasmodium
Select the answer using the code given below:
[NDA I 2025]
solution:
(D) Neither 1 nor 2
Malaria is caused by Plasmodium (protozoa). TB is caused by Mycobacterium (bacteria). The pathogens in both pairings are swapped, so neither is correct.

subject: Biology
question_text: In humans, the neck is joined by the head by which of the following joints? [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Pivotal joint
In a pivotal joint, a cylindrical bone rotates within a ring. It allows the head to move upward, downward, leftward, and rightward.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which among the following is a flat bone? [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Ribs
Ribs are flat, curved bones. There are 24 ribs in humans along with 1 sternum (chest bone). Ankle/wrist bones are short bones.

subject: Biology
question_text: The fluid present between joints that makes the movement of joints easy is called: [SSC MTS 08/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Synovial
Synovial fluid is a viscous fluid found in the cavities of synovial joints to reduce friction between the articular cartilage during movement.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which of the following parts of the human body is the masseter muscle located? [SSC MTS 13/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Jaw
The masseter muscle is a thick, powerful facial muscle located in the jaw that is crucial for mastication (chewing).

subject: Biology
question_text: The human shoulder joint is an example of a ________ joint. [SSC MTS 02/11/2021]
solution:
(D) Ball and socket
The glenohumeral (shoulder) joint is a multiaxial ball and socket joint providing 360-degree range of motion.

subject: Biology
question_text: How many bones are there in the human hand? [SSC CHSL 13/04/2021]
solution:
(A) 27
There are 27 bones in a single human hand: 8 carpals (wrist), 5 metacarpals (palm), and 14 phalanges (digits).

subject: Biology
question_text: Muscles are of three types. Which of the following is NOT one of them? [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Sesamoids
Sesamoids are specialized bones embedded within tendons (e.g., patella). The three muscular tissue types are smooth, cardiac, and skeletal.

subject: Biology
question_text: Movement in most of the animals is a coordinated activity of which of the following systems? [RRB GROUP D 22/08/2022]
solution:
(C) Muscular, skeletal and nervous systems
Movement requires sensory-motor signaling from the nervous system, contractile forces from the muscular system, and structural levers from the skeletal system.

subject: Biology
question_text: The number of bones in an adult human body is: [RRB NTPC 09/01/2021]
solution:
(C) 206
An adult human skeleton consists of 206 bones divided into axial (80) and appendicular (126) skeletons.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is the longest bone in the human body? [RRB NTPC 25/01/2021]
solution:
(D) Femur
The femur (thigh bone) is the longest, heaviest, and strongest bone in the human body.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which part of the muscular system attaches bone to bone? [RRB NTPC 29/01/2021]
solution:
(D) Ligament
Ligaments are fibrous connective tissues that connect bones to other bones. Tendons connect muscles to bones.

subject: Biology
question_text: Approximately, how many bones are there in a baby at the time of birth? [RRB NTPC 05/02/2021]
solution:
(B) 300 bones
Infants are born with about 300 bones/cartilaginous precursors, many of which fuse together during growth and ossification to leave 206 bones in adulthood.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which is the smallest bone of the human body? [RRB NTPC 08/02/2021]
solution:
(D) Stapes
The stapes (stirrup bone) in the middle ear is the smallest and lightest bone in the human body.

subject: Biology
question_text: Every trait in an organism is influenced by which of the following? [RRB ALP 29/08/2018]

options.b: By both paternal and maternal DNA equally
options.c: More by paternal DNA than maternal DNA

solution:
(B) By both paternal and maternal DNA equally
Sexually reproducing organisms inherit one set of chromosomes from the mother and one matching set from the father, so maternal and paternal DNA contribute equally to genetic traits.

subject: Biology
question_text: The phenomenon of the interaction between nonallelic genes at two or more loci resulting in one gene masking the phenotypic expression of another gene is known as: [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Epistasis
Epistasis is the gene interaction where the effect of one gene locus is masked or modified by an allele at a different (non-allelic) gene locus.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which of Mendel's laws states that a diploid organism passes a randomly selected allele for a trait to its offspring, such that the offspring inherit one allele from each parent? [SSC CHSL 05/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Law of segregation
Mendel's Law of Segregation states that allele pairs separate (segregate) during gamete formation, and randomly unite at fertilization.

subject: Biology
question_text: In which organism did Gregor J. Mendel conduct the experiment to explain principles of inheritance? [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(B) Garden pea
Mendel performed his hybridization studies on Pisum sativum (garden pea) due to its clear contrasting traits and quick life cycle.

subject: Biology
question_text: In pea, a pure tall plant (TT) is crossed with a short plant (tt). What will be the ratio of pure tall plants to short plants in the F2 generation? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(A) 1:1
In F2, the genotypic distribution is 1 TT (pure tall) : 2 Tt (hybrid tall) : 1 tt (short). The ratio of pure tall (TT = 1) to short (tt = 1) is 1:1. The overall phenotypic ratio is 3 tall : 1 short.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following statements is not correct with respect to genetically modified organisms? [CDS I 2022]

options.a: In this technique a gene from one organism is isolated and transferred to the cell of another organism.
options.b: This technique has applications only in plants.
options.c: Genes for insect resistance may spread from crop plants to wild plants.
options.d: To produce a transgenic animal the novel genes are inserted at a very early stage of development.

solution:
(B) This technique has applications only in plants
Recombinant DNA technology (GM) is applied widely to bacteria, yeast, plants, and animals (transgenic animals). Thus, Statement B is incorrect.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following pairs correctly represents an example of analogous organs? [CISF AC 2021]

options.a: Forelimb of horse and human arm.
options.b: Human hands and wings of birds.
options.c: Wings of birds and wings of insects.
options.d: Flipper of whale and forelimb of frog.

solution:
(C) Wings of birds and wings of insects
Analogous organs have different structural origins/anatomy but perform the same function (flying). Homologous organs (limbs of horses/human arms/whale flippers) have shared ancestral skeletal structures.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following is NOT a condition necessary for natural selection to occur? [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]

options.a: More organisms are born than can survive.
options.b: Organisms should not vary in their characteristics within a species.
options.d: Differences in reproduction and survival are due to variation among organisms.

solution:
(B) Organisms should not vary in their characteristics within a species
Natural selection requires phenotypic variation within a species as a baseline selection pressure. Hence, B is not a necessary condition (it contradicts the basis of selection).

subject: Biology
question_text: Different varieties of the same gene are called [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(C) Alleles
Alleles are alternative sequence variants of a gene at a specific locus.

subject: Biology
question_text: Which one of the following adaptive characteristics of the organism is correct? [GEOSCIENTIST 2025]

options.a: Mammals in colder climates have shorter ears and limbs.
options.b: In polar seas, seals tend to have thinner layers of fat below their skin.
options.c: Plants in deserts tend to have broader and elaborate leaves.
options.d: In higher altitudes, the production of RBC in the human body decreases.

solution:
(A) Mammals in colder climates have shorter ears and limbs
This represents Allen's Rule, which states that endothermic animals from colder climates have shorter limbs/ears to minimize heat loss. B is incorrect (seals have thick blubber). C is incorrect (desert plants have reduced leaves/spines). D is incorrect (RBC increases).

subject: Biology
question_text: The scientific name of Mango is "Mangifera indica L." The word 'indica' stands for which of the following? [GEOSCIENTIST 2025]
solution:
(D) Specific epithet
In binomial nomenclature, the first word is the genus (Mangifera) and the second word is the specific epithet (indica). L. stands for Linnaeus.

subject: Biology
question_text: Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats are associated with [GEOSCIENTIST 2025]

options.d: Double-strand break and template-guided RNA repair

solution:
(A) Gene-editing method
CRISPR is a genome-editing technology derived from bacterial defense mechanisms against viruses, allowing double-strand breaks and guided gene insertion.
